items.title: The Employment and Training (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of provisions of the Employment Act 1988 to which section 34(4) of that Act applies. It amends the law with respect to persons to whom facilities for work experience and training for employment are made available.

items.title: The Appropriation (No. 2) (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order authorises the issue out of the Consolidated Fund of Northern Ireland of a further sum for the year ending on 31st March 1989 and appropriates that sum for specified services in Northern Ireland. It also authorises the application of certain sums as appropriations in aid for the year ending on 31st March 1989.

items.title: The Farm Businesses (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of section 1 of the Farm Land and Rural Development Act 1988.

items.title: The Sex Discrimination (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Sex Discrimination (Northern Ireland) Order 1976 (“the 1976 Order”) and contains other provisions relating to sex discrimination.

items.title: The Corneal Tissue (Northern Ireland) Order 1988 (repealed)
items.description: This Order amends the Human Tissue Act (Northern Ireland) 1962 to permit the removal of eyes or parts of eyes for therapeutic purposes or purposes of medical education and research by persons who are not medically qualified acting on the instructions of a registered medical practitioner.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Firearms)(Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of section 44(3) and (4) of the Criminal Justice Act 1988.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Serious Fraud) (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of sections 4 to 12 of the Criminal Justice Act 1987 and of section 144 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988.

items.title: Criminal Justice (Evidence, Etc.) (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of Part II, Part III (except section 32) and sections 47 and 160 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988. It extends only to Northern Ireland

items.title: The Malicious Communications (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of the Malicious Communications Act 1988.

items.title: The Scotch Whisky (Northern Ireland) Order 1988 (revoked)
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of the Scotch Whisky Act 1988.

items.title: The Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the law relating to evidence in criminal proceedings in Northern Ireland. Article 3 specifies circumstances in which inferences may be drawn from an accused’s failure to mention particular facts when questioned about or charged with an offence. Under Article 4, an accused person may be called upon by the court to give evidence at his trial, and certain inferences may be drawn if he refuses to do so. Article 5 authorises inferences to be drawn from an accused person’s failure or refusal to account for objects, substances or marks in certain circumstances. Article 6 allows inferences to be drawn from an accused person’s presence at a place about the time when the offence in respect of which he was arrested was committed, if specified conditions are satisfied.

items.title: The Education (Academic Tenure) (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which is made only for purposes corresponding to those of sections 202 to 207 of, and Schedule 11 to, the Education Reform Act 1988, establishes the University Commissioners for Northern Ireland and confers on them functions relating to the modification of university statutes so as to comply with certain requirements specified in the Order.

items.title: The Education (Unrecognised Degrees) (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which is made only for purposes corresponding to those of sections 214 to 216 of the Education Reform Act 1988, makes it an offence for a person in the course of business to grant, offer to grant or issue any invitation relating to degrees other than those recognised for the purposes of this Order.

items.title: The Housing (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes further amendments to the law relating to housing in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Health and Medicines (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of provisions of the Health and Medicines Act 1988 mentioned in section 27(2) of that Act. The Order amends or supplements existing provision in the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 with respect to health services.

items.title: The Appropriation (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order authorises the issue out of the Consolidated Fund of Northern Ireland of a further sum for the year ending 31st March 1988 and of sums on account for the year ending on 31st March 1989 and app.ropriates those sums for specified services in Northern Ireland. It also authorises the application of certain further sums as appropriations in aid for the year ending on 31st March 1988 and reduces certain sums already authorised to be applied as appropriations in aid.

items.title: The Social Security (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Order)

items.title: The Statistics of Trade and Employment (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Statistics of Trade and Employment (Northern Ireland) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Criminal Injuries (Compensation) (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the Criminal Injuries (Compensation) (Northern Ireland) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Crossbows (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is made only for purposes corresponding to those of the Crossbows Act 1987.

items.title: The General Assistance Grants (Abolition)(Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order abolishes general assistance grants under Part IV of the Industrial Development (Northern Ireland) Order 1982 in respect of capital expenditure incurred on or after 1st April 1988.

items.title: The Wages (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Order)

items.title: The Fees &c (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Fees &c (Northern Ireland) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Minors' Contracts (Northern Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order removes certain restrictions on the enforceability of contracts entered into by minors.

items.description: These Regulations, made under Article 5 of the Artificial Reproduction of Animals Order (Northern Ireland) 1975 (“the 1975 Order”), revoke and replace the Artificial Insemination (Cattle) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1976 as amended by the Artificial Insemination (Cattle) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1981. They provide for a new system of control of the practice of the artificial insemination of cattle and the provision of a commercial artificial insemination service following the disengagement by the Department of Agriculture from the provision of the Artificial Insemination Service to farmers in Northern Ireland.

The Royal Ulster Constabulary Pensions (Purchase of Increased Benefits) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Students Awards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Traffic Weight Restriction (Omagh) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Personal Pension Schemes (Compensation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Family Credit (Transitional) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Incentive Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Health and Medicines (1988 Order) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Beef Cattle Improvement Scheme (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Sheep Improvement Scheme (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Colouring Matter in Food (Amendment) Regulations (NI) 1987
The Taxis (Omagh) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Taxis (Strabane) Bye-Laws (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Planning (Conservation Areas) (Demolition) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Registered Rents (Increase) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Rates (Regional Rate) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The General Dental Services (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Rates (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Traffic Weight Restriction (Antrim) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Travelling People (Encampments) (Dungannon) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Travelling People (Encampments) (Newry and Mourne) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Arctic Foxes (Importation and Keeping) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Social Fund (Maternity and Funeral Expenses) (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Meat Inspection (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Pensions Increase (Review) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Colleges of Education Salaries (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Prohibition of Traffic (Bus Turning Circle, Belfast) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Teachers' Superannuation (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Unfair Dismissal (Increase of Limits of Basic and Special Awards) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Industrial Relations (Variation of Limits) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Social Security (Claims and Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Homes Insulation Scheme and Grants Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Police (1987 Order) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland)) (Amendment No. 2) (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Teachers' (Compensation for Redundancy and Premature Retirement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Social Security (Earnings Factor) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Control of Asbestos at Work Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Control of Asbestos at Work (Medical Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Social Security Benefit (Persons Abroad) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Control of Traffic (Belfast) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Charges for Drugs and Appliances (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Royal Ulster Constabulary Reserve (Part-time) (Discipline and Disciplinary Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Payments for Optical Appliances (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Dental Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Social Security (Adjudication) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Unemployment Benefit (Disqualification Period) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Further Education Teachers' Salaries Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Legal Aid (Assessment of Resources) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Legal Aid (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Legal Advice and Assistance (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Legal Advice and Assistance (Financial Conditions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Legal Aid (Financial Conditions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Royal Ulster Constabulary (Complaints etc.) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Legal Advice and Assistance (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Mourne Mountains and Slieve Crobb) Designation Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Statutory Maternity Pay (Compensation of Employers) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Statutory Sick Pay (Additional Compensation of Employers) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
The Electricity (Permitted Supply) Order (Northern Ireland) 1988
Greater Manchester (Light Rapid Transit System) Act 1988
Corn Exchange Act 1988
British Railways (London) Act 1988
British Railways Order Confirmation Act 1988
St. Bennet Fink Burial Ground (City of London) Act 1988
Felixstowe Dock and Railway Act 1988
Scottish Development Agency (Oban South Pier) Order Confirmation Act 1988
Dartmouth-Kingswear Floating Bridge Act 1988
Brighton and Preston Cemetery Act 1988
Associated British Ports (Barrow) Act 1988
Bredero (Bon Accord Centre, Aberdeen) Order Confirmation Act 1988
Greater Manchester (Light Rapid Transit System) (No. 2) Act 1988
Highland Region (Lochinver Harbour) Order Confirmation Act 1988
Eastbourne Harbour Act 1988
Tor Bay Harbour (Oxen Cove and Coastal Footpath, Brixham) Act 1988
University of Wales College of Cardiff Act 1988
Imperial College Act 1988
British Waterways Act 1988
County of South Glamorgan (Taff Crossing) Act 1988
South Yorkshire Light Rail Transit Act 1988
Harwich Parkeston Quay Act 1988
Northampton Act 1988
University College London Act 1988
City of Glasgow District Council Order Confirmation Act 1988
Newcastle-upon-Tyne Town Moor Act 1988
Birmingham City Council Act 1988
Southern Water Authority Act 1988
Harwich Harbour Act 1988
Keble College London Act 1988
Selwyn College Cambridge Act 1988
Whitchurch Bridge Act 1988
Hastings Borough Council Act 1988
City of Westminster Act 1988
Liverpool Exchange Act 1988
Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988
items.description: An Act to consolidate certain of the enactments relating to income tax and corporation tax, including certain enactments relating also to capital gains tax; and to repeal as obsolete section 339(1) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1970 and paragraphs 3 and 4 of Schedule 11 to the Finance Act 1980.

items.title: Duchy of Lancaster Act 1988
items.description: An Act to extend the leasing powers of the Chancellor and Council of the Duchy of Lancaster.

items.title: Regional Development Grants (Termination) Act 1988 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to preclude the making of grants under Part II of the Industrial Development Act 1982 after 31 March 1988 unless previously applied for and to restrict the payment of such grants in certain cases; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to the registration of ships; to make provision for the giving of financial assistance in connection with the training of seamen and crew relief costs; to make provision for the establishment of a Merchant Navy Reserve; to make further provision with respect to the safety of shipping, with respect to liability and compensation for oil pollution and with respect to the financing and administration of the lighthouse service; to make other amendments of the law relating to shipping, seamen and pollution; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Coroners Act 1988
items.description: An Act to consolidate the Coroners Acts 1887 to 1980 and certain related enactments, with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Law Commission.

items.title: Immigration Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make further provision for the regulation of immigration into the United Kingdom; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Public Utility Transfers and Water Charges Act 1988
items.description: An Act to confer powers on water authorities, electricity boards and the Electricity Council to act in relation to proposals for the transfer of any of the property or functions of water authorities or electricity boards to other bodies corporate; and to make provision for and in connection with the fixing of charges by water undertakers.

items.title: Farm Land and Rural Development Act 1988
items.description: An Act to provide for the payment of grants for certain purposes connected with farm businesses or with the conversion of agricultural land to woodlands; and to increase the limit on the number of members of the Development Commission.

items.title: Licensing Act 1988 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend the Licensing Act 1964.

items.title: Matrimonial Proceedings (Transfers) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to empower the High Court to transfer to county courts certain matrimonial proceedings and to validate certain High Court orders purporting to transfer such proceedings to county courts; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Employment Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make provision with respect to trade unions, their members and their property, to things done for the purpose of enforcing membership of a trade union, to trade union ballots and elections and to proceedings involving trade unions; to provide for the Manpower Services Commission to be known as the Training Commission; to amend the law with respect to the constitution and functions of that Commission and with respect to persons to whom facilities for work-experience and training for employment are made available; to enable additional members to be appointed to industrial training boards and to the Agricultural Training Board; and to provide that the terms on which certain persons hold office or employment under the Crown are to be treated for certain purposes as contained in contracts of employment.

items.title: Arms Control and Disarmament (Privileges and Immunities) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to provide for conferring privileges and immunities on observers, inspectors and auxiliary personnel exercising functions under international agreements or arrangements for furthering arms control or disarmament.

items.title: Dartford-Thurrock Crossing Act 1988
items.description: An Act to provide for the construction of a bridge over the river Thames between Dartford in Kent and Thurrock in Essex and of associated works; to provide for the Secretary of State to be the highway authority for the highways passing through the tunnels under that river between Dartford and Thurrock and their approaches instead of Kent and Essex County Councils; to provide for the levying of tolls, by a person appointed by the Secretary of State or by the Secretary of State, in respect of traffic using the crossing; to provide for transfers of property and liabilities of those Councils to the person appointed and the Secretary of State and for the transfer to the Secretary of State of property and liabilities of the person appointed on termination of his appointment; to provide for the management of the crossing, including the imposition of prohibitions, restrictions and requirements in relation to traffic, and otherwise in relation to the crossing; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Consumer Arbitration Agreements Act 1988 (repealed 31.1.1997)
items.description: An Act to extend to consumers certain rights as regards agreements to refer future differences to arbitration and for purposes connected therewith.

items.title: Scotch Whisky Act 1988 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make provision as to the definition of Scotch whisky and as to the production and sale of whisky; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Motor Vehicles (Wearing of Rear Seat Belts by Children) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make provision for the wearing of rear seat belts by children; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Community Health Councils (Access to Information) Act 1988 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to provide for access by the public to meetings of, and to certain documents and information relating to, Community Health Councils and committees of those Councils and for related purposes.

items.title: Licensing (Retail Sales) Act 1988 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to amend the definition of “sale by retail” in section 201 of the Licensing Act 1964; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Landlord and Tenant Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make new provision for imposing statutory duties in connection with convenants in tenancies against assigning, underletting, charging or parting with the possession of premises without consent.

items.title: Malicious Communications Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make provision for the punishment of persons who send or deliver letters or other articles for the purpose of causing distress or anxiety.

items.title: Access to Medical Reports Act 1988
items.description: An Act to establish a right of access by individuals to reports relating to themselves provided by medical practitioners for employment or insurance purposes and to make provision for related matters.

items.title: Protection of Animals (Amendment) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to enable a court to disqualify a person for having custody of an animal on a first conviction of cruelty; and to increase the penalties for offences relating to animal fights, and to make further provision with respect to attendance at such fights, in England and Wales and to penalise attendance at such fights in Scotland.

items.title: Land Registration Act 1988
items.description: An Act to amend section 112 of the Land Registration Act 1925, and for connected purposes.

items.title: Environment and Safety Information Act 1988
items.description: An Act to establish public registers of certain notices served concerning health, safety and environmental protection; and for related purposes.

items.title: Protection against Cruel Tethering Act 1988 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to protect horses, asses and mules against cruel tethering; and for purposes connected therewith.

items.title: Civil Evidence (Scotland) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make fresh provision in relation to civil proceedings in Scotland regarding corroboration of evidence and the admissibility of hearsay and other evidence; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Criminal Justice Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make fresh provision for extradition; to amend the rules of evidence in criminal proceedings; to provide for the reference by the Attorney General of certain questions relating to sentencing to the Court of Appeal; to amend the law with regard to the jurisdiction and powers of criminal courts, the collection, enforcement and remission of fines imposed by coroners, juries, supervision orders, the detention of children and young persons, probation and the probation service, criminal appeals, anonymity in cases of rape and similar cases, orders under sections 4 and 11 of the Contempt of Court Act 1981 relating to trials on indictment, orders restricting the access of the public to the whole or any part of a trial on indictment or to any proceedings ancillary to such a trial and orders restricting the publication of any report of the whole or any part of a trial on indictment or any such ancillary proceedings, the alteration of names of petty sessions areas, officers of inner London magistrates' courts and the costs and expenses of prosecution witnesses and certain other persons; to make fresh provision for the payment of compensation by the Criminal Injuries Compensation Board; to make provision for the payment of compensation for a miscarriage of justice which has resulted in a wrongful conviction; to create an offence of torture and an offence of having an article with a blade or point in a public place; to create further offences relating to weapons; to create a summary offence of possession of an indecent photograph of a child; to amend the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 in relation to searches, computer data about fingerprints and bail for persons in customs detention; to make provision in relation to the taking of body samples by the police in Northern Ireland; to amend the Bail Act 1976; to give a justice of the peace power to authorise entry and search of premises for offensive weapons; to provide for the enforcement of the Video Recordings Act 1984 by officers of a weights and measures authority and in Northern Ireland by officers of the Department of Economic Development; to extend to the purchase of easements and other rights over land the power to purchase land conferred on the Secretary of State by section 36 of the Prison Act 1952; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Legal Aid Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make new provision for the administration of, and to revise the law relating to, legal aid, advice and assistance.

items.title: British Steel Act 1988
items.description: An Act to provide for the vesting of the property, rights and liabilities of the British Steel Corporation in a company nominated by the Secretary of State and for the subsequent dissolution of the Corporation; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Court of Session Act 1988
items.description: An Act to consolidate, with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Scottish Law Commission, certain enactments relating to the constitution, administration and procedure of the Court of Session and procedure on appeal therefrom to the House of Lords; and to repeal, in accordance with recommendations of the Scottish Law Commission, certain enactments relating to the aforesaid matters which are no longer of practical utility.

items.title: Electricity (Financial Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to raise the financial limit imposed by and by virtue of section 29(1) of the Electricity (Scotland) Act 1979.

items.title: Appropriation Act 1988
items.description: An Act to apply a sum out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the year ending on 31st March 1989, to appropriate the supplies granted in this Session of Parliament, and to repeal certain Consolidated Fund and Appropriation Acts.

items.title: Finance Act 1988
items.description: An Act to grant certain duties, to alter other duties, and to amend the law relating to the National Debt and the Public Revenue, and to make further provision in connection with Finance.

items.title: Norfolk and Suffolk Broads Act 1988
items.description: An Act to establish an authority to be known as the Broads Authority; to make provision with respect to its powers; to make provision with respect to the area commonly known as the Broads and with respect to the Great Yarmouth Port and Haven and its Commissioners; to provide for the making of grants to the Authority by the Secretary of State; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Education Reform Act 1988
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to education.

items.title: Local Government Finance Act 1988
items.description: An Act to create community charges in favour of certain authorities, to create new rating systems, to provide for precepting by certain authorities and levying by certain bodies, to make provision about the payment of grants to certain authorities, to require certain authorities to maintain certain funds, to make provision about the capital expenditure and the administration of the financial affairs of certain authorities, to abolish existing rates, precepts and similar rights, to abolish rate support grants and supplementary grants for transport purposes, to make amendments as to rates and certain grants, to make certain amendments to the law of Scotland as regards community charges, rating and valuation, to provide for the establishment of valuation and community charge tribunals, and for connected purposes.

items.title: Solicitors (Scotland) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to amend the Solicitors (Scotland) Act 1980; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Housing (Scotland) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to establish a body having functions relating to housing; and, as respects Scotland, to make further provision with respect to houses let on tenancies; to confer on that body and on persons approved for the purpose the right to acquire from public sector landlords certain houses occupied by secure tenants; to make new provision as to the limit on discount on the price of houses purchased by secure tenants; to provide for the making of local authority grants to assist local authority tenants to obtain accommodation otherwise than as such tenants; to abolish, and make interim provision for the capitalisation of, certain subsidies and contributions relating to housing; to make further provision about rent officers and the administration of housing benefit and rent allowance subsidy; to make provision for the disposal of housing land by development corporations; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Foreign Marriage (Amendment) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to amend the Foreign Marriage Act 1892 and to repeal certain enactments which are spent relating to the validation of marriages of British subjects solemnised outside the United Kingdom.

items.title: Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to amend the Firearms Act 1968 and to make further provision for regulating the possession of, and transactions relating to, firearms and ammunition.

items.title: European Communities (Finance) Act 1988 (repealed 16.1.1995)
items.description: An Act to amend the definition of “the Treaties” and “the Community Treaties” in section 1(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 so as to include the decision of 24th June 1988 of the Council of the Communities on the Communities' system of own resources and the undertaking by the Representatives of the Governments of the member States, as confirmed at their meeting within the Council on 24th June 1988 in Luxembourg, to make payments to finance the Communities' general budget for the financial year 1988.

items.title: School Boards (Scotland) Act 1988 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make new provision for the government of public schools in Scotland; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988
items.description: An Act to restate the law of copyright, with amendments; to make fresh provision as to the rights of performers and others in performances; to confer a design right in original designs; to amend the Registered Designs Act 1949; to make provision with respect to patent agents and trade mark agents; to confer patents and designs jurisdiction on certain county courts; to amend the law of patents; to make provision with respect to devices designed to circumvent copy-protection of works in electronic form; to make fresh provision penalising the fraudulent reception of transmissions; to make the fraudulent application or use of a trade mark an offence; to make provision for the benefit of the Hospital for Sick Children, Great Ormond Street, London; to enable financial assistance to be given to certain international bodies; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Health and Medicines Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make further provision in relation to the National Health Service, the testing of sight and instruction in matters relating to health and welfare; to amend the Medicines Act 1968 and the Medicines Act 1971 and to empower the Secretary of State to make regulations about HIV testing kits and services.

items.title: Welsh Development Agency Act 1988 (repealed 19.12.1991)
items.description: An Act to increase the financial limit which applies by virtue of section 18 of the Welsh Development Agency Act 1975 to the general external borrowing of the Agency and certain other amounts.

items.title: Housing Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make further provision with respect to dwelling-houses let on tenancies or occupied under licences; to amend the Rent Act 1977 and the Rent (Agriculture) Act 1976; to establish a body, Housing for Wales, having functions relating to housing associations; to amend the Housing Associations Act 1985 and to repeal and re-enact with amendments certain provisions of Part II of that Act; to make provision for the establishment of housing action trusts for areas designated by the Secretary of State; to confer on persons approved for the purpose the right to acquire from public sector landlords certain dwelling-houses occupied by secure tenants; to make further provision about rent officers, the administration of housing benefit and rent allowance subsidy, the right to buy, repair notices and certain disposals of land and the application of capital money arising thereon; to make provision consequential upon the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Rate Support Grants Act 1988 (repealed)
items.description: An Act to make further provision about rate support grants.

items.title: Road Traffic Act 1988
items.description: An Act to consolidate certain enactments relating to road traffic with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Law Commission and the Scottish Law Commission.

items.title: Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988
items.description: An Act to consolidate certain enactments relating to the prosecution and punishment (including the punishment without conviction) of road traffic offences with amendments to give effect to recommendations of the Law Commission and the Scottish Law Commission.

items.title: Road Traffic (Consequential Provisions) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to make provision for repeals (including a repeal to give effect to a recommendation of the Law Commission and the Scottish Law Commission), consequential amendments, transitional and transitory matters and savings in connection with the consolidation of enactments in the Road Traffic Act 1988 and the Road Traffic Offenders Act 1988.

items.title: Consolidated Fund (No. 2) Act 1988
items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the years ending on 31st March 1989 and 1990.

items.title: Consolidated Fund Act 1988
items.description: An Act to apply certain sums out of the Consolidated Fund to the service of the years ending on 31st March 1987 and 1988.

items.title: Social Security Act 1988
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to social security and to make fresh provision in relation to welfare foods and for the remission of charges for services provided under the National Health Service Act 1977 and the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 and the payment of travelling and overnight expenses for the purpose of persons availing themselves of services provided under those Acts; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency Act 1988
items.description: An Act to enable the United Kingdom to give effect to the Convention establishing the Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency.

items.title: Local Government Act 1988
items.description: An Act to secure that local and other public authorities undertake certain activities only if they can do so competitively; to regulate certain functions of local and other public authorities in connection with public supply or works contracts; to authorise and regulate the provision of financial assistance by local authorities for certain housing purposes; to prohibit the promotion of homosexuality by local authorities; to make provision about local authorities' publicity, local government administration, the powers of auditors, land held by public bodies, direct labour organisations, arrangements under the Employment and Training Act 1973, the Commission for Local Authority Accounts in Scotland, the auditing of accounts of local authorities in Scotland, and dog registration, dog licences and stray dogs; and for connected purposes.

items.title: The Origin of Goods (Petroleum Products) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 5th January 1988, lay down the conditions under which certain petroleum products, specified in the Schedule to the Regulations, are to be treated as originating in a country for the purposes of preferential rates of, or exemption from, customs duties charged in accordance with section 5(1) and (2) of the European Communities Act 1972. They revoke and re-enact the provisions of the Origin of Goods (Petroleum Products) Regulations 1987 following the entry into force of the Customs Duties (Repeal) (Revocation of Savings) Order 1987. There are at present no Community origin regulations for these products.

items.title: The Housing (Improvement of Amenities of Residential Areas) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Housing (Improvement of Amenities of Residential Areas) (Scotland) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Intervention Functions (Delegation) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Intervention Functions (Delegation) Regulations 1972 so as to extend the range of crops in respect of which the Home-Grown Cereals Authority is empowered to carry out functions on behalf of the Intervention Board for Agricultural Produce.

items.title: The Cereals Co-responsibility Levy Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to the United Kingdom, supersede the Cereals Co-responsibility Levy Regulations 1986. They provide for the adminstration, collection and enforcement of co-responsibility levy imposed in respect of specified cereals by Article 4 of Council Regulation (EEC) 2727/75 (OJ No. L281, 1.11.1975, p.1), as amended in particular by Council Regulation (EEC) 1079/88 (OJ No. L110, 29.4.88, p.7). Detailed rules for the application of the levy have been laid down by Commission Regulation (EEC) 1432/88 (OJ No. L131, 27.5.88, p.37) and come into force in the United Kingdom at the beginning of the cereals marketing year on 1st July 1988.

items.title: The Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (Appointed Day No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order appoints 9th June 1988 as the day on which section 729 of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (sale and repurchase of securities) ceases to apply to securities which are securities for the purpose of sections 710 to 728 of that Act (accrued income scheme).

items.title: The Medicines (Labelling of Medicinal Products for Incorporation in Animal Feeding Stuffs and of Medicated Animal Feeding Stuffs) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Medicines (Labelling of Medicated Animal Feeding Stuffs) Regulations 1973 and that part of the Medicines (Labelling) Regulations 1976, as amended, (“the 1976 Regulations”) which dealt with medicinal products for incorporation in animal feeding stuffs and medicated animal feeding stuffs in respect of which a product licence or an animal test certificate had been granted under the Medicines Act 1968. The 1976 Regulations implemented in part Council Directive 81/851/EEC (OJ No. L317, 6.11.81, p.1) on the approximation of the laws of the Member States relating to veterinary medicinal products.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 4th July 1988 the provisions of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988 specified in the Schedule to the Order, and also effects the repeals set out in the Appendix to the Schedule.

items.title: The Income Tax (Building Societies) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st July 1988, amend the Income Tax (Building Societies) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”).

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Minimum Contributions under the Social Security Act 1986) Regulations 1988
items.description: Subsection (1) of section 649 of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 provides that where, under Part I of the Social Security Act 1986, the Secretary of State pays minimum contributions for the purposes of approved personal pension arrangements, the employee’s share of those contributions shall, instead of being the amount provided for in that Part, be“the grossed up equivalent” of the amount so provided for; and by subsection (2) of that section“the grossed up equivalent” of such an amount is such sum as, after deduction of income tax at the basic rate for the year of assessment for which the contributions are paid, is equal to that amount.

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Relief at Source) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations supplement section 639 of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“section 639”) which provides for relief by deduction from contributions made under approved pension arrangements (“arrangements”) made in accordance with an approved personal pension scheme (“scheme”) in such cases and subject to such conditions as the Commissioners of the Inland Revenue (“the Board”) may prescribe in regulations.

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Transfer Payments) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 638(2) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 provides that the Board shall not approve a personal pension scheme unless it makes such provision for the making, acceptance and application of transfer payments as satisfies any requirements imposed by or under regulations made by the Board; and these Regulations, which come into force on 1st July 1988 impose such requirements.

items.title: The Medicines (Exemptions from Restrictions on the Retail Sale or Supply of Veterinary Drugs) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Exemptions from Restrictions on the Retail Sale or Supply of Veterinary Drugs) Order 1985 which provides for exemptions from the restrictions imposed by section 52 of the Medicines Act 1968.

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Transfer to Self-employed Pension Arrangements) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Occupational Pension Schemes (Transfer Values) Regulations 1985 and the Personal Pension Schemes (Transfer Values) Regulations 1987. They make provision for a member of an occupational or personal pension scheme who, on termination of his pensionable service under the scheme, has acquired a right under Part II of Schedule 1A to the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 to a cash equivalent of his accrued rights under the scheme and who is, or is about to become, self-employed to require the trusteees or managers of the scheme to use that cash equivalent to secure rights to money-purchase benefits under a self-employed pension arrangement. The right so to require does not extend to any part of the cash equivalent which represents guaranteed minimum pensions or protected rights. A “self-employed pension arrangement” is an arrangement which is a personal pension scheme for the purposes of Chapter IV of Part XIV of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 and which is approved by the Inland Revenue under that Chapter; but which is neither a personal pension scheme for the purposes of the Social Security Act 1986 nor a contract or a scheme approved under Chapter III of Part XIV of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988. The Regulations also amend certain references to the income tax legislation to take account of the consolidation of that legislation.

items.title: The Restriction on Conduct (Specialist Advertising Services) Order 1988
items.description: This Order applies to publishers of magazines intended for campers, climbers or walkers (“specialist publishers”). It makes it unlawful, except in specified circumstances, for specialist publishers to withhold advertising services from persons seeking to place advertisements containing information relating to the price of goods advertised. It also makes it unlawful for specialist advertisers to discriminate in the prices charged for advertising services or terms as to credit or discount, between persons placing orders for advertisements containing such information and others, or to give other preferential treatment to persons placing orders for advertisements not containing such information.

items.title: The Video Recordings Act 1984 (Commencement No. 7) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings the Video Recordings Act 1984 into force in England and Wales and Northern Ireland, in so far as the Act is not already in force, on 1st September 1988. The Act is similarly brought into force on that date in Scotland by the Video Recordings Act 1984 (Scotland) (Commencement No. 7) Order 1988. (At the date of this Order, the Criminal Justice Bill, which inserts a new section, section 16A, in the Video Recordings Act 1984 to provide for the enforcement of that Act by officers of a Weights and Measures Authority and in Northern Ireland by officers of the Department of Economic Development, had not received Royal Assent.)

items.title: The London Government Reorganisation (Transfer of Loans) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the London Government Reorganisation (Transfer of Loans) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/233;“the principal Order”) by substituting a new article 4 (application of general provisions relating to borrowing).

items.title: The Education (School Information) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend paragraph 15(1)(d) of Schedule 2 to the Education (School Information) Regulations 1981 by including references in that paragraph to the General Certificate of Secondary Education and the advanced supplementary level of the General Certificate of Education. This will mean that the information relating to the grades obtained in public examinations required to be published under that paragraph will include information relating to these new public examinations.

items.title: The Stanswood Bay Oyster Fishery Order 1988
items.description: This Order confers on Stanswood Bay Oystermen Limited (“the company”) the right of several fishery for oysters in parts of Stanswood Bay, Hampshire, which are adjacent to the area in which a similar right was conferred on the company by the Stanswood Bay Oyster Fishery Order 1973 (“the 1973 Order”). This Order thus has the effect of enlarging the area in respect of which the company has the right of several fishery for oysters.

items.title: The Amusements with Prizes (Variation of Monetary Limits) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the maximum amount permitted to be taken by way of the sale of chances in any one determination of winners in amusements provided at certain fairs and other commercial entertainments in England and Wales under section 16 of the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Monetary Limits) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases in England and Wales certain monetary limits specified in sections 21(2) and 41 of the Gaming Act 1968.

items.title: The Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) Regulations 1984 to extend the permitted hours for gaming on bingo club premises in England and Wales so that gaming may commence on weekdays and Saturdays at 10 a.m. instead of noon.

items.title: The Gaming (Small Charges) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the maximum daily charge which may be made in clubs and miners' welfare institutes in England and Wales in respect of a person taking part in games of equal chance, except where the only games played are bridge and whist.

items.title: The A4 Trunk Road (Bath Road, Hounslow) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A4 Trunk Road (Bath Road, Hounslow) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Savings Bank (Investment Deposits) (Limits) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which further amends the National Savings Bank (Investment Deposits) (Limits) Order 1977, permits the Director of Savings to receive without limit sums by way of investment deposits for accounts operated by the Accountant General of the Supreme Court of Judicature of Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Motor Cycles (Eye Protectors) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Cycles (Eye Protectors) Regulations 1985 by prescribing two types of eye protectors as authorised for use by persons driving or riding on motor bicycles, in addition to those prescribed by the 1985 Regulations.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.3) (Shorthand Writers' Fees) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the fees for shorthand writers in the Court of Session by increasing certain fees by about 5 per cent.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Shorthand Writers' Fees) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the fees for shorthand writers in the Sheriff Court by increasing certain fees by about 5 per cent.

items.title: The Road Traffic Act 1972 (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: Sections 68 to 81(1) and part of 82 of the Road Traffic Act 1972 were repealed by section 24(3) and Schedule 7 to the Road Traffic Act 1974. Section 9(5) of the 1974 Act enables the Secretary of State by regulations to make such amendments as he considers appropriate to take account of the preceding provisions of that section in any enactment which refers to any provision of sections 68 to 82 of the 1972 Act which was repealed by the 1974 Act.

items.title: The Transport Act 1981 (Commencement No. 12) Order 1988
items.description: This Order appoints 13th July 1988 for the purpose of the coming into force of paragraph 16 of Schedule 9 to the Transport Act 1981. Paragraph 16 amends section 188(6) of the Road Traffic Act 1972 (c. 20) which contains provisions relating to the application of that Act to the Crown).

items.title: The Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A3 Trunk Road (Wandsworth Common North Side, Wandsworth) (Box Junction) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A3 Trunk Road (Wandsworth Common North Side, Wandsworth) (Box Junction) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Gloucester Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Gloucester Harbour Revision Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education Authority Bursaries (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations are made in replacement of the Education Authority Bursaries (Scotland) Regulations 1986, as amended. They prescribe the conditions subject to which bursaries may be made and make provision with regard to requirements to be fulfilled by persons in relation to whom the powers of an education authority under section 49 of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 may be exercised.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Commencement No.2) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force in two stages in Scotland all the provisions of Schedule 6 to the Local Government Act 1988 which are not brought into force by the Act directly.

items.title: The AIDS (Control) (Contents of Reports) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Schedule to the AIDS (Control) Act 1987 so as to secure that reports of the number of positive results of HIV antibody tests are covered by the confidentiality provisions in paragraph 3. The previous Order of 1988, which required this number to be reported without that safeguard, is revoked.

items.title: The Water (Meters) Regulations 1988
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Trunk Road A1081 (Bell Roundabout — London Colney Roundabout, Hertfordshire) (Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Trunk Road A1081 (Bell Roundabout — London Colney Roundabout, Hertfordshire) (Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A41 Trunk Road (Watford Way, Barnet) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A41 Trunk Road (Watford Way, Barnet) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Monetary Limits) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases in Scotland certain monetary limits specified in section 21(2) and 41 of the Gaming Act 1968.

items.title: The Gaming (Small Charges) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the maximum daily charge which may be made in clubs and miners' welfare institutes in Scotland in respect of a person taking part in games of equal chance, except where the only games played are bridge and whist.

items.title: The Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gaming Clubs (Hours and Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 1984 to extend the permitted hours for gaming on bingo club premises in Scotland so that gaming may commence on weekdays and Saturdays at 10 a.m. instead of noon.

items.title: The Amusements with Prizes (Variation of Monetary Limits) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the maximum amount permitted to be taken by way of the sale of chances in any one determination of winners in amusements provided at certain fairs and other commercial entertainments in Scotland under section 16 of the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976.

items.title: The Crown Prosecution Service (Witnesses' Allowances) (Amendment No. 8) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase the maximum locum and compensatory allowances payable under regulation 4 of the Crown Prosecution Service (Witnesses' Allowances) Regulations 1986 to a professional witness who, at the instance of the Crown Prosecution Service, attends court to give evidence and thereby incurs certain expenditure or suffers certain loss as described in that regulation.

items.title: The Education (School Teachers' Pay and Conditions) Order 1988
items.description: This Order refers to a document entitled "School Teachers' Pay and Conditions Document 1988" published by Her Majesty’s Stationery Office, which is obtainable from Government bookshops and through booksellers. The Order, which comes into force on 8th July 1988, directs that the provisions set out in the Document shall have effect, except for the rates of the London Area allowances, from the 1st April 1988 for the purpose of determining the remuneration of school teachers in England and Wales. The rates of the London Area allowances have effect from 1st July 1987. By virtue of section 3(6)(a) of the Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1987, the remuneration of teachers to whom the Order applies is to be determined, and paid to teachers by local education authorities, in accordance with the scales and other provisions set out in the Document.

items.title: The Agriculture Improvement (Variation) Scheme 1988
items.description: This Scheme varies the Agriculture Improvement Scheme 1985 (“the principal Scheme”) and complies with Article 8(2) and (4) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (O.J. No. L93, 30.3.85, p.1), as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1137/88 (O.J. No. L108, 29.4.88, p.1), on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures.

items.title: The Electricity Supply Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Electricity Supply Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Financial Services (Disclosure of Information) (Designated Authorities) (No. 4) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the person appointed by the Secretary of State to investigate and report on the discharge of certain regulatory functions as an authority for the purposes of section 180 of the Financial Services Act 1986 and section 449 of the Companies Act 1985 in relation to the functions of that person in his capacity as such. The effect of designation is to permit the disclosure of information which would otherwise be restricted if disclosure is for the purposes of enabling or assisting the designated authority to discharge the specified functions.

items.title: The Diplomatic and Consular Premises Act 1987 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 3rd February 1988 those provisions of the Act which are not already in force.

items.title: The (A47) Birmingham-Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Easton Hornstocks Bends Improvement) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A47) Birmingham-Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Easton Hornstocks Bends Improvement) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations 1987 by substituting a new regulation 7.

items.title: The Customs Duties (ECSC) (Amendment No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 20th June 1988 reimposes duties on certain iron or steel products originating in Yugoslavia.

items.title: The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Ham Barn-Petersfield Section Slip Roads) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London-Portsmouth Trunk Road A3 (Ham Barn-Petersfield Section Slip Roads) (No. 2) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Special Educational Needs) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Special Educational Needs) Regulations 1983. They permit a child’s statement of special educational needs to be disclosed to enable social services authority officers to undertake the needs assessment of that child required by section 5(5) of the Disabled Persons (Services, Consultation and Representation) Act 1986 on the child ceasing to receive full time education (regulation 2(c) to (e)). They also provide that where a child moves from the area of one local education authority to another the old authority shall transfer to the new authority any statement of special educational needs they maintained in respect of that child (regulation 2(f)). The old authority are also required to inform the new authority if they have received an opinion under section 5(1) or (2) of the Disabled Persons (Services, Consultation and Representation) Act 1986 as to whether or not the child is disabled, or have given notification for the purposes of section 5(5) of that Act of the date the disabled child is expected to leave full-time education (regulation 2(g)).

items.title: The Charities (University of Liverpool) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect to a Scheme of the Charity Commissioners in respect of the University of Liverpool. Under the Liverpool University Act 1931 certain future gifts made to the University are required to be added to a consolidated fund established and regulated by that Act. The Scheme modifies those statutory requirements so that a gift is added to that fund only where the donor of the gift has expressly stipulated that the gift is to form part of the fund.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Increase of Lump Sums) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases from £500 to £1000 the maximum amount a magistrates' court can award as a lump sum for maintenance in proceedings for affiliation, guardianship or custodianship orders, and in domestic proceedings under the Domestic Proceedings and Magistrates' Courts Act 1978.

items.title: The Education (Publication and Consultation Etc.) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Education (Publication and Consultation Etc.) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services and Charges for Drugs) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 which regulate the terms on which doctors and chemists provide general medical services and pharmaceutical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The (A43) Oxford—Market Deeping Trunk Road (Moulton Crossroads to Overstone Grange Improvement) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A43) Oxford—Market Deeping Trunk Road (Moulton Crossroads to Overstone Grange Improvement) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Video Recordings Act 1984 (Scotland) (Commencement No.7) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings the Video Recordings Act 1984 into force in Scotland, in so far as the Act is not already in force, on 1st September 1988. The Act is similarly brought into force on that date in England, Wales and Northern Ireland by the Video Recordings Act 1984 (Commencement No. 7) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1018). At the date of this Order, the Criminal Justice Bill, which inserts a new section, section 16A, in the Video Recordings Act 1984 to provide for the enforcement of that Act by officers of a Weights and Measures Authority and in Northern Ireland by officers of the Department of Economic Development, had not received Royal Assent.

items.title: The Amusements with Prizes (Variation of Fees) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, increases the fee payable to a local authority or other appropriate authority on the grant or renewal of a permit for amusements with prizes provided under section 16 of the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) Order 1988
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, a safety zone, having a radius of 500 metres from a specified point, around the installation specified in the Schedule to this Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, a safety zone, having a radius of 500 metres from a specified point, around the installation specified in the Schedule to this Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: The Crown Office (Forms and Proclamations Rules) Order 1988
items.description: This Order in Council makes Rules which consolidate, with minor modifications, previous Rules made between 1878 and 1975. It prescribes the form of words to be used in certain writs of summons, Commissions of the Peace, Letters Patent and Royal Assent Commissions. It also provides for the publication of Royal Proclamations to take account of changes made in the organisation of local government.

items.title: The Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) Order 1986. It adds the United States of America to the list of the Contracting States to the Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction, The Hague, 25th October 1980 (Cm.33).

items.title: The Environment Protection (Overseas Territories) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends, to the territories specified in Schedule 2 thereto, the provisions of Parts II and IV of the Food and Environment Protection Act 1985, and related Schedules, subject to exceptions, adaptations and modifications.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Tonnage) (Overseas Territories) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends to the territories specified in Schedule 1, with the necessary modifications, section 91 of the Merchant Shipping Act 1970, which amends section 1(5) and (6) of the Merchant Shipping Act 1965, as extended to the territories by the Merchant Shipping (Tonnage) (Overseas Territories) Order 1971. The extension of section 91 enables effect to be given in the territories to the International Convention on Tonnage Measurement of Ships, 1969 (Cmnd. 4332).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1970 (Overseas Territories) Order 1988
items.description: This Order applies, with the necessary modifications, the Merchant Shipping Act 1970 to Anguilla, the Falkland Islands, Montserrat and the British Virgin Islands. This has the effect, principally, of repealing Part II of the Merchant Shipping Act 1894 (c. 60) as it applies there, and replacing the repealed provisions with those of the 1970 Act so as to bring the territories' laws concerning masters and seamen more closely into line with the law in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Lord Chancellor’s Salary Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the total salary payable to the Lord Chancellor under the Ministerial and other Salaries Act 1975 and as Speaker of the House of Lords. The Order provides for the Lord Chancellor to receive a £2,000 lead over the Lord Chief Justice.

items.title: The Terms of Employment of Pilots (Arbitration) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations make provision as respects the referral and determination of disputes as to the terms of employment of pilots under section 5(1) of the Pilotage Act 1987, and the expenses of arbitrations held to determine such disputes.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Fees) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, increases the fee to be charged under the Gaming Act 1968 for the grant or renewal of a permit under section 34 of that Act for premises where gaming machines are used.

items.title: The Veterinary Surgery (Blood Sampling) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which amends the Veterinary Surgery (Blood Sampling) Order 1983, permits the taking of blood from farm animals and the taking of faeces from the rectum of a farm animal by a person who is not qualified as a veterinary surgeon or veterinary practitioner and which would otherwise be prohibited by section 19(1) of the Veterinary Surgeons Act 1966 (article 2).

items.title: The Social Work (Residential Establishments-Child Care) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Work (Residential Establishment-Child Care) (Scotland) Regulations 1987 by deleting the existing definition of “care authority” and substituting an amended version and by deleting the existing definition of “managers” and substituting a new expanded definition.

items.title: The Residential Care Order (Secure Accommodation) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Residential Care Order (Secure Accommodation) (Scotland) Regulations 1988 by deleting the existing definition of “managers” and substituting a new expanded definition.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property) (Tyne and Wear) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers certain land owned by the Tyne and Wear Residuary Body to the district councils in whose area the land is situated. The transfers are in addition to those effected by the Local Government Reorganisation (Property) (Tyne and Wear) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/1288).

items.title: The Sutton District Water Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Sutton District Water Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Ministry of Defence Police (Police Committee) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations relate to the membership of the Ministry of Defence Police Committee.

items.title: The Ministry of Defence Police (Representation at Disciplinary Proceedings) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations lay down the procedure for notifying a member of the Ministry of Defence Police of the rank of chief superintendent or below of his right to be legally represented at a disciplinary hearing where, if found guilty, such member would be liable to a punishment of dismissal, requirement to resign or reduction in rank.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Order 1988 Approved by both Houses of Parliament is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Consolidation) 1988
items.description: This Act of Adjournal re-enacts with minor amendments the provisions of the Acts of Adjournal revoked by rule 168 and Schedule 2 so far as not already revoked, except those relative to fees in inferior Courts which are revoked as unnecessary.

items.title: The Industrial Training Orders (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends paragraph 8 of Schedule 2 to each of the seven industrial training orders mentioned in article 1(2) of the Order. The effect of the amendments is that only those members of the Industrial Training Boards who are appointed by virtue of sub-paragraph (a) of paragraph 3 of Schedule 1 to the Industrial Training Act 1982, after consultation with such organisations or associations of organisations representative of employers engaging in the industry as is required by that sub-paragraph, shall vote on any matter relating to the imposition of a levy.

items.title: The Heavy Goods Vehicles (Drivers' Licences) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Heavy Goods Vehicles (Drivers' Licences) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These regulations amend regulation 33 of the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) Regulations 1980.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Drivers' Licences) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Public Service Vehicles (Drivers' Licences) Regulations 1985 by increasing the fees for driving licences in regulation 11. The fee for an exchange psv driver’s licence in paragraph (1)(a) and the fee for a duplicate psv driver’s licence in paragraph (2) are increased from £3 to £5. The fee for a psv driver’s licence in any other case in paragraph (1)(b) is increased from £10 to £22.50. These increases apply where an application for a licence is received on or after 26th September 1988.

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1987 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st July 1988 section 12 of the Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1987, which substitutes new provisions for section 28 of the Northern Ireland (Emergency Provisions) Act 1978 (c. 5) (providing for compensation for acts done under the 1978 Act interfering with private rights of property). The new provisions provide for a decision by the Secretary of State on an application for compensation and a right of appeal to the county court against his decision. Provision is also made for time limits for applications which may be extended by the Secretary of State or, in case of his refusal, by the county court.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations 1974 (“the 1974 Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which doctors and chemists provide general medical services and pharmaceutical services under the National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1987 for a number of purposes, but principally so as to increase the fees allowable to solicitors for civil legal aid (and for legal aid in certain proceedings relating to children). The overall increase is around 4½% and applies to work done on or after 1st August 1988.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Civil Proceedings) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Civil Proceedings) Regulations 1984 so as to increase the fees allowable to solicitors for legal aid in civil proceedings. The overall increase is around 4 ½% and will apply to work done on or after 1st August 1988.

items.title: The Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1987 (“the principal Regulations”), principally so as to increase the fees allowable to solicitors for criminal legal aid by around 3 1/2% (regulations 4, 5 and 6).

items.title: The Legal Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Regulations 1973.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Criminal Proceedings) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Criminal Proceedings) Regulations 1984 so as to increase the fees allowable to solicitors for legal aid in criminal proceedings given under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1967. The increased fees will apply to proceedings concluded on or after 1st August 1988.

items.title: The Trade Marks and Service Marks (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: Rule 2 of these Rules revises the procedure for renewal of the registration of a trade mark or a service mark. The Registrar must send a reminder notice between one and three months before renewal is due, and the proprietor must renew either by returning, duly completed, the notice sent by the Registrar (or an exact replica of it) or by filing a duly completed, pre-printed, prescribed form, in either case accompanied by the prescribed fee. A revised form (TM 11) is prescribed.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Medical Stores) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Medical Stores) Regulations 1986, which require ships to carry the appropriate medical stores.

items.title: The Employment Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 26th July 1988 the sections of Part I of the Employment Act 1988 set out in the Schedule to the Order and the amendments and repeals, which are related to those sections, set out in the Appendices to the Schedule.

items.title: The Prosecution of Offences Act 1985 (Specified Proceedings) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order adds three offences under the Road Traffic Act 1972 to those contained in the Schedule to the Prosecution of Offences Act 1985 (Specified Proceedings) Order 1985. The effect is that the duty of the Director of Public Prosecutions as head of the Crown Proscecution Service to take over the conduct of all criminal proceedings instituted on behalf of a police force will not include a duty to take over the conduct of proceedings for these further offences.

items.title: The Funds for Trade Union Ballots (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the scheme under which the Certification Officer makes payments to independent trade unions in respect of postal, printing and stationery expenses which they have incurred in holding secret postal ballots for certain purposes. The amendments are largely made to reflect additions and changes to the statutory requirements in respect of certain ballots brought in by the Employment Act 1988 and coming into force on 26th July 1988.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Special Provisions) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order disallows the deduction of input tax on the purchase of goods or services used or to be used for business entertainment.

items.title: The Farm Business Non-Capital Grant Scheme 1988 (revoked)
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Scheme)

items.title: The Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.
