items.title: The Customs Duties (Quota Relief) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st July 1988, provides for the administration of the United Kingdom’s share of the tariff quota opened for the period from 1st July 1988 to 30th June 1989 by the European Economic Community (EEC), under the provisions of the EEC Regulation specified in the Order, providing exemption from customs duty on import into the United Kingdom for home use of rum, arrack and tafia originating in various African, Caribbean and Pacific States (ACP).

items.title: The Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations update the references to the Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) Regulations in the Calibration of Tanks of Vessels (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1975, the Alcoholometers and Alcohol Hydrometers (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1977 and the Taximeters (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1979 by substituting references to the Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1988. They also correct an error in Schedule 1 to the last-mentioned Regulations as to the identifying letters for Greece and Spain, and make a consequential correction to the Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) (Gas Volume Meters) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Dartford-Thurrock Crossing Act 1988 (Appointed Day) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes 31st July 1988 the appointed day for the purposes of section 4(1) of the Dartford-Thurrock Crossing Act 1988 (“the Act”). On that date, known in the Act as the transfer date, the highway through the Dartford Tunnels and the parts of the tunnel approaches which are not already trunk roads become trunk roads for the purposes of the Highways Act 1980 (c. 66).

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988
items.description: These Regulations consolidate and amend regulation 4 of the Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1973. By virtue of the amendments additional documents are required to be produced with an application for a vehicle excise licence under the Vehicles (Excise) Act (Northern Ireland) 1972.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Advice and Assistance (Scotland) Regulations 1987 (“the principal Regulations”) in the following respects.

items.title: The Home-Grown Cereals Authority (Rate of Levy) Order 1988
items.description: For the purposes of financing the Home-Grown Cereals Authority’s non-trading functions under Part I of the Cereals Marketing Act 1965 for the year beginning 1st August 1988, this Order specifies the rates of dealer levy, grower levy and processor levy which appear to Ministers to be sufficient to meet the amounts apportioned to certain cereals grown in the United Kingdom namely, wheat (including durum wheat), barley, oats, rye, maize, triticale or any two or more of such cereals grown as one crop.

items.title: The Immigration Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st August 1988 the provisions of the Immigration Act 1988 specified in the Schedule to this Order, namely sections 1 to 5.

items.title: The Immigration (Restricted Right of Appeal against Deportation) (Exemption) Order 1988
items.description: Section 5(1) of the Immigration Act 1988 (which comes into force on 1st August 1988) provides that a person who was last given leave to enter the United Kingdom less than seven years before the date of the decision to make a deportation order against him by virtue of section 3(5)(a) of the Immigration Act 1971 (breach of limited leave) or by virtue of section 3(5)(c) of that Act as belonging to the family of a person who is or has been ordered to be deported by virtue of section 3(5)(a), shall not be entitled to appeal under section 15 of the 1971 Act except on the ground that on the facts of his case there is in law no power to make the deportation order for the reasons stated in the notice of the decision.

items.title: The Pilotage Act 1987 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Pilotage Act 1987 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Nuclear Installations) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace the Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Atomic Energy Establishments) Regulations 1983 and make the following amendments:— 1The position of the restricted airspace around Coulport is altered so as to include Faslane. 2“Calder/Windscale” is now referred to as“Sellafield” in the Schedule. 3Devonport and Rosyth are added to the Schedule subject to specified exceptions. 4Aircraft may now fly within the restricted airspace for Capenhurst, Chapelcross, Sellafield and Springfields for the purpose of landing at or taking off from the helicopter landing area at the installation with the permission of the person in charge and in accordance with any conditions to which that permission is subject. 5All heights referred to in the Regulations are now calculated as above mean sea level.

items.title: The Revaluation Rate Rebates (Scotland) Order 1987
items.description: This version of the The Revaluation Rate Rebates (Scotland) Order 1987 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Building Societies (Commercial Assets and Services) Order 1988
items.description: This Order empowers building societies to acquire, hold and dispose of four additional forms of commercial asset, and varies Schedule 8 to the Building Societies Act 1986 to increase the range of services which may be provided by societies.

items.title: The Building Societies (Limits on Commercial Assets) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases, in three stages, the percentage limit on commercial assets in classes 2 and 3 which a building society may hold. Commercial assets means, in relation to building societies, assets classified as class 1, 2 or 3. Class 1 assets are advances secured on residential property, class 2 assets are generally advances secured on other land, and class 3 assets generally comprise loans, residential land and interests in bodies corporate.

items.title: The Land Registration (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Land Registration (Scotland) Rules 1980 by altering forms 1, 2, 3 and 6 of Schedule A to take account of necessary changes in layout and content of said forms since their introduction in 1980.

items.title: The Central Manchester Development Corporation (Area and Constitution) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates an area totalling about 180 hectares in the city of Manchester as an urban development area and establishes an urban development corporation to regenerate the area. The area is shown hatched black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Leeds Development Corporation (Area and Consitution) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates two areas totalling about 540 hectares in the city of Leeds as an urban development area and establishes an urban development corporation to regenerate the area. The two areas are shown hatched black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Sheffield Development Corporation (Area and Constitution) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates an area totalling about 900 hectares in the city of Sheffield as an urban development area and establishes an urban development corporation to regenerate the area. The area is shown hatched black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Wolverhampton Urban Development Area Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates three areas totalling about 253 hectares in the borough of Wolverhampton as an urban development area and provides for the functions of an urban development corporation to be performed in the designated area by the Black Country Development Corporation. The three areas are shown hatched black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Building Societies (Transfer of Business) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations which are made under section 102 of, and paragraph 5 of Schedule 17 to, the Building Societies Act 1986 (“the Act”) prescribe certain matters connected with the transfer of the property, rights and liabilities of a building society to a company incorporated under the Companies Acts which may, by virtue of sections 97(3) and (12) of the Act, be either a company specially formed by the society (and by no others than its nominees) for the purpose of assuming and conducting its business in its place or an already existing company carrying on business as a going concern.

items.title: The Crown Office (Preparation and Authentication of Documents Rules) Order 1988
items.description: This Order consolidates, with minor modifications, all the Rules made under section 5 of the Crown Office Act 1877 (as to the preparation and authentication of documents to which the Act applies) which are in current use. The lists of documents specified in the Rules revoked by this Order have been reduced in number by the removal of those documents which have become obsolete or spent or which are no longer required.

items.title: The St. Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) Regulations 1986 (“the principal Regulations”) to uprate the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and making of grants and in certain other respects.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Regulations 1986 (“the principal Regulations”) to uprate the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and making of grants under the assisted places scheme and in certain other respects.

items.title: The Public Utility Transfers and Water Charges Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st October 1988 the following provisions of the Public Utility Transfers and Water Charges Act 1988, together with related amendments —

items.title: The National Savings Bank (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Savings Bank Regulations 1972 by omitting the definition of “trustee savings bank” in regulation 2(1), by revoking regulation 34 and by omitting from regulation 41(2)(c) the reference to stock and securities recorded in the parts of the National Savings Stock Register kept by trustee savings banks.

items.title: The Post Office Register (Trustee Savings Banks) (Revocation) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Post Office Register (Trustee Savings Banks) Regulations 1930 together with the related amending instruments.

items.title: The Trustee Savings Banks Act 1985 (Appointed Day) (No. 6) Order 1988
items.description: This Order appoints 5th July 1988 as the date for the purpose of section 220(2) of the Insolvency Act 1986 and for the repeal of those provisions of the Trustee Savings Banks Act 1981 which are set out in article 2 of the Order.

items.title: The Vaccine Damage Payments (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Vaccine Damage Payments Regulations 1979 so as to provide that members of the tribunals other than the chairman are to be appointed by the President of social security appeal tribunals and medical appeal tribunals or, as the case may be, the President of social security appeal tribunals and medical appeal tribunals for Northern Ireland. Provision is also made for the appointment of officers and staff for vaccine damage tribunals, and for the training of, and supply of works of reference to, tribunal chairmen and other members.

items.title: The AIDS (Control) (Contents of Reports) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Schedule to the AIDS (Control) Act 1987 by specifying additional information to be contained in the reports made by a District Health Authority and a Health Board in pursuance of section 1(2) of the said Act. The additional information concerns the number of positive results obtained in the reporting period from blood samples tested for HIV antibodies.

items.title: The Transport Act 1981 (Commencement No. 13) Order 1988
items.description: This Order appoints 13th July 1988 for the purpose of the coming into force of section 30(3) of the Transport Act 1981 in so far as it is not already in force. Section 30(3) makes consequential and minor amendments to the Road Traffic Act 1972 (c. 20) and section 56 of the Criminal Justice Act 1967 (c. 80).

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Regulations 1987 in the following respects.

items.title: The London—Birmingham Trunk Road (A41) (M25 Interchange) (Trunking and De-trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London—Birmingham Trunk Road (A41) (M25 Interchange) (Trunking and De-trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A405 Trunk Road (Bricket Wood Junction) (Trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A405 Trunk Road (Bricket Wood Junction) (Trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Goods Imported for Private Purposes) Relief Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect to the Community obligation to implement Article 2.2 of Council Directive 77/388/EEC (OJ No. L145, 13.6.1977) as interpreted by the European Court of Justice in Case 15/81 (Gaston Schul Douane-expediteur B.V. -v-Inspecteur der Invoerrechten en Accijnzen [1982] ECR 1409) and Case 47/84 (Staatssecretaris van Financien -v-Gaston Schul Douane-expediteur B.V. [1985] ECR 1501) which established that tax chargeable on the importation of goods for private purposes is to take account of tax paid in a member State which is still contained in the value of the goods at the time of the importation.

items.title: Church of England (Legal Aid) Rules 1988
items.description: This version of the Church of England (Legal Aid) Rules 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A127 London—Southend Trunk Road (Rayleigh Weir Improvement) Slip Roads Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A127 London—Southend Trunk Road (Rayleigh Weir Improvement) Slip Roads Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988 which were defective, and provide that the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 shall have effect as if the (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations had never been made.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 4) Regulations 1988
items.description: These regulations amend regulation 33 of the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Unitary Development Plans (Merseyside) (Appointed Day) Order 1988
items.description: The Local Government Act 1985 abolished the Greater London Council and metropolitan county councils. The Act consequently provides for the replacement in their areas of the existing structure and local development plans under town and country planning legislation by “unitary development plans”. This Order brings the provisions of the 1985 Act relating to unitary development plans into force in the county of Merseyside on 4th August 1988.

items.title: The Pig Carcase (Grading) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Pig Carcase (Grading) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Northern Ireland Act 1974 (Interim Period Extension) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends until 16th July 1989 the period specified in section 1(4) of the Northern Ireland Act 1974 (as extended by the Northern Ireland Act 1974 (Interim Period Extension) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/1207)) for the operation of the temporary provisions for the government of Northern Ireland contained in Schedule 1 to that Act.

items.title: The Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) (Fees) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace with amendments the Measuring Instruments (EEC Initial Verification Requirements) (Fees) Regulations 1982, as amended, and the Measuring Instruments (EEC Pattern Approval Requirements) (Fees) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Pupils' Registration (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Pupils' Registration Regulations 1956 (as amended) make provision for an Admission Register and (in most cases) for an Attendance Register to be maintained by the proprietor of every school.

items.title: The Licensing (Fees) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Licensing (Fees) Order 1978 by prescribing fees chargeable by justices' clerks in relation to orders under section 87A of the Licensing Act 1964 which concerns the variation of permitted hours in on-licensed vineyard premises.

items.title: The Licensing Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st August 1988 the provisions of the Licensing Act 1988 set out in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Licensing (Extended Hours Orders) (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Licensing (Extended Hours Order) Rules 1962 to take account of the abolition of the requirement to renew an extended hours order whenever the justices' licence for the premises is renewed or transferred effected by section 4 of the Licensing Act 1988 (c. 17) which comes into force on 1st August 1988 (S.I. 1988/1187).

items.title: The Nursing Homes and Mental Nursing Homes (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase the fees payable under the Nursing Homes and Mental Nursing Homes Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Residential Care Homes (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Residential Care Homes Regulations 1984. They increase the registration fees payable on applications by managers of homes from £100 to £155, on applications by persons in control of homes from £550 to £570 and on applications on changes of ownership to £570 (regulation 2). They increase the annual fee payable from £15 to £28 multiplied by the maximum number of persons in the home (regulation 3).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Imported Goods) Relief (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: The Value Added Tax (Imported Goods) Relief Order 1984, which implemented certain Community obligations arising under Council Directive No. 83/181/EEC, provides relief from value added tax chargeable on certain goods permanently imported. From 1 January 1988 the Combined Nomenclature replaced the Common Customs Tariff nomenclature and the Value Added Tax (Imported Goods) Relief (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/2108) substituted the new headings and sub-headings in Part II of Schedule 1 to the 1984 Order. This Order amends the definition of “alcoholic beverages” in Article 2 of the 1984 Order to take account of the headings of the Combined Nomenclature under which those beverages are now classified.

items.title: The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) Order 1988
items.description: This Order consolidates the Orders revoked by article 5 and makes further provision as to the bodies corporate which a building society may invest in or support under section 18 of the Building Societies Act 1986.

items.title: The Building Societies (Limits on Lending) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases from £10,000 to £15,000 the limit on loans which a building society may make to an individual under section 15 of the Building Societies Act 1986 for the purchase of a mobile home, and increases from £5,000 to £10,000 the limit on loans (other than mobile home loans and loans fully secured on land) which a building society may make to an individual under section 16 of the 1986 Act.

items.title: The Blood Tests (Evidence of Paternity) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase the charges which may be made under the Blood Tests (Evidence of Paternity) Regulations 1971 in respect of blood tests carried out for the purpose of determining paternity in civil proceedings. These were last increased in August 1987 (S.I. 1987/1199).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Assessment of Environmental Effects) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations are concerned with the implementation in England and Wales of Council Directive 85/337/EEC (OJ No. L175, 5.7.85, p. 40.) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment.

items.title: The Capacity Serving Measures (Intoxicating Liquor) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Capacity Serving Measures (Intoxicating Liquor) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 (Codes of Practice) Order 1988
items.description: This order appoints as the date on which the code of practice for the tape-recording of police interviews under section 60(1) of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 will come into operation.

items.title: The Agriculture Improvement (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Agriculture Improvement Regulations 1985 (“the principal Regulations”), implementing in part the provisions of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (O.J. No. L93, 30.3.85, p.1), as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1137/88 (O.J. No. L108, 29.4.88, p.1), on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures.

items.title: The Immigration (Restricted Right of Appeal against Deportation) (Exemption) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: Section 5(1) of the Immigration Act 1988 (which comes into force on 1st August 1988) provides that a person who was last given leave to enter the United Kingdom less than seven years before the date of the decision to make a deportation order against him by virtue of section 3(5)(a) of the Immigration Act 1971 (breach of limited leave) or by virtue of section 3(5)(c) of that Act as belonging to the family of a person who is or has been ordered to be deported by virtue of section 3(5)(a), shall not be entitled to appeal under section 15 of the 1971 Act except on the ground that on the facts of his case there is in law no power to make the deportation order for the reasons stated in the notice of the decision.

items.title: The Meters (Determination of Questions) (Expenses) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Meters (Determination of Questions) (Expenses) Regulations 1987. They provide for Electricity Boards to make monthly payments towards administrative expenses incurred by the Secretary of State in connection with the determination of questions as to the correctness of electricity meters. The amount payable by each Board as compared with the amount payable under the Regulations of 1987 is increased or reduced, according to their use of the Electricity Meter Examining Service in the previous year.

items.title: The Environmental Assessment (Afforestation) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout Great Britain, implement in relation to initial afforestation the Council Directive 85/337/EEC on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment, (O.J. No. L175, 5.7.85, p. 40).

items.title: The East of Birmingham/Birkenhead Trunk Road (A41 Improvement at Chester and Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The East of Birmingham/Birkenhead Trunk Road (A41 Improvement at Chester and Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Assisted Places) Regulations 1985 in respect of a school year beginning on or after 1 st September 1988.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Incidental Expenses) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (Assisted Places) (Incidental Expenses) Regulations 1985. They come into force on 1st August 1988 and apply to a school year beginning on or after that date (regulation 1).

items.title: The Education (Grants) (Music and Ballet Schools) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education (Grants) (Music and Ballet Schools) Regulations 1985. They come into force on 1st August 1988 and apply to a school year beginning on or after that date.

items.title: The Petroleum (Production) (Seaward Areas) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the provisions of the Petroleum (Production) Regulations 1982 (as amended) in relation to (a) applications to the Secretary of State for petroleum production licences in respect of seaward areas and (b) applications to the Secretary of State for petroleum exploration licences in respect of seaward areas and landward areas below the low water line.

items.title: The Education Support Grants (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 4 of the Education Support Grant Regulations 1984 so as to provide that for all expenditure incurred before 1st April 1988 grant shall be payable at the rate of 70 per cent. Expenditure incurred on or after that date in respect of item 4(a) of the Schedule (pilot projects to provide records of achievement for pupils in secondary schools or students at further education establishments) is to be payable at the rate of 50 per cent. Before this amendment that rate applied to such expenditure whenever it was incurred.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Citizens' Band and Amateur Apparatus) (Various Provisions) Order 1988
items.description: This Order restricts the import, manufacture, sale, hire, offer or advertisement for sale or hire, custody and control of certain specified Citizens' Band (“CB”) radio apparatus and the import and manufacture of certain specified amateur radio apparatus. The CB apparatus restricted is that which is designed or adapted, or has facilities permitting its adaptation, for the purpose of transmitting spoken messages in the frequency band 26.1 MHz to 28 MHz. This CB apparatus is restricted unless it operates only on one or more of the frequencies specified in Performance Specifications MPT 1320 or MPT 1333 and complies with the requirements of Schedule 1 to the Order. CB apparatus which falls within the purview of MPT 1333 must also be type approved by the Secretary of State for the purposes of the Order. The manufacture and import of amateur radio apparatus which operates only in the frequency band 28 MHz to 29.7 MHz is also restricted.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Control of Interference from Citizens' Band Radio Apparatus) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Wireless Telegraphy (Control of Interference from Citizens' Band Radio Apparatus) Regulations 1982 (“the principal Regulations”) (S.I. 1982/635). The principal Regulations, as amended, prescribe the technical requirements to be observed by persons who manufacture, assemble or import Citizens' Band (“CB”) radio apparatus which is designed or adapted to operate in the frequency band 934 MHz to 935 MHz (previously 934.025 MHz to 934.975 MHz). The technical requirements are based on those contained in Performance Specification MPT 1321.

items.title: The Land Drainage Improvement Works (Assessment of Environmental Effects) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations implement for England and Wales, in respect of land drainage improvement works proposed by a water authority, internal drainage board or local authority (“the drainage body”), the Council Directive 85/337/EEC on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment (O.J. No. L175, 5.7.85, p.40). They provide a procedure for the provision of an environmental statement covering the matters specified in the Schedule, relating to such improvements by the drainage body where that body considers that by reason of, inter alia, their nature, size or location the proposed works are likely to have a significant effect on the environment (regulation 3).

items.title: The Environmental Assessment (Salmon Farming in Marine Waters) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Regulations implement for Great Britain Council Directive 85/337/EEC (O.J. No. L175, 5.7.85, p.40) on the assessment of the environmental effects of certain projects in respect of salmon farming in marine waters.

items.title: The British Waterways Board (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: The aggregate rateable values of the lands and heritages comprising the operational under-taking of the British Waterways Board in Scotland are, by virtue of the British Waterways Board (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1985, calculated by a formula applied to the Board’s gross receipts in the years which are two and three years prior to the year in respect of which the aggregate rateable value is being determined. The formula provides however for the receipts in each year to be adjusted by reference to the retail prices index for September of that year (in order to counteract the effect of inflation).

items.title: The M53 Motorway (Southern Terminal Roundabout Section) Scheme 1988
items.description: This version of the The M53 Motorway (Southern Terminal Roundabout Section) Scheme 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Docks and Harbours (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: The rateable values of lands and heritages occupied by statutory dock and harbour under-takings are, by virtue of the Docks and Harbours (Rateable Values) (Scotland) Order 1985, calculated by a formula applied to the undertaking’s relevant income in the year preceding the year in respect of which the rateable values are being determined. That income is adjusted by reference to the retail prices index for September 1984 (in order to counteract the effect of inflation).

items.title: The Environmental Assessment (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Regulations implement, for Scotland, Council Directive No. 85/337/EEC (O.J. No. L175, 5.7.85, p. 40) (“the directive”) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment.

items.title: The Health and Safety (Training for Employment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations give to certain trainees who are not employees the protection for and duties of employees under the relevant statutory provisions as defined in the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974.

items.title: The Bournemouth and District Water Company (Amendment of Local Enactments) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Bournemouth and District Water Company (Amendment of Local Enactments) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Newcastle and Gateshead Water (Birch Trees No. 3 Spring) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Newcastle and Gateshead Water (Birch Trees No. 3 Spring) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into force on 12th September 1988 of the following provisions of the Social Security Act 1988 —

items.title: The Child Benefit (General) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Child Benefit (General) Regulations 1976 (the principal Regulations). They define“remunerative work” and“youth training scheme” (regulation 2); revise the circumstances in which child benefit is not payable for a period of interruption of full-time education (regulation 3); and substitute a reference to remunerative work for a reference to“full-time gainful employment” in regulation 7(3) (regulation 4). They also substitute a new regulation 7B to provide that child benefit shall not be payable in respect of a child who is receiving training under the youth training scheme in respect of which an allowance may be paid (regulation 5); and add a new regulation 7D to provide for a person to be treated as a child during a further period where that person is registered for work or training (regulation 6). In addition, the Regulations incorporate regulation 12 of the Child Benefit (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1984 (modification of section 6(3) of the Child Benefit Act 1975 regarding the rule that only one person is entitled to receive child benefit in respect of a child) with technical amendments into the principal Regulations (regulation 7) and revoke the Child Benefit (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1984 in so far as they have not yet been revoked (regulation 8).

items.title: The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 3 Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987. They are made before the end of a period of 12 months from commencement of the enactments under which they are made and are therefore exempt, under section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986, from the requirement in section 10 of the Social Security Act 1980 to refer proposals to make regulations to the Social Security Advisory Committee.

items.title: The Income Support (Transitional) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations make transitional provision in connection with the commencement on 12th September 1988 of section 4(1) and (2) of the Social Security Act 1988, which relates to income support for persons aged 16 or 17 years. They provide for a claimant who is a member of a couple or a polygamous marriage before that date and whose partner is under the age of 18 to continue to receive income support after that date at the rate appropriate to persons over 18 (regulations 2 and 3). They also contain provision for the adjudicating authorities to review current awards of income support to persons under 18 in advance of, but with effect from, 12th September 1988 (regulation 4). These regulations are made before the expiry of 12 months from the commencement of the provisions under which they are made: they are accordingly exempt, by section 61(5) of the Social Security Act 1986, from reference to the Social Security Advisory Committee and have not been so referred.

items.title: The Housing Revenue Account Rate Fund Contribution Limits (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order limits the amount of contribution from their general fund to the housing revenue account which local authorities may include in their estimates for the year 1988-89.

items.title: The Social Security (Credits) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Credits) Regulations 1975 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 13) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie above the Beryl oilfield, approximately 130 miles east of the Orkney Islands.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 14) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-lines lie above the Buchan oilfield, situated north-east of Peterhead, and between that oilfield and the Forties oilfield.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 15) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-line lies between two platforms above the Sean oilfield, which is situated east of the coast of Lincolnshire.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 16) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-line lies above the Fulmar oilfield, which is situated east of the coast of the Fife Region of Scotland.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 17) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-line lies between the Indefatigable and Leman gasfields, situated north-east of the Norfolk coast.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 18) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 19) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie above the Leman Bank gasfield, situated north-east of the Norfolk coast.

items.title: The Homes Insulation Grants Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies the amount of £144 (instead of the amount of £137 provided by S.I. 1987/2185) as the maximum money sum available by way of grant under the Homes Insulation Scheme 1987 as amended.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies (Approved Bodies) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: Assured tenancies are tenancies which, but for section 56 of the Housing Act 1980, would have been protected tenancies or housing association tenancies under the Rent Act 1977 (c. 42), and which meet certain other conditions. Such tenancies are subject to Part II of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954 (c. 56), as modified by Schedule 5 to the 1980 Act. They can only be granted by bodies approved under section 56 of the 1980 Act.

items.title: The Highways (Assessment of Environmental Effects) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations implement for England and Wales Council Directive 85/337/EEC of the 27th June 1985 (OJ No. L175, 5.7.85, p.4O) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment, in respect of those proposals to construct new highways and to improve highways to which the Directive applies in so far as those proposals are those of the Secretary of State. The Regulations amend the Highways Act 1980. The effect of those amendments will be that in any case where the Secretary of State has under consideration the construction of a new highway or the improvement of an existing highway he shall determine before the date of publication of details of the project whether or not it requires an environmental assessment under the Council Directive. If the Secretary of State determines that the project does require such an assessment he shall publish an environmental statement and ensure that the public concerned (and if on completion of the project any part of the highway would lie on, or within 100 metres of, certain categories of land, also the appropriate environmental body) is given an opportunity to express an opinion before the project is initiated. The Secretary of State shall consider the environmental statement, any opinion expressed by a member of the public and, where obtained, by the appropriate environmental body, and publish his decision as to whether or not to initiate the project.

items.title: The Gipsy Encampments (District of Lewes) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the District of Lewes as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Development) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is linked with the Environmental Assessment (Scotland) Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”), which are concerned with the implementation in Scotland of the provisions of Council Directive No. 85/337/EEC (O.J. No. L175, 5.7.85, p.40) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment.

items.title: The Gaming (Amendment) Act 1986 (Commencement) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings the Gaming (Amendment) Act 1986 into force on 19th September 1988.

items.title: The Gaming (Records of Cheques) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply in England and Wales, require the holder of a licence under the Gaming Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”) in respect of any premises, except bingo premises, to make and retain for the period specified in regulation 3(2) the records required by regulations 4, 5 and 6 of these Regulations.

items.title: The Redundant Mineworkers and Concessionary Coal (Payments Schemes) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Redundant Mineworkers and Concessionary Coal Payments Schemes scheduled to the Redundant Mineworkers and Concessionary Coal (Payments Schemes) Orders 1978, 1983, 1984 and 1986.

items.title: The London—Penzance Trunk Road A30 (Penhale to Carland Cross Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London—Penzance Trunk Road A30 (Penhale to Carland Cross Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London-Penzance Trunk Road A30 (Penhale to Carland Cross Improvement) (Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London-Penzance Trunk Road A30 (Penhale to Carland Cross Improvement) (Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Housing (Contributions Towards Expenditure for Area Improvement) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Housing (Contributions Towards Expenditure for Area Improvement) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Customs Duties (Spain and Portugal) (Revocation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order revokes the Customs Duties (Spain and Portugal) Order 1987. The Order is replaced by the Customs Duties (Spain) Order 1988 and the Customs Duties (Portugal) Order 1988 which provide for the reductions in the customs duties charged on the importation of goods into the United Kingdom from Spain and Portugal respectively pursuant to Council Regulation (EEC) No. 839/88 (O.J. No. L87 31.3.1988, p.1.).

items.title: The Customs Duties (Portugal) Order 1988
items.description: This Order implements obligations of the United Kingdom concerning Portugal under the Treaty of Accession of Spain and Portugal to the European Communities from 10th August 1988 until 31st December 1995. It provides for the reductions in the customs duties charged on the importation of goods into the United Kingdom from Portugal which are to be made under the Act annexed to the Treaty from 10th August 1988 and from the beginning of each subsequent calendar year during the remainder of the transitional period (see European Communities No. 27 and 28 (1985) Cmnd 9634 and 9635). Article 6 of the Customs Duties (Spain and Portugal) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/2183) would be effective to implement the reduction in customs duties determined by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 839/88 (OJ No. L87, 31.3.1988, p.1) from 1st July 1988 to the end of 1988 but as from 10th August 1988 this Order replaces as far as Portugal is concerned the Customs Duties (Spain and Portugal) Order, (which is revoked by the Customs Duties (Spain and Portugal) (Revocation) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1259)) in order to make known the actual rates of duty, which are shown in the Schedules to this Order.

items.title: The Customs Duties (Spain) Order 1988
items.description: This Order implements obligations of the United Kingdom concerning Spain under the Treaty of Accession of Spain and Portugal to the European Communities from 10th August 1988 up to and including 31st December 1995. It provides for the reductions in the customs duties charged on the importation of goods into the United Kingdom from Spain which are to be made under the Act annexed to the Treaty from 10th August 1988 and from the beginning of each subsequent calendar year during the remainder of the transitional period, (see European Communities No. 27 and 28 (1985) Cmnd 9634 and 9635). Article 6 of the Customs Duties (Spain and Portugal) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/2183) would be effective to implement the reduction in customs duties determined by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 839/88 (OJ No. L87, 31.3.1988, p. 1) from 1st July 1988 to the end of 1988 but as from 10th August 1988 this Order replaces as far as Spain is concerned the Customs Duties (Spain and Portugal) Order, (which is revoked by the Customs Duties (Spain and Portugal) (Revocation) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1259) in order to make known the actual rates of duty, which are shown in the Schedules to this Order.

items.title: The Children and Young Persons (Amendment) Act 1986 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings sections 2 and 3 of the Children and Young Persons (Amendment) Act 1986 into force on 1st August 1988. Section 2 provides for a parent or guardian to have an automatic right of appeal from care proceedings. Section 3 enables a parent or guardian to be made a party to care proceedings when there is a conflict of interest between the parent and child. Further, section 3 provides that grandparents may, by leave of the court and in accordance with criteria given in Rules, become parties to care proceedings.

items.title: The Grants by Local Housing Authorities (Eligible Expense Limits) Order 1988
items.description: Grants for the improvement, repair or conversion of a dwelling or a house in multiple occupation are made by local housing authorities under Part XV of the Housing Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”). The amount, or in some cases the maximum amount, of a grant is the appropriate percentage of the estimated expense which is eligible to be taken into account.

items.title: The Sole (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Sole (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Prescribed Forms) (Welsh Forms) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Housing (Right to Buy) (Prescribed Forms) (Welsh Forms) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Cereals Co-responsibility Levy (Certified Seed Exemption) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations exempt, pursuant to the provisions of Articles 1 and 9(1) of Commission Regulation (EEC) 1432/88 (OJ No. L131, 27.5.88, p.37), from the co-responsibility levy imposed by Articles 4 and 4b of Council Regulation (EEC) 2727/75 (OJ No. L281, 1.11.75, p.1) 84 per cent of the cereal seed delivered to a seed merchant under multiplication contracts provided that at least 75 per cent of the seed purchased by that merchant is certified as seed and sold as such.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 11 of the Clean Air Act 1956 makes the occupier of a building within a smoke control area guilty of an offence if smoke is emitted from the chimney of that building, unless it can be proved that the emission of smoke was caused solely by the use of an authorised fuel. These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, add the fuels known as Ancit 40, Ancit 60, Centurion, Firegold and Flamelite to the list of authorised fuels.

items.title: The Electricity (Miscellaneous Pension Schemes) (Winding Up) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide for the winding up of 44 pension schemes most of which were established by authorised undertakers, power station companies or electricity holding companies prior to vesting day, 1st April 1948, under the Electricity Act 1947 to provide pension benefits for their employees.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order is linked with the Town and Country Planning (Assessment of Environmental Effects) Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”), which are concerned with the implementation in England and Wales of the provisions of Council Directive 85/337/EEC (OJ No. L175, 5.7.85, p.40) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment.

items.title: Ecclesiastical Judges and Legal Officers (Fees) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the fees fixed by the Ecclesiastical Judges and Legal Officers (Fees) Order 1987. It also fixes fees for cases where the chancellor orders a petition for a faculty to be disposed of on written representations, without an oral hearing, under the new procedure introduced by The Faculty Jurisdiction (Amendment) Rules 1987.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Weighing of Goods Vehicles and other Cargo) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations apply to United Kingdom Class II and II(A) ro/ro passenger ships, and require the weighing of all road vehicles (except buses) and all non-vehicular items of cargo exceeding, in each case, 7.5 tonnes for all voyages on which passengers are carried. Subject to certain exceptions, cargo must be weighed within the port at which the ship is to be loaded, and the weighing machine must be certified, and operated in accordance with conditions laid down, by an inspector of weights and measures.

items.title: The Estate Duty (Interest on Unpaid Duty) Order 1988
items.description: Estate Duty is chargeable in relation to deaths before 13th March 1975; it was replaced for deaths on or after that date by capital transfer tax, now known as inheritance tax. Article 2 of this Order provides that interest on unpaid estate duty will run at 8 per cent. (instead of at the previous rate of 6 per cent. as imposed by S.I. 1987/892) under the various provisions which impose a fixed rate of interest. By Article 3 the limit on the discretionary rate of interest which may be charged where payment of estate duty is postponed on grounds of hardship is correspondingly altered by substituting 8 per cent. for 6 per cent. The effect of this Order in conjunction with section 48(1) of the Finance Act 1975 (c. 7) is that from 6th August 1988 interest will also run at 8 per cent. when overpaid estate duty is repaid.

items.title: The Estate Duty (Northern Ireland) (Interest on Unpaid Duty) Order 1988
items.description: Estate Duty is chargeable in relation to deaths before 13th March 1975; it was replaced for deaths on or after that date by capital transfer tax, now known as inheritance tax. Article 2 of this Order provides that interest on unpaid estate duty will run at 8 per cent. (instead of at the previous rate of 6 per cent. as imposed by S.I. 1987/893) under the various provisions which impose a fixed rate of interest. By Article 3 the limit on the discretionary rate of interest which may be charged where payment of estate duty is postponed on grounds of hardship is correspondingly altered by substituting 8 per cent. for 6 per cent. The effect of this Order in conjunction with section 48(1) of the Finance Act 1975 (c. 7) is that from 6th August 1988 interest will also run at 8 per cent. when overpaid estate duty is repaid.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest on Unpaid Tax and Repayment Supplement) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: On and after 6th August 1988 this Order increases from 7.75 per cent. per annum to 9.75 per cent. per annum the rate of interest chargeable on unpaid income tax, surtax, capital gains tax, corporation tax (including advance corporation tax), development land tax, petroleum revenue tax, profits tax, excess profits tax, excess profits levy and on overpaid development land tax. The Order also provides that on and after 6th August 1988 supplement in respect of repayments of certain taxes will be at the rate of 9.75 per cent. per annum instead of 7.75 per cent. per annum.

items.title: The Income Tax (Official Rate of Interest on Beneficial Loans) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: By this Order the official rate of interest for the purposes of section 160 of, and Schedule 7 to, the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (charge to income tax on beneficial loans) is prescribed to be 12 per cent. per annum on and after 6th August 1988.

items.title: The Measuring Equipment (Liquid Fuel and Lubricants) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace the Measuring Equipment (Liquid Fuel and Lubricants) Regulations 1983 as amended. They apply to all measuring equipment, other than capacity measures and those used for the measurement of liquefied petroleum gas, for use for trade in the making of any measurement of liquid fuel or lubricants or any mixture of such fuel and lubricants in a quantity not exceeding 20 gallons or 100 litres. They make provision as to the principles of construction and marking of measuring equipment, inspection, testing, passing as fit for use for trade and stamping of such equipment (and obliteration of stamps on such equipment), and the prescribed limits of error. The Regulations prescribe this equipment for the purposes of section 11(1) of the Weights and Measures Act 1985 so that it is unlawful to use it for trade purposes unless it has been tested, passed as fit for such use and stamped by an inspector of weights and measures.

items.title: The Inheritance Tax and Capital Transfer Tax (Interest on Unpaid Tax) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases with effect from 6th August 1988 the annual rate of interest on unpaid inheritance tax, or unpaid capital transfer tax where the liability to tax arose before 25th July 1986, payable under section 233 of the Inheritance Tax Act 1984 (formerly the Capital Transfer Tax Act 1984). The annual rate is increased to 8 per cent. This increase also has effect in relation to repayments of capital transfer tax or inheritance tax since, by virtue of section 235 of the Act, these carry interest at the rate applicable to unpaid tax under section 233. Section 236 of the Act, as amended by paragraph 33 of Schedule 19 to the Finance Act 1986, applies the provisions of section 233 to certain special cases where tax has been overpaid or underpaid.

items.title: The Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Interest on Tax Repaid) (No.2) Order 1988
items.description: Section 92 of the Finance Act 1986 provides that, if certain conditions have been fulfilled and a claim for repayment is made within the stipulated period, stamp duty reserve tax shall be repaid. Where the tax paid is not less than £25, it shall be repaid with interest at the appropriate rate. The section defines the "appropriate rate" as 11 per cent. per annum or such other rate as the Treasury may from time to time specify by order.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Training) Order 1988
items.description: This Order exempts from VAT supplies of training or retraining, and goods or services essential thereto, provided under schemes arranged by the Training Commission and the Department of Economic Development for Northern Ireland and paid for by those bodies.

items.title: The Landlord and Tenant Act 1987 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st September 1988 those provisions of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1987 which are not already in force, except section 42 of that Act (service charge contributions to be held on trust) which it brings into force on 1st April 1989. Sections 41, 43 and 44, brought into force on 1st September 1988, concern amendments relating to service charges, and the insurance of, and appointment of managing agents for, leasehold properties. The Schedule to the Order contains transitional provisions to disapply amendments made by section 41 and Schedule 2 (relating to service charges) in respect of certain actions taken before 1st September 1988 and certain periods which began before that date.

items.title: The Service Charge Contributions (Authorised Investments) Order 1988
items.description: The Landlord and Tenant Act 1987 provides that service charge contributions made by tenants are to be held in trust in certain cases. Section 42 enables the Secretary of State to prescribe ways in which contributions held in the trust fund may be invested in addition to those generally authorised for the investment of trust funds. This Order enables the contributions to be invested in a deposit account with a body or person referred to in article 2 of the Order or in a share account with a building society.

items.title: The Service Charge (Estimates and Consultation) Order 1988
items.description: Section 20 of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1985 limits the amount of service charges payable by tenants of dwellings by reference to the relevant costs of any qualifying works. Where the costs exceed £25 multiplied by the number of dwellings let to the tenants concerned or, if more, £500, the excess is irrecoverable unless certain requirements are complied with or dispensed with by the court. This Order increases the limits of £25 and £500 to £50 and £1,000 respectively except in the case described.

items.title: The Rate Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Rate Support Grant (Scotland) Order 1988 Approved by the House of Commons is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No.5) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 by making special provisions in relation to 3 axle semi-trailers that are fitted with air or other compressible fluid suspension systems and meet certain other requirements.

items.title: The Water (Meters) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace regulation 7 of the Water (Meters) Regulations 1988, which provides for water undertakers to recover in certain cases the expenses reasonably incurred in testing water meters installed on or after 11th July 1988, subject to certain monetary limits. The new regulation restricts those limits to the testing of domestic meters.

items.title: The Farm Woodland Scheme 1988
items.description: This Scheme, which applies to Great Britain and operates from 1st October 1988, provides for the payment of annual grants to owners or lessees of agricultural land which has been converted to farm woodlands for abating any financial loss which has been or will be suffered in consequence of that conversion.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Amendment No.3 Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Order 1987 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Army, Air Force and Naval Discipline Acts (Continuation) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Army, Air Force and Naval Discipline Acts (Continuation) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Naval Medical Compassionate Fund (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Standing Orders and Regulations of the Naval Medical Compassionate Fund. It provides for the Assistant Under Secretary of State (Naval Personnel) for the time being to be a trustee of the Fund instead of the Accountant General of the Navy for the time being. It also enables a person to become a subscriber for life by paying a single lump sum.

items.title: The Service Departments Registers (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Service Departments Register Order 1959. The principal changes are:—1Spent references to the Admiralty, Army Council and Air Council have been replaced by reference to the Defence Council and the reference to the Minister of Aviation has been revoked.2The reference to “column” contained in article 5(3) has been omitted to reflect the change in the layout of the register.3The list of welfare organisations contained in Part II of the Schedule has been brought up to date by removing some references and adding the Services Sound and Vision Corporation.4That amended list takes effect now to apply to persons serving abroad in an organisation concerned with the welfare of the armed forces there (and their families) in whose case records of births as well as deaths may be kept there under the Order (previously deaths only were recorded).

items.title: The Antarctic Treaty (Agreed Measures) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes it an offence to enter, without a permit, into a specially protected area designated under section 7(2) of the Antarctic Treaty Act 1967 (being an area of outstanding scientific interest for purposes of the Agreed Measures for the Conservation of Antarctic Fauna and Flora). It also makes provision with respect to permits authorising entry into such areas.

items.title: The Copyright (Singapore) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends to Bermuda the Copyright (Singapore) Order 1987, which provides for the copyright protection in the United Kingdom and other dependent countries of the Commonwealth of works and other subject-matter originating in Singapore.

items.title: The EUMETSAT (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1988
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities on the European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites (EUMETSAT), on representatives of its Members and on its staff and experts. These privileges and immunities are conferred in accordance with the Protocol on the Privileges and Immunities of the European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites (EUMETSAT) (Cm. 397) which was opened for signature at Darmstadt on 1st December 1986 and in accordance with Article 1(3) of the Convention for the Establishment of a European Organisation for the Exploitation of Meteorological Satellites (EUMETSAT) (Cmnd. 9203), as corrected by a Procès-Verbal of Rectification signed at Berne on 17th October 1984 (Cmnd. 9483). The Order revokes the EUMETSAT (Legal Capacities) Order 1985 (S.I. 1985/750). It will enable Her Majesty’s Government to give effect to the Protocol and will come into operation on the date on which the Protocol enters into force in respect of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The EUTELSAT (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1988
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities on the European Telecommunications Satellite Organisation “EUTELSAT”, on representatives of its Parties and its Signatories and on its staff members and experts. These privileges and immunities are conferred in accordance with the Protocol on the Privileges and Immunities of the European Telecommunications Satellite Organisation (EUTELSAT) (Cm. 305) which was opened for signature at Paris on 13th February 1987 and in accordance with Articles IV and XVII of the Convention on the European Telecommunications Satellite Organisation “EUTELSAT” (Cmnd. 9069). The Order revokes the EUTELSAT (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1984 (S.I. 1984/1980). It will enable Her Majesty’s Government to give effect to the Protocol and will come into operation on the date on which the Protocol enters into force in respect of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) (Scotland) Regulations 1982 which prescribe charges for some overseas visitors for services forming part of the National Health Service.

items.title: The Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (Overseas Territories) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends the provision of sections 3, 4, 5 and 6 of the Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency Act 1988, subject to modifications and exceptions, to the British Virgin Islands, St Helena and the Turks and Caicos Islands.

items.title: The Reciprocal Enforcement of Foreign Judgments (Canada) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends article 3 of the Reciprocal Enforcement of Foreign Judgments (Canada) Order 1987 so as to include the courts of the Province of Prince Edward Island as recognised courts.

items.title: The General Optical Council (Contact Lens (Qualifications etc.) Rules) Order of Council 1988
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order prescribe the qualifications required and certification procedures necessary to permit the fitting of contact lenses by registered opticians. The Rules also provide for a period of patient after-care lasting six months from the date when contact lenses were first fitted and make consequential amendments to the Rules on the Fitting of Contact Lenses 1985 (scheduled to S.I 1985/856).

items.title: The Local Government Administration (Matters Subject to Investigation) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 5 to the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1975 so that action taken in connection with the investigation or prevention of crime, hitherto totally excluded from investigation by the Commissioner for Local Administration in Scotland, is so excluded only if it is undertaken by a police authority or a joint police committee.

items.title: The Copyright (International Conventions) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Copyright (International Conventions) Order 1979 to take account of the accession of Peru and of Trinidad and Tobago to the Berne Copyright Convention.

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Borough of Sandwell) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the council of the borough of Sandwell or any other local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Borough of Walsall) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the council of the borough of Walsall or any other local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Swansea-Manchester Trunk Road (A483) and the Shrewsbury-Dolgellau Trunk Road (A458) (Welshpool North-South Relief Road) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Swansea-Manchester Trunk Road (A483) and the Shrewsbury-Dolgellau Trunk Road (A458) (Welshpool North-South Relief Road) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (British Railways Board) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the British Railways Board or any local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Central Electricity Generating Board) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the Central Electricity Generating Board, or any local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (General) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the councils of the boroughs of Sandwell and Walsall, the British Waterways Board, the Severn—Trent Water Authority or any other local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Port of Tyne Authority) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the Port of Tyne Authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Customs Duties (ECSC) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 28th July 1988, reimposes duties on certain categories of iron or steel products originating in Yugoslavia.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (British Coal Corporation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the British Coal Corporation or any local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (British Shipbuilders and British Steel Corporation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in British Shipbuilders and the British Steel Corporation or any local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (British Steel Corporation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Black Country Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the British Steel Corporation or any local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (City of Newcastle upon Tyne) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the council of the city of Newcastle upon Tyne or any other local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Various Local Authorities) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the council of the city of Newcastle upon Tyne, the borough of North Tyneside and the borough of South Tyneside or any other local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Milk Marketing Scheme (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations come into force on 29th February 1988 and further amend the Milk Marketing Scheme 1933 which applies to England and Wales.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Borough of Sunderland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the borough of Sunderland, or any other local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Development Corporation (Vesting of Land) (Tyne and Wear Passenger Transport Executive) Order 1988
items.description: This Order vests in the Tyne and Wear Development Corporation certain land which is at present vested in the Tyne and Wear Passenger Transport Executive or any local authority or other body mentioned in section 141(1) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, and related easements and similar rights. The land is shown coloured black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Crown Court Rules 1982 to take account of section 67B of the Licensing Act 1964 which confers a right of appeal to the Crown Court against a decision of licensing justices or a magistrates' court to grant a restriction order under section 67A of that Act (which restricts the afternoon permitted hours in licensed premises or a registered club) or as to the terms of such an order. (Section 67B has similar application in relation to licensed canteens by virtue of section 156A of the 1964 Act.)

items.title: The Public Charitable Collections (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide for the exemption of banks from the requirement to record the contents of individual collecting boxes when opening and counting their contents on behalf of the organisers of public charitable collections.

items.title: The Furniture and Furnishings (Fire) (Safety) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations (“the Regulations”) which revoke the Upholstered Furniture (Safety) Regulations 1980 as amended by the Upholstered Furniture (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 1983 impose requirements in relation to new domestic furniture, including garden furniture which may be used indoors and furniture for use in caravans, subject to exceptions from some of the Regulations for certain furniture. Some of the requirements apply to second-hand furniture. The Regulations do not apply to goods for export or to the supply of goods made before 1st January 1950 or to the supply of materials intended for re-upholstering furniture made before that date.

items.title: Parochial Fees Order 1988
items.description: Article 1 and the Schedule establish a new table of fees payable for certain matters in connection with baptisms, marriages and burials, for the erection of monuments in churchyards and for other miscellaneous matters. The fees are in substitution for fees prescribed by the Parochial Fees Order 1986.

items.title: The Matrimonial Causes (Costs) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules revoke the Matrimonial Causes (Costs) Rules 1979, subject to the transitional provisions in rule 1, and make fresh provision for the taxation of costs in matrimonial proceedings in the High Court and county courts following closely the revision of Order 62 of the Rules of the Supreme Court by the Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment) 1986, S.I. 1986/632.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Amendment No. 3 Order 1988
