items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Order 1987, as amended by S.I. 1988/9 and 951, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Commission for Local Authority Accounts in Scotland Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to how the expenses of the Commission for Local Authority Accounts in Scotland in respect of the financial year 1988-89 and subsequent financial years are to be met insofar as they are not met by grants paid by the Secretary of State under section 98(1)(a) of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973. They do not apply to any expenses incurred by the Commission as the designated body for Scotland in connection with the discharge of its functions under Part II of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1975.

items.title: The Licensing Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 22nd August 1988 and 1st March 1989 respectively the provisions of the Licensing Act 1988 set out in Schedules 1 and 2 to the Order. All other provisions of the Act not set out in those Schedules will come into force on 1st August 1988 (S.I. 1988/1187).

items.title: The Companies (Disclosure of Information) (Designated Authorities) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the Director General of Fair Trading and the Monopolies and Mergers Commission as authorities for the purposes of section 449 of the Companies Act 1985 in relation to their functions under the Fair Trading Act 1973, the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 and the Competition Act 1980. The effect of designation is to permit the disclosure of information which would otherwise be restricted if disclosure is for the purposes of enabling or assisting the designated authority to discharge the specified functions. There have been four previous orders designating public or other authorities for the purposes of Section 449 (S.I. 1986/2046, 1987/859/1141 and 1988/1058).

items.title: The London Government Reorganisation (Property) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals of the London Residuary Body, as modified by the Secretary of State, for the transfer of certain of the properties which it holds as successor in title to the Greater London Council. The effect of the modifications is that the transfers made by articles 2 and 3 are unconditional.

items.title: The Harbour Works (Assessment of Environmental Effects) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations implement for England and Wales Council Directive 85/337/EEC of the 27th June 1985 (OJ No. L175, 5.7.85, p.40) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment, in respect of the procedure for making harbour revision orders and harbour empowerment orders relating to harbour works to which the Directive applies.

items.title: The Licensing (Special Hours Certificates) (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Licensing (Special Hours Certificates) Rules 1982 to take account of amendments to the provisions of the Licensing Act 1964 concerning special hours certificates effected by the Licensing Act 1988, which in particular confer a discretion with respect to the grant of such certificates and make fresh provision for the attachment of limitations to them.

items.title: The Police Pensions (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1988, amend the Police Pensions Regulations 1987 (“the principal Regulations”) with effect from 27th February 1986 (retrospection is authorised by section 1(5) of the Police Pensions Act 1976).

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment No. 2) 1988
items.description: This version of the The Rules of the Supreme Court (Amendment No. 2) 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Administration of Justice Act 1985 (Commencement No. 5) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st October 1988 section 53 of the Administration of Justice Act 1985 (reimbursement of additional costs resulting from death or incapacity of presiding judge etc).

items.title: The Reimbursement of Costs (Monetary Limit) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes £8000 as the maximum amount which a party to proceedings may be reimbursed in respect of additional costs incurred as a result of the incapacity or death of a judge presiding at those proceedings.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Repayment Supplement) Regulations 1988
items.description: Under section 20 of the Finance Act 1985 (as substituted by section 20 of the Finance Act 1988), a person becomes entitled to a repayment supplement where the Commissioners have not authorised a payment or refund of tax owing to him within 30 days of their receiving the tax return or claim for a refund. These Regulations replace the Value Added Tax (Repayment Supplement) (No. 2) Regulations 1986, which did not deal with claims for refund of tax under section 20 of the Value Added Tax Act 1983 (refunds of tax for certain public bodies). Those Regulations are revoked. The new Regulations provide four specific sets of circumstances in which the 30 day period is to be treated as extended and they prescribe rules for determining the duration of such extensions. These rules are substantially the same as in the previous Regulations, but include claims for refunds. The rules are based on objective criteria to establish the dates on which the extensions start and end.

items.title: The Building Societies (Prescribed Contracts) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes swap contracts, sterling interest rate option contracts, swap option contracts and sterling interest rate forward and futures contracts as types of contract which a building society may enter into under section 23 of the Building Societies Act 1986 for the purpose of reducing the risk of loss.

items.title: The Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy Order 1988. The changes of substance made to that Order are as follows:—1section 32 of the Animal Health Act 1981 (power to slaughter animals) is applied to bovine spongiform encephalopathy (article 2(b));2where a veterinary inspector is of the opinion (following a veterinary enquiry) that a bovine animal is affected, or suspected of being affected, with bovine spongiform encephalopathy, he is required to serve a notice in Form A containing movement restrictions on the owner or person in charge of that animal (article 2(c)(i));3a notice in Form A will remain in force for a period of 28 days from the date specified in the notice unless renewed or withdrawn earlier by a notice in Form B (article 2(c)(ii)); and4a veterinary inspector is required to serve a notice of intended slaughter (in Form C) on the owner of any bovine animal which is affected, or suspected of being affected, with bovine spongiform encephalopathy and which the Minister proposes to cause to be slaughtered under the powers conferred on him by section 32 of the Act in its application to that disease (article 2(e)).

items.title: The Bovine Spongiform Encephalopathy Compensation Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes the amount of compensation payable for bovine animals slaughtered by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food under section 32 of the Animal Health Act 1981 because they are affected, or suspected of being affected, with bovine spongiform encephalopathy.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest Relief) (Housing Associations) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Income Tax (Interest Relief) (Housing Associations) Regulations 1983, as amended.

items.title: The Personal Equity Plan (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Personal Equity Plan Regulations 1986 and secure that losses in respect of plan investments are disregarded for the purposes of capital gains tax where they accrue on the disposal of investments on or after 18th January 1988.

items.title: The Textile Products (Determination of Composition) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Textile Products (Determination of Composition) Regulations 1976, which specified the test methods to be used to determine the composition of certain textile products. In order to implement Commission Directive 87/184/EEC (OJ No. L75, 17.3.87, p.21) a new edition of B.S. 4407 has been prepared and the Regulations refer to this edition.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which amend the Wireless Telegraphy (Licence Charges) Regulations 1986 (“the principal Regulations”), increase the fees payable on the issue and renewal of most wireless telegraphy licences granted under section 1 of the Wireless Telegraphy Act 1949.

items.title: The Textile Products (Indications of Fibre Content) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Textile Products (Indications of Fibre Content) Regulations 1986 (“the principal Regulations”) which require that when textile products are supplied or offered for supply commercially or are described in certain advertisements an indication of their fibre content shall be given.

items.title: The A47 Leicester—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Norwich Southern Bypass and Slip Roads) (Western Section) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A47 Leicester—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (Norwich Southern Bypass and Slip Roads) (Western Section) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Set-Aside Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations implement in part Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (OJ No. L93, 30.3.85, p.l) on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1760/87 (OJ No. L167, 26.6.87, p.1) and Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1094/88 (OJ No. L106, 27.4.88, p.28) as regards the set aside of arable land, and Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 1272/88 (OJ No. L121, 11.5.88, p.36), which laid down detailed rules for the set aside of arable land.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Amendment No.2 Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Order 1988 (“the principal Order”) contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the Ukraine, USSR.

items.title: The Finance Act 1988 (Repayment Supplement) (Appointed Day) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into effect section 20 of the Finance Act 1985 which was revised by section 20 of the Finance Act 1988 (“Repayment Supplement in respect of certain delayed payments or refunds”). It has two effects. It makes eligible for a repayment supplement certain qualifying returns relating to any prescribed accounting period ending on or after 29th July 1988.

items.title: The National Savings Stock Register (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Savings Stock Register Regulations 1976.

items.title: Premium Savings Bonds (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Premium Savings Bonds Regulations 1972.

items.title: The Savings Certificates (Yearly Plan) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Savings Certificates (Yearly Plan) Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Savings Contracts (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Savings Contracts Regulations 1969.

items.title: The Companies (Forms) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Companies (Forms) Regulations 1985 as amended by the Companies (Forms) (Amendment) Regulations 1986 and the Companies (Forms) (Amendment) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which replaces and revokes the Third Country Fishing (Enforcement) Order 1987, makes breaches of specified articles of the Community Regulations set out in column 1 of the Schedule to the Order offences for the purposes of United Kingdom law where they occur within British fishery limits.

items.title: The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on the 1st September 1988, supersede the Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1987 which are revoked (regulation 6(1)).

items.title: The Legal Aid Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Legal Aid Act 1988 relating to the Legal Aid Board. Section 3(1), (5), (6), (7), (8), (9) and (10), together with Schedule 1, paragraph 1, establish the Board as a body corporate and provide for the appointment of members. Schedule 1 makes further provision concerning membership and enables the Board to appoint staff and to regulate its own proceedings. Schedule 7, paragraphs 6, 7 and 8 provide for offers of employment to be made by the Board to certain employees of the Law Society.

items.title: The Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) Regulations 1978 relating to eligibility for appointment as chief officer of a brigade. The description of the minimum level of rank in which previous service is required is changed from Divisional Officer (Class 1) to Divisional Officer (Grade 1) and completion of the Senior Officers' Course or the Senior Command Course is made a qualification for such appointment as an alternative to the existing requirement of completion of the Brigade Command Course. The Regulations also provide for the eligibility (for further such appointment) of existing chief officers.

items.title: The Social Security (Contribution Conditions for Unemployment and Sickness Benefit) Transitional Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 18(4) of the Social Security Act 1988. They are consequential upon the coming into force of section 6 of that Act. Section 6 changes the contribution conditions for unemployment and sickness benefit. These Regulations contain savings for existing beneficiaries to both these benefits and for people receiving statutory sick pay.

items.title: The Dartford—Thurrock Crossing Tolls Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st September 1988, fixes the amounts of the tolls to be paid to use the Dartford—Thurrock Crossing, presently consisting of the Dartford Tunnels.

items.title: The Education (Welsh Medium Teacher Training Incentive Supplement) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations empower the Secretary of State to pay training supplements to persons undergoing training as teachers to facilitate and encourage their training to teach subjects in Welsh as well as in English (regulation 3).

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Appropriate Schemes) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations are all made under the Social Security Act 1986, and are made before the end of a period of 12 months from the commencement of the relevant sections of that Act. Consequently, by virtue of section 61(5) of that Act, the provisions of section 61(2) and (3) of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (as amended by section 86(1) of, and paragraph 94 of Schedule 10 to, the Social Security Act 1986), which require reference to the Occupational Pensions Board of, and a report by the Board on, proposals to make regulations for certain purposes of both Acts, do not apply to them.

items.title: The Cable Programme Services (Exceptions) Order 1988
items.description: This Order excepts from the requirement of a licence under section 4 of the Cable and Broadcasting Act 1984 the types of licensable cable programme service described in the Order. These are the simultaneous relaying of programmes broadcast by the BBC and the IBA; diffusion services in sound only whose signals are conveyed by a public telecommunications operator other than one licensed to provide a prescribed diffusion service; public presentations insofar as the signals are conveyed by such a person; and public presentations for which no charge is made and which do not include advertisements. The order also revokes the corresponding Order made under the Telecommunications Act 1984 (c. 12).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (England) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 6(3) of the Local Government Act 1988 enables the Secretary of State to require that an authority mentioned in section 1(1) of the Act (a defined authority) may only carry out functional work falling within an activity listed in section 2(2) of the Act (a defined activity) after subjecting the work to competition. These Regulations exclude the Inner London Education Authority, new town development corporations and the Commission for the New Towns from the competition requirements and specify the dates from which and the circumstances in which other defined authorities (“specified authorities”) in England may not carry out functional work without first exposing it to competition.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (England) Order 1988
items.description: Section 4 of the Local Government Act 1988 precludes an authority mentioned in section 1 of the Act (a defined authority) from entering into a works contract on or after 1st April 1989 under which they are to carry out work falling within an activity listed in section 2(2) of the Act (a defined activity) unless they have competed for the contract.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Specified Periods) (England) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations specify the minimum and maximum periods for which certain authorities may invite offers to carry out work in respect of activities which must be open to competition under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988.

items.title: The Teachers' Superannuation (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Teachers' Superannuation Regulations 1976 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The British Steel Act 1988 (Appointed Day) Order 1988
items.description: Section 1(1) of the British Steel Act 1988 provides that, on such day as the Secretary of State may by order appoint, all the property, rights and liabilities to which the British Steel Corporation was entitled or subject immediately before that day shall (subject to section 2(3) of the Act which extinguishes any liability of the Corporation to the Secretary of State in respect of the Corporation’s public dividend capital) become by virtue of section 1 of the Act property, rights and liabilities of a company nominated for the purposes of the section by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The British Steel Act 1988 (Nominated Company) Order 1988
items.description: Section 1(1) of the British Steel Act 1988 provides that, on such day as the Secretary of State may by order appoint, all the property, rights and liabilities to which the British Steel Corporation was entitled or subject immediately before that day shall (subject to section 2(3) of the Act which extinguishes any liability of the Corporation to the Secretary of State in respect of the Corporation’s public dividend capital) become by virtue of section 1 of the Act property, rights and liabilities of a company nominated for the purposes of the section by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Statutory Water Companies (Occupational Pension Schemes) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 17(2) of the Social Security Act 1986, as amended by the Social Security Act 1988, empowers the Secretary of State to make regulations modifying the effect of specified Acts in consequence of the provisions of Part I of the 1986 Act.

items.title: The Pesticides (Maximum Residue Levels in Food) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, made under Part III of the Food and Environment Protection Act 1985, specify the maximum levels of pesticide residues which may be left in food. The Regulations, inter alia, implement Council Directives 86/362/EEC and 86/363/EEC of 24th July 1986 on the fixing of maximum levels for pesticide residues in and on cereals, and in and on foodstuffs of animal Origin (viz. meat and milk and products derived therefrom) (O.J. No. L221, 7.8.86, pp.37 and 43 respectively).

items.title: The London Regional Transport (Levy) Order 1988
items.description: Section 13 of the London Regional Transport Act 1984 provides that the Secretary of State may, in respect of any year, make a levy on the rating authorities for all the rating areas in Greater London to recover a contribution from Greater London ratepayers towards his estimated expenditure in that year on grants to London Regional Transport, and for the purpose of raising the levy the Secretary of State may issue a demand for payments to the rating authorities. Such a demand shall require the rating authorities to levy as part, or as an additional item of the rate, a rate of an amount in the pound specified by Order in respect of the year to which the demand relates.

items.title: The Welsh Water Authority (Extension of Operation of Byelaws) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Welsh Water Authority (Extension of Operation of Byelaws) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Sheriffs' Fees (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the mileage fee payable to sheriffs on execution of writs of fieri facias, possession or delivery from 22.3 to 29.2 pence per mile and increases the fee payable to sheriffs for a copy of a return on a writ of execution from £1 to £5.

items.title: The Public Record Office (Fees) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Regulations replace the Public Record Office (Fees) Regulations 1987 and prescribe the full range of fees to be charged for authentication of copies of records and for other services provided by the Public Record Office.

items.title: The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (East London River Crossing (A13 to A2) Supplementary Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (East London River Crossing (A13 to A2) Supplementary Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (East London River Crossing (A13 to A2) Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (East London River Crossing (A13 to A2) Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Legal Aid Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Legal Aid Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) (Scotland) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Scotland) (Prospective Cost) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase from £50 to £60 the cost of legal advice and assistance (including assistance by way of representation) under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986 which a solicitor may grant without obtaining the prior approval of the Scottish Legal Aid Board.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Unitary Development Plans) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Local Government Act 1985 abolished the Greater London Council and metropolitan county councils. Schedule 1 to that Act provides for a new form of development plan under town and country planning legislation for Greater London and the metropolitan counties (the unitary development plan).

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Assistance by Way of Representation) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Amendment Regulations provide for assistance by way of representation to be available in relation to certain further summary criminal proceedings in Scotland and appeals to the Sheriff under the Mental Health (Scotland) Act 1984, for which criminal legal aid is not available in terms of the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986.

items.title: The Education (Fees and Awards) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 3 to the Education (Fees and Awards) Regulations 1983 which provide, subject to certain exceptions, that it is lawful to differentiate between students with and those without a specified connection with the United Kingdom, the Channel Islands and the Isle of Man or with a particular part of the United Kingdom either as respects fees charged or eligibility for awards.

items.title: The State Awards (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the State Awards Regulations 1978 relating to the bestowal of State Awards. Changes are made in the rules of eligibility for migrant workers of the European Community and their children contained in Schedule 2. The main effect of the changes is that a European Community migrant worker will no longer have to have been in employment in the United Kingdom for a period of nine months nor be seeking an award in respect of a course provided by a vocational training establishment to be eligible for an award. Instead they will be eligible for an award where they are so eligible by virtue of Article 7(2) or (3) or Article 12 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1612/68 on freedom of movement for workers within the Community and where they satisfy a test of a minimum period of ordinary residence in the Community or (prior to their accession) Spain and Portugal.

items.title: The Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Building Societies (Designation of Qualifying Bodies) Order 1988, which designates corporate bodies and types of corporate body as suitable for investment or support by building societies under section 18 of the Building Societies Act 1986. The main amendments are contained in paragraphs 1 and 2 of the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Building Societies (Guernsey and Alderney) Order 1988
items.description: This Order empowers building societies with commercial assets of at least £100 million to make advances on the security of land in Guernsey and Alderney, and provides for the classification of those advances within the asset classification provisions of the Building Societies Act 1986. By virtue of article 5 of this Order, the power to make such an advance does not require adoption by a special resolution of a society in order to be exercisable by it.

items.title: The Royal Marines Terms of Service Regulations 1988
items.description: (This is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Health and Safety: General Duties) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the penalties in the Merchant Shipping (Health and Safety: General Duties) Regulations 1984. They provide that offences by an employer of contravening his general duty to ensure, so far as practicable, the health and safety of his employees may also be tried on indictment and punished by a fine or up to two years imprisonment (as an alternative to being triable summarily).

items.title: The Education (Bursaries for Teacher Training) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Education (Bursaries for Teacher Training) Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/1324, amended by S.I. 1987/499 and 1393). They empower the Secretary of State to pay training bursaries to persons undergoing initial training as teachers (regulation 3). To be eligible for a bursary, a person must not previously have completed a course of initial teacher training or be under a contract of employment with a local education authority and further the person must have been accepted for the course and must satisfy the Secretary of State of his commitment to teaching in a secondary or middle school in England or Wales which is either maintained by a local education authority or grant-maintained (regulation 4). Bursaries are tenable on courses of initial teacher training provided by an establishment in England and Wales named by the Secretary of State when granting the training bursary, which begins on or after the 1st September 1986 and is designed to prepare persons to teach pupils either aged 11 to 16 or of all ages in secondary schools and of which the principal subject matter is mathematics, physics, technology, physics and technology or craft, design and technology and is of up to two year’s duration. A bursary is for the normal duration of the course in respect of which it is granted but, in certain circumstances, the period may be extended or the payment of the bursary may be suspended or terminated (regulation 6). The value of the bursary is £1,300 per annum for a full-time course or a proportion of that amount ascertained in accordance with the regulations in respect of a part-time course. In addition in the case of those attending a course the subject matter of which is craft, design and technology an equipment allowance of £200 is payable (regulation 7). Training bursaries are repayable on request to the Secretary of State where he is satisfied that the holder has been under a contract of employment with a local education authority for the whole or a substantial part of the period of training (regulation 8).

items.title: The Farm Diversification Grant (Variation) Scheme 1988
items.description: This Scheme varies the Farm Diversification Grant Scheme 1987 by providing that the Minister will not approve farm diversification plans containing proposals for the carrying out of certain land improvement works on land which has been withdrawn from agricultural production under the Set-Aside Regulations 1988 (regulation 3).

items.title: The Black Country Development Corporation (Planning Functions) (Wolverhampton) Order 1988
items.description: The Wolverhampton Urban Development Area Order 1988 designates the Wolverhampton urban development area and provides for the functions of an urban development corporation for the area to be performed by the Black Country Development Corporation.

items.title: The Amusements with Prizes (Variation of Fees) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases in England and Wales the fee payable to a local authority or other appropriate authority on the grant or renewal of a permit for amusements with prizes provided under section 16 of the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976.

items.title: The Unitary Development Plans (West Midlands) (Appointed Day) Order 1988
items.description: The Local Government Act 1985 abolished the Greater London Council and metropolitan county councils. The Act consequently provides for the replacement in their areas of the existing structure and local development plans under town and country planning legislation by“unitary development plans”. This Order brings the provisions of the 1985 Act relating to unitary development plans into force in the county of the West Midlands other than the metropolitan district of Dudley on 29th February 1988.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Wolverhampton Urban Development Area) Special Development Order 1988
items.description: This Order is a special development order applying to land within the Wolverhampton urban development area. The functions of an urban development corporation for that area are performed by the Black Country Development Corporation by virtue of the Wolverhampton Urban Development Area Order 1988.

items.title: The British Shipbuilders Borrowing Powers (Increase of Limit) Order 1988
items.description: Section 11(7) of the Aircraft and Shipbuilding Industries Act 1977, as last amended by section 1(1) of the British Shipbuilders (Borrowing Powers) Act 1987, limits the aggregate of the borrowings by British Shipbuilders and its wholly owned subsidiaries (other than money borrowed on excluded loans as defined by section 11(9) of the 1977 Act) and the public dividend capital received by British Shipbuilders to £1550 million. Section 11(7), as so amended, also makes provision for that limit to be increased by order to a sum not exceeding £1800 million, with no single Order increasing the limit by more than £150 million. This Order increases the limit from £1550 million to £1700 million.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st October 1988 sections 123, 125, 126, 127 and 128 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 and associated amendments and repeals. These provisions introduce a new sentence of detention in a young offender institution, to replace the youth custody sentence and detention centre order, and amend certain provisions of the Children and Young Persons Act 1933 and the Children and Young Persons Act 1969 in relation to young offenders. The Order does not affect Scotland.

items.title: The Social Security (Employment Training: Payments) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, made under section 26 of the Employment Act 1988 (c. 19), modifiesPart I of the Social Security Act 1975 (c. 14), which makes provision as to National Insurance contributions.

items.title: The A45 Felixstowe-Weedon Trunk Road (Kirton Road Roundabout Trimley St Martin) Detrunking Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A45 Felixstowe-Weedon Trunk Road (Kirton Road Roundabout Trimley St Martin) Detrunking Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Peterborough Development Corporation (Transfer of Property and Dissolution) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for the transfer of all the property of the Peterborough Development Corporation to the Commission for the New Towns on 1st October 1988 (article 2(a)), the winding up of the affairs of the Corporation (articles 2(b), 3 and 4) and its subsequent dissolution on 31st December 1988 (article 6). The Order also provides for section 11 of the New Towns Act 1981 (which enables the Secretary of State and highways authorities to acquire land for highways) and related provisions of that Act to continue to have effect in relation to the new town of Peterborough for a period of 10 years (article 5).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 6(3) of the Local Government Act 1988 enables the Secretary of State to require that an authority specified in section 1(1) of the Act (a defined authority) may only carry out functional work falling within an activity listed in section 2(2) of the Act (a defined activity) after subjecting the work to competition.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Specified Periods) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations specify the minimum and maximum periods for which certain authorities may invite offers to carry out work in respect of activities which must be open to competition under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: Section 4 of the Local Government Act 1988 precludes an authority mentioned in section 1 of the Act (a defined authority) from entering into a works contract on or after 1st April 1989 under which they are to carry out work falling within an activity listed in section 2(2) of the Act (a defined activity) unless they have competed for the contract. Section 5 of the Act imposes a similar prohibition in respect of works contracts entered into before 1st April 1989 under which work is to be carried out on or after that date.

items.title: The Gaming (Records of Cheques) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, require the holder of a licence under the Gaming Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”) in respect of any premises, except bingo premises, to make and retain for the period specified in regulation 3(2) the records required by regulations 4, 5 and 6 of these Regulations.

items.title: The Judicial Pensions (Personal Pension Option) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, amend the Judicial Pensions Act 1981 to allow members of the judicial pension schemes to elect to have personal pensions instead of acquiring pension rights under the 1981 Act. These Regulations enable the judicial pension schemes to comply with the provisions contained in the Social Security Act 1986 and the Social Security (Northern Ireland) Order 1986, which enable members of occupational pension schemes to exercise personal pension options. The modifications to the 1981 Act in the Schedule set out how a member of a judicial pension scheme may elect for a personal pension and the effect of such an election.

items.title: The Judicial Pensions (Preservation of Benefits) Order 1988
items.description: This Order supersedes the Judicial Pensions (Preservation of Benefits) Order 1987, (which consolidated existing Orders modifying pension schemes applicable to holders of the judicial offices listed in schedule 1). This Order provides for a reduced pension and derivative benefits (lump sum and widows' and children’s pensions) to be preserved, and to become payable, in the case of an office-holder who ceases to hold office before reaching normal pension age.

items.title: The Judicial Pensions (Preserved Benefits and Personal Pension Option) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that any period during which an election made by a member of a judicial pension scheme under the Judicial Pensions (Personal Pension Option) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1417) is in force, shall not count as relevant service in connection with the preservation of benefits under the Judicial Pensions (Preservation of Benefits) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1418). A member may elect under those Regulations to have a personal pension instead of acquiring pension rights under the Judicial Pensions Act 1981.

items.title: The Judicial Pensions (Requisite Benefits) Order 1988
items.description: This Order supersedes the Judicial Pensions (Requisite Benefits) Order 1987 which consolidated existing Orders which modified the schemes relating to certain judicial pensions so as to make the changes required in order to meet the contracting-out requirements laid down by the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (“the 1975 Act”).

items.title: The Prison (Amendment) (No. 3) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison Rules 1964, as amended, so that, where appropriate, they apply to offenders who are serving a sentence of detention in a young offender institution under Part I of the Criminal Justice Act 1982, as amended by section 123 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (which comes into force on 1st October 1988 (S.I.1988/1408)) and are detained in prison in accordance with the provisions of section 1C of the 1982 Act.

items.title: The Young Offender Institution Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules make provision for the management of young offender institutions provided under section 43 of the Prison Act 1952 as amended by section 11 of the Criminal Justice Act 1982 and Schedules 8 and 15 to the Criminal Justice Act 1988 which comes into force on 1st October 1988 (S.I. 1988/1408). They replace the Detention Centre Rules 1983 (S.I. 1983/569) and the Youth Custody Rules 1983 (S.I. 1983/570), as amended. They include provision for the treatment, occupation, discipline and control of inmates who may be detained therein, the conduct of officers of young offender institutions and the constitution, powers and duties of boards of visitors.

items.title: The Education Authority Bursaries (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education Authority Bursaries (Scotland) Regulations 1988 to alter provisions relating to the eligibility of nationals of member states of the European Community. They remove conditions which provided for a minimum period of employment in the United Kingdom. Nationals of other member states of the European Community will be eligible for bursaries where they fall to be treated as workers for the purposes of Article 7 of the Council Regulation 1612/68 on freedom of movement for workers within the Community and are therefore entitled to equality of treatment with United Kingdom nationals, provided that other conditions as to ordinary residence in the European Community are satisfied and provided that they are appropriately connected with the area of the education authority concerned. Children of such nationals of other member states will similarly be eligible by virtue of Article 12 of Council Regulation 1612/68 where their parent is or has been a worker in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Students' Allowances (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Students' Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 1987 to alter provisions relating to the eligibility of nationals of member states of the European Community. They remove conditions which provided for a minimum period of employment in the United Kingdom. Nationals of other member states of the European Community will be eligible for allowances where they fall to be treated as workers for the purposes of Article 7 of Council Regulation 1612/68 on freedom of movement for workers within the community and are therefore entitled to equality of treatment with United Kingdom Nationals, provided that other conditions as to ordinary residence in the European Community are satisfied. Children of such nationals of other member states will similarly be eligible by virtue of Article 12 of Council Regulation 1612/68 where their parent is or has been a worker in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The National Health Service (Payments for Optical Appliances) (Scotland) Amendment (No.3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Payments for Optical Appliances) (Scotland) Regulations 1986 which provide for payments to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of the cost incurred by certain categories of person for the supply of optical appliances supplied following a sight test under the National Health Service in Scotland.

items.title: The Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (England and Wales) Order 1988
items.description: This Order which revokes and replaces the Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (England and Wales) Order 1987 prescribes fees in respect of the services listed in Schedules 1 and 2 to the Order (articles 4 to 8). These services are performed in connection with applications received for the issue of phytosanitary certificates or licences in accordance with the provisions of the Plant Health (Great Britain) Order 1987. The certificates are required to accompany exports of certain plants, plant products etc and licences are issued to enable the importation of those articles which would otherwise be prohibited.

items.title: The Agricultural Holdings (Units of Production) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes units of production for the assessment of the productive capacity of agricultural land and sets out the amount which is to be regarded as the net annual income from each such unit for the year 12th September 1988 to 11th September 1989 inclusive. In so far as the units of production prescribed in the Order are relevant to the assessment they are to be used, in conjunction with the net annual income ascribed to them, in calculating the net annual income of the unit of land when assessing whether it is a commercial unit of agricultural land for the purposes of the succession provisions in Part IV of the Agricultural Holdings Act 1986, that is to say a unit of agricultural land which is capable, when farmed under competent management, of producing a net annual income not less than the aggregate of the average annual earnings of two full-time, male agricultural workers aged 20 or over, (see paragraph 3(1) of Schedule 6 to the Act). Therefore the assessment will not necessarily be based on the system of farming carried out on the holding or on historical data from the holding.

items.title: The National Health Service (Payments for Optical Appliances) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Payments for Optical Appliances) Regulations 1986 which provide for payments to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of the cost incurred by certain categories of persons for the supply of optical appliances supplied following a sight test under the National Health Service in England and Wales.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide for the disapplication and modification of provisions contained in Schedule 23 to the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the Schedule”) (occupational pension schemes approved before 23rd July 1987) in the circumstances prescribed in them; authorise the Commissioners of Inland Revenue (“the Board”) to direct that the Schedule or any provision of it shall not apply in any particular case where in the opinion of the Board the facts are such that its application would not be appropriate; and provide that the Regulations shall take effect as from 17th March 1987 and that any direction given under them shall take effect as from that date or any later date.

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Provisional Approval) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 2nd September 1988, amend the Personal Pension Schemes (Provisional Approval) Regulations 1987 (“the Principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Family Credit (General) Amendment No. 3 Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987. They provide for work to be treated as remunerative work only if the person is employed at the date of claim (regulation 2); for the disregard of any period in the assessment period of self-employed earners to be not less than complete weeks (regulation 3); and for the weekly earnings of such earners to be determined by dividing earnings over the assessment period by the number of weeks in that period (regulation 4). They also provide for members of the family to be treated as possessing income or capital used for ordinary clothing or footwear (regulation 5); for an increase in the student disregard for books or equipment (regulation 7); for the disregard of payments made by the Secretary of State to compensate for the loss of housing benefit, any payment in kind made by a charity, and payments to persons participating in training (regulations 8 and 9(c)); and for certain other minor amendments (regulations 6 and 9(a) and (b)).

items.title: The Social Security (Credits) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Credits) Regulations 1975 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The A12 Trunk Road (Leyton Way, Waltham Forest) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A12 Trunk Road (Leyton Way, Waltham Forest) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) Amendment No. 3 Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 to provide, in the calculation of income and capital, for the disregard of payments made to the incapacitated and very severely disabled out of the Independent Living Fund (regulations 2 and 4); in the calculation of a student’s grant income, for an increase in the disregard for books and equipment (regulation 6); and, in the calculation of capital, for the disregard of a claimant’s interest in his former dwelling to include any garage, garden or other outbuildings (regulation 8).

items.title: The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 4 Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 4 Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 5th September 1988, amend the definition of “training allowance” in the Social Security (Overlapping Benefits) Regulations 1979 so as to exclude from its scope any training premium or training bonus payable by or on behalf of the Secretary of State to any person undertaking training where the facilities used are provided in pursuance of arrangements made under section 2 of the Employment and Training Act 1973.

items.title: The Central Institutions (Recognition) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations recognise certain educational establishments for the provision of further education as central institutions and revoke the Central Institutions (Recognition) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 1985.

items.title: The Dundee College of Technology (Change of Name) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations re-name Dundee College of Technology as “Dundee Institute of Technology” and make consequential amendments to the Dundee College of Technology Scheme 1975.

items.title: The Napier College of Commerce and Technology (Change of Name) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations re-name Napier College of Commerce and Technology as “Napier Polytechnic of Edinburgh” and make consequential amendments to the Napier College of Commerce and Technology (No. 2) Regulations 1985.

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Order 1978 by adding to the list of approved disinfectants in that Order those newly approved disinfectants which are mentioned in Schedule 1 to this Order (article 2).

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms upon which doctors provide general medical services under the National Health Service in Scotland.

items.title: The Local Government Finance Act 1988 Commencement (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order appoints the days on which the provisions of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the Act”) which extend only to Scotland are to come into force.

items.title: The Gipsy Encampments (City of Durham) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the City of Durham as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Education Reform Act 1988 relating to the admission of pupils to county and voluntary schools.

items.title: The (A167) London—Edinburgh—Thurso Trunk Road (Former Darlington County Borough Boundary to Coatham Mundeville (Aycliffe) Interchange De-Trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A167) London—Edinburgh—Thurso Trunk Road (Former Darlington County Borough Boundary to Coatham Mundeville (Aycliffe) Interchange De-Trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Control of Industrial Major Accident Hazards (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Control of Industrial Major Accident Hazards Regulations 1984 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations implemented with respect to Great Britain Council Directive 82/501/EEC (OJ No. L230, 5.8.82, p.l) “on the major-accident hazards of certain industrial activities”. These Regulations implement with respect to Great Britain the provisions of Council Directive 87/216/EEC (OJ No. L85, 28.3.87, p.36), which amends that Directive.

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Clothing and Allied Products) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 19th September 1988 gives effect to proposals of the Clothing and Allied Products Industry Training Board which were submitted to and approved by the Training Commission and thereafter submitted by the Training Commission to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the Clothing and Allied Products industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Liquor Licensing (Fees) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the fees payable from 1st January 1989 under the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976 by an applicant to a licensing board in respect of the matters listed in the Schedule to the Order. The fees are increased by 33 1/3 per cent.

items.title: The East of Birmingham — Birkenhead Trunk Road (A41 Handley Bypass) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The East of Birmingham — Birkenhead Trunk Road (A41 Handley Bypass) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Grants by Local Housing Authorities (Eligible Expense Limits) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: Grants for the improvement, repair or conversion of a dwelling or a house in multiple occupation are made by local housing authorities under Part XV of the Housing Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”). The amount, or in some cases the maximum amount, of a grant is the appropriate percentage of the estimated expense which is eligible to be taken into account.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (Wales) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 6(3) of the Local Government Act 1988 enables the Secretary of State to require that an authority mentioned in section 1(1) of the Act (a defined authority) may carry out functional work falling within an activity listed in section 2(2) and described in schedule 1 of the Act (a defined activity), only after subjecting the work to competition. These Regulations specify the dates from which and the circumstances in which those bodies which are defined authorities (“specified authorities”) in Wales may not carry out functional work without first exposing it to competition.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemptions) (Wales) Order 1988
items.description: Section 4 of the Local Government Act 1988 precludes an authority mentioned in section 1 of the Act (a defined authority) from entering into a works contract (defined in section 3(2) and (3)) on or after 1 April 1989 under which they are to carry out work falling within an activity listed in section 2(2) of the Act (a defined activity) unless they have competed for the contract. Section 5 of the Act frustrates the further performance on or after 1 April 1989 of works contracts entered into before that date unless the contract was competed for.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Specified Periods) (Wales) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations specify the minimum and maximum periods for which certain authorities may invite offers to carry out work in respect of activities which must be open to competition under Part 1 of the Local Government Act 1988.

items.title: The Education (Parental Ballots for Acquisition of Grant-maintained Status) (Prescribed Body) Regulations 1988
items.description: Under section 61 of the Education Reform Act 1988 a ballot of parents of registered pupils at a school is required to be held as part of the procedure for applying for grant-maintained status. By virtue of subsection (1) of that section such a ballot must be held by a body prescribed in regulations made by the Secretary of State. These Regulations prescribe the Electoral Reform Society for the purposes of that subsection.

items.title: The Grants by Local Housing Authorities (Appropriate Percentage and Exchequer Contributions) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: Grants for the improvement, repair or conversion of a dwelling or of a house in multiple occupation are made under Part XV of the Housing Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”). The amount, or in some cases the maximum amount, of the grant is the appropriate percentage of the estimated expense which is eligible to be taken into account. Section 509 of the 1985 Act provides for this percentage to be prescribed by Order made by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Customs and Excise (Common Transit) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide a penalty and a sanction of forfeiture for breaches of certain provisions of the Convention on a Common Transit Procedure and for diversion of goods carried under that procedure from any route specified for their movement by the Commissioners of Customs and Excise. The penalty proceedings and forfeiture procedure are governed by the Customs and Excise Management Act 1979.

items.title: The Abolition of Domestic Rates (Domestic and Part Residential Subjects) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 15th September 1988, prescribe certain matters which fall to be prescribed in relation to Part I of the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the Act”), as amended by the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”).

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Plating and Testing) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations re-enact the Goods Vehicles (Plating and Testing) Regulations 1982 with amendments. In addition to minor and drafting amendments, they make the following changes of substance.

items.title: The Berry Head and Berry Head (Southern Redoubt) (Areas of Special Protection) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Berry Head and Berry Head (Southern Redoubt) (Areas of Special Protection) Order 1984 by establishing a part of the coastal waters lying off Berry Head and the Southern Redoubt as an area of special protection for wild birds under section 3 of the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981.

items.title: The Whitehaven (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order establishes, under section 22 of the Petroleum Act 1987, safety zones, having a radius of 500 metres from a specified point, around the installations specified in the Schedule to the Order and stationed in waters to which section 21(7) of that Act applies (these include territorial waters and waters in areas designated under section 1(7) of the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29)).

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Offshore Installations (Safety Zones) (No. 48) Order 1987 by deleting the references relating to two installations which will accordingly cease to be protected by safety zones established by the 1987 Order.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Social Security Act 1986 Modifications) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations modify the provisions relating to housing benefit in the Social Security Act 1986 so as to provide for the making by levying authorities in Scotland of community charge rebates, in respect of a person’s liability for personal community charges or collective community charge contributions after the abolition of domestic rates in Scotland on 1st April 1989. The Act as modified specifies the conditions governing entitlement to such rebates and how such rebates are to be calculated. The modifications also provide for the payment of subsidies to levying authorities in respect of their expenses for the community charge rebates.

items.title: The Food (Meat Inspection) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, revoke and re-enact with amendments the provisions of the Food (Meat Inspection) (Scotland) Regulations 1961. They apply to fresh meat of domestic bovine animals (including buffalo), swine, sheep, goats, solipeds and farmed deer. They prescribe conditions which must be satisfied for the production of meat intended for sale for human consumption on the domestic market.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Fees) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Merchant Shipping (Fees) Regulations 1987, as amended.

items.title: The International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (1988 General Capital Increase) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for the payment to the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development, in accordance with arrangements made with it relating to the 1988 General Capital Increase of the resources of the Bank, of an additional subscription equivalent to US$3,673,335,750. The Order also provides for the redemption of non-interest-bearing and non-negotiable notes issued by the Secretary of State in payment of the additional subscription. The Order further provides that certain sums which may be received by the Government of the United Kingdom from the Bank shall be paid into the Consolidated Fund.

items.title: The Export of Goods (Control) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Export of Goods (Control) Order 1987.

items.title: The Dock Workers (Regulation of Employment) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Dock Workers (Regulation of Employment) Scheme 1947, as varied in 1960, 1961, 1967, and 1980. The 1947 Scheme as varied is set out in Schedule 2 to the Dock Workers (Regulation of Employment) (Amendment) Scheme 1967 and is now known as the Dock Workers Employment Scheme 1967 (“the 1967 Scheme”). Paragraph (3) of Clause 1 of the 1967 Scheme was amended by paragraph 3 of the Schedule to the Dock Workers (Regulation of Employment) (Amendment) Order 1980.

items.title: The Mersey Docks and Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends the time for the completion of certain works of reclamation of the river bed at Tranmere authorised by section 3 of the Mersey Docks and Harbour Board Act 1901 (as extended).

items.title: The Fishguard (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purposes of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the Authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for Pilotage have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Penzance and Newlyn (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Teignmouth (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Shoreham (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Port of Ramsgate (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Gaming Act (Variation of Fees) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases in England and Wales the fee to be charged under the Gaming Act 1968 for the grant or renewal of a permit under section 34 of that Act for premises where gaming machines are used.

items.title: The St Ives (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Police (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Police (Scotland) Regulations 1976 (“the principal Regulations”) and come into force on 27th September 1988.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1978 as amended by the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1986 by increasing the fees payable to Sheriff Officers by about 12%.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1978 as amended by the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1986 by increasing the fees payable to Messengers-at-Arms by about 12%.

items.title: The A47 Trunk Road (Castor and Ailsworth Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A47 Trunk Road (Castor and Ailsworth Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A47 Trunk Road (Detrunking at Castor and Ailsworth) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A47 Trunk Road (Detrunking at Castor and Ailsworth) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A1400 Trunk Road (Woodford Avenue Service Road, Redbridge) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A1400 Trunk Road (Woodford Avenue Service Road, Redbridge) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Publication of Proposals for Reduction in Standard Number) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the manner in which proposals by a local education authority or the governing body of a county or voluntary secondary school for a reduction in any standard number applying to the school under section 27 of the Education Reform Act 1988 are to be published before application is made to the Secretary of State for an order bringing about such a reduction.

items.title: The West Strathclyde Protection Order 1988
items.description: This Order prohibits the fishing for or taking of freshwater fish in inland waters in the area prescribed, without legal right or written permission from someone having that right.

items.title: The Irvine (Pilotage Powers) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that the Irvine Harbour Company is to be a competent harbour authority for the purposes of the Pilotage Act 1987 and the Order defines the area of its harbour for these purposes.

items.title: The Fowey Harbour, Par and Charlestown (Application of Pilotage Act 1987) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that the Pilotage Act 1987 shall apply to the Fowey Harbour Commissioners as if their harbour included waters at Par and Charlestown.

items.title: The Portsmouth Mile End Quay Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Portsmouth Mile End Quay Harbour Revision Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Ports (Finance) Act 1985 (Increase of Grants Limit) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Ports (Finance) Act 1985 (Increase of Grants Limit) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Specified Periods) (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations rectify errors in the drafting of the Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Specified Periods) (Wales) Regulations 1988. Part II of the Schedule to those regulations sets out the minimum and maximum periods for which certain authorities without education functions may invite offers to carry out work in respect of activities which must be open to competition under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988. These Regulations substitute a new Part II of the Schedule to the principal regulations, the effect of which is to amend the maximum periods in respect of the activities of building cleaning and ground maintenance from 4 years to 5 years in each case.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.4) (Commercial Actions) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session by introducing new rules for commercial actions.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Great Britain) Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval for Goods Vehicles) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval for Goods Vehicles) (Great Britain) Regulations 1982.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 6) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (Popes Lane (B4491) to Western Avenue (A40) Improvement, Trunk Road) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (Popes Lane (B4491) to Western Avenue (A40) Improvement, Trunk Road) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (Popes Lane (B4491) to Western Avenue (A40) Improvement, Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A406 London North Circular Trunk Road (Popes Lane (B4491) to Western Avenue (A40) Improvement, Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Foreign Compensation Commission (People’s Republic of China) Rules Approval Instrument 1988
items.description: By this Instrument the Lord Chancellor approves rules made by the Foreign Compensation Commission to govern the procedure to be followed by the Commission in the disposal of applications under the Foreign Compensation (People’s Republic of China) Order 1987. They are substantially based on those made for the purposes of previous Orders providing for the determination of claims and the distribution of compensation by the Commission.

items.title: The Local Government (Prescribed Expenditure) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 72(3) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 ( “the Act”) limits the prescribed expenditure of a local authority, and other bodies to whom Part VIII of the Act applies, to the aggregate of various amounts including its net capital receipts. “Prescribed expenditure” is expenditure, essentially of a capital nature, described in Schedule 12 to the Act.

items.title: The A3 Trunk Road (Roehampton Vale, Wandsworth) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A3 Trunk Road (Roehampton Vale, Wandsworth) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Community Water Charges (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: Paragraph 11 of Schedule 5 to the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987 (as amended by paragraph 38 of Schedule 12 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988) provides for the provisions of Part II of, and Schedules 1A and 2 to, that Act (as amended by section 129 of, and Schedules 12 and 13 to, the 1988 Act) to have effect, subject to paragraphs 7 and 8 of Schedule 5 (liability to pay community water charges) and to such adaptations, exceptions and modifications as may be prescribed, in relation to the community water charges as they have effect in relation to the corresponding community charges.

items.title: The Community Charges (Registration) (Scotland) (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe matters relating to the Community Charges Register (“the register”) which is to be maintained under the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Standard and Collective Community Charges (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Standard and Collective Community Charges (Scotland) Regulations 1988 by prescribing an additional class of premises in respect of which the standard community charge is not payable.

items.title: The Personal Community Charge (Exemption for the Severely Mentally Impaired) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: Schedule 1A to the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the 1987 Act”), as inserted by paragraph 35 of Schedule 12 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, describes the persons who are exempt from liability to pay the personal community charge.

items.title: The London Government Reorganisation (Staff Compensation) Order 1988
items.description: The Greater London Council had power, under section 75 of the London Government Act 1963, to pay compensation to its officers or their dependants in respect of injuries sustained at work. This Order enables the London Residuary Body to make payments in respect of similar claims made before 5th October 1988, to the extent that it is not already able to do so under existing powers. In exercising the new power the Residuary Body is required to apply the same principles as were applied by the abolished council when considering claims under section 75.

items.title: The Social Security (Credits) Amendment (No. 4) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Credits) Regulations 1975 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Public Health (Infectious Diseases) Regulations 1988 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the provisions of the Public Health (Infectious Diseases) Regulations 1968.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Medical Stores) (Fishing Vessels) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Merchant Shipping (Medical Scales) (Fishing Vessels) Regulations 1974. The Regulations apply to all fishing vessels registered in the UK and to unregistered fishing vessels owned by persons resident in or having a principal place of business in the United Kingdom. Every fishing vessel going to sea is required to carry medicines and other medical stores appropriate to the type and length of voyage and the geographical area into which it is proposed to proceed (Regulation 3).

items.title: The (A16) Norman Cross—Grimsby Trunk Road (Louth Bypass) Order 1988
