items.description: This version of the The (A16) Norman Cross—Grimsby Trunk Road (Louth Bypass) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The (A16) Norman Cross—Grimsby Trunk Road (Louth Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A16) Norman Cross—Grimsby Trunk Road (Louth Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Public Health (Notification of Infectious Diseases) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Public Health (Infectious Diseases) (Scotland) Regulations 1975 (“the 1975 Regulations”) relating to the notification and prevention of infectious disease.

items.title: The Leeds Development Corporation (Planning Functions) Order 1988
items.description: The Leeds Development Corporation was established by the Leeds Development Corporation (Area and Constitution) Order 1988 for the purpose of regenerating the Leeds urban development area. This Order makes the corporation the local planning authority for that area for all the purposes of Part III (general planning control) of the Town and Country Planning Act 1971 (article 3(a)), and for those purposes which are specified in Part I of Schedule 29 to the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 which deal with additional control in special cases, enforcement of control, listed buildings and conservation areas (article 3(b)).

items.title: The Central Manchester Development Corporation (Planning Functions) Order 1988
items.description: The Central Manchester Development Corporation was established by the Central Manchester Development Corporation (Area and Constitution) Order 1988 for the purpose of regenerating the Central Manchester urban development area. This Order makes the corporation the local planning authority for that area for all the purposes of Part III (general planning control) of the Town and Country Planning Act 1971 (article 3(a)), and for those purposes which are specified in Part I of Schedule 29 to the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 which deal with additional control in special cases, enforcement of control, listed buildings and conservation areas (article 3(b)).

items.title: The Sheffield Development Corporation (Planning Functions) Order 1988
items.description: The Sheffield Develoment Corporation was established by the Sheffield Development Corporation (Area and Constitution) Order 1988 for the purpose of regenerating the Sheffield urban development area. This Order makes the corporation the local planning authority for that area for all the purposes of Part III (general planning control) of the Town and Country Planning Act 1971 (article 3(a)), and for those purposes which are specified in Part I of Schedule 29 to the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 which deal with additional control in special cases, enforcement of control, listed buildings and conservation areas (article 3(b)).

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 20) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which are associated with the Claymore, Scapa and Piper oilfields, situated east of the Orkney Islands.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 21) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of that part of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule which lies in controlled waters, that is, the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29). The pipe-line lies between the Beatrice oil field, which is situated east of the Moray Firth, and Britoil’s Shandwick Terminal at Nigg Bay in the Highland Region of Scotland.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 22) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the two companies specified in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Parts II and III of the Schedule. In the case of the pipe-lines specified in Part II, the designation applies only to those parts which are in controlled waters, that is the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29).

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 23) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie above and between two offshore gasfields situated east of the coast of Lincolnshire.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 24) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie above the Claymore oilfield, situated east of the Orkney Islands.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 25) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-lines lie between installations situated above the Forties oilfield, east of the Grampian Region of Scotland.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 26) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies specified in Part I of the Schedule as owners of those parts of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule which lie in controlled waters, that is, the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29). The pipe-line lies between the Brent oilfield and St Fergus, near Peterhead, in the Grampian Region of Scotland.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 27) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates owners of the pipe-line which lies between the Forties Charlie platform, an offshore installation situated above the Forties oilfield east of the coast of Scotland, and the terminal at Cruden Bay in the Grampian Region of Scotland. The companies listed in Part I of the Schedule are designated as owners of apparatus and works which are both part of the pipe-line and are attached to the platform. BP Petroleum Development Ltd is designated as the owner of those parts of the remainder of the pipe-line which are in controlled waters, that is the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29).

items.title: The Transfrontier Shipment of Hazardous Waste Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England, Wales and Scotland, implement Council Directive No. 84/631/EEC, as amended and adapted to technical progress, on the supervision and control within the European Community of the transfrontier shipment of hazardous waste. Such waste is, with certain exceptions, “special waste” as defined in the Control of Pollution (Special Waste) Regulations 1980. The Regulations provide a system of consignment notes and uniform documents to be used by persons who produce, transport and dispose of consignments of hazardous waste.

items.title: The Block Grant (Education Adjustments) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Block Grant (Education Adjustments) (England) Regulations 1987 which provide for the adjustment of block grant payable to local authorities in England in respect of the financial year 1987/8 and subsequent years.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1987 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 31st October 1988 sections 4 to 11, and, so far as they are not already in force, section 15 of and Schedule 2 to the Criminal Justice Act 1987. Sections 4 to 11 provide for the transfer of criminal proceedings involving serious or complex fraud from the magistrates' court to the Crown Court. Section 15 and Schedule 2 make related consequential amendments. The other provisions of the Criminal Justice Act 1987 are already in force by virtue of earlier commencement orders (S.I. 1987/1061, 1988/397).

items.title: The Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout the United Kingdom and come into force on 30 September 1988, consolidate with amendments the Agricultural or Forestry Tractors and Tractor Components (Type Approval) Regulations 1979 and its amending Regulations. Those amending Regulations are listed in Schedule 3 to these Regulations.

items.title: The Community Charges (Registration) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe matters relating to the Community Charges Register (“the register”) which is to be maintained under the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the Act”).

items.title: The United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting (Electoral Scheme) (Variation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect to the electoral scheme variation scheme prepared by the United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting which varies the electoral scheme given effect by the United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting (Electoral Scheme) Order 1982.

items.title: The London—Fishguard Trunk Road (A48) (Cross Hands Industrial Site Interchange, Slip Roads Trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London—Fishguard Trunk Road (A48) (Cross Hands Industrial Site Interchange, Slip Roads Trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Electro-medical Equipment (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive No. 84/539/EEC relating to electro-medical equipment which is intended, by its nature, for use in human or veterinary medicine.

items.title: The Energy Act 1983 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st October 1988 further repeals contained in Part I of Schedule 4 to the Energy Act 1983. The Electricity Supply Regulations 1937 and the Electricity (Overhead Lines) Regulations 1970 (S.I. 1970/1355) which were made or continued in force under section 60 of the Electricity Act 1947 will be revoked on the repeal of that section. They are replaced by the Electricity Supply Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1057).

items.title: The Gas (Register) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes the hours during which the register maintained by the Director General of Gas Supply under section 36 of the Gas Act 1986 is to be kept open for public inspection and the fees payable to the Director for the supply of copies or extracts from the register.

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Residuary Body (Winding Up) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for the winding up of the Tyne and Wear Residuary Body in pursuance of a scheme submitted by that Body to the Secretary of State under section 67 of the Local Government Act 1985.

items.title: The Anglian Water Authority (Cringle Brook) Water Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Anglian Water Authority (Cringle Brook) Water Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Hartlepools Water (Leechmire Borehole) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Hartlepools Water (Leechmire Borehole) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London—Penzance Trunk Road A303 (Mere—Wincanton Improvement and Slip Road) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London—Penzance Trunk Road A303 (Mere—Wincanton Improvement and Slip Road) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London—Penzance Trunk Road A303 (Mere—Wincanton Improvement) (Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London—Penzance Trunk Road A303 (Mere—Wincanton Improvement) (Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A523 Derby—Macclesfield—South of Stockport Trunk Road (Macclesfield Relief Road) No. 2 Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A523 Derby—Macclesfield—South of Stockport Trunk Road (Macclesfield Relief Road) No. 2 Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A523 Derby—Macclesfield—South of Stockport Trunk Road (Macclesfield Relief Road) (Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A523 Derby—Macclesfield—South of Stockport Trunk Road (Macclesfield Relief Road) (Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Consumer Arbitration Agreements Act 1988 (Appointed Day No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: The Order provides that the appointed day for sections 1 to 5 of the Consumer Arbitration Agreements Act 1988 (which are concerned with England, Wales and Northern Ireland) is 1st October 1988.

items.title: The Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) Regulations 1983 in their application to highway work.

items.title: The Gaming Machine Licence Duty Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations modify the arrangements for administration of gaming machine licence duty, consequent on the more flexible licensing system introduced in the Finance Act 1987. They replace the Gaming Machine (Licence Duty) Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Treasury Bills (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Treasury Bills Regulations 1968. Regulation 2 is amended to enable an invitation to tender for Treasury bills to be published by means other than the London Gazette. Regulation 3 and Schedule 1 are amended to set out the form of Treasury bills denominated in ECU. Regulation 4 is amended to provide that Treasury bills will be prepared in the denominations specified in the warrant issued by the Treasury. Regulation 6 is amended to describe how payment is to be made where a bill is denominated in ECU. Payment will be made in ECU except that, if the ECU is not the unit of account in which the operations of the European Monetary Co-operation Fund are expressed, payment will be made in a currency chosen by the Bank of England if the holder of the bill so elects or the Bank of England determines that it would not be practicable for payments to be made in ECU. The regulation specifies the currencies that can be chosen and how the amount of the payment in the chosen currency is to be computed. A new regulation 9A is inserted defining the ECU.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 11 of the Clean Air Act 1956 makes the occupier of a building within a smoke control area guilty of an offence if smoke is emitted from the chimney of that building, unless it can be proved that the emission of smoke was caused solely by the use of an authorised fuel. These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales, declare Ancit 40, Ancit 60 and Thermaglow to be authorised fuels if they are manufactured as described in the Schedule. They also revoke Regulations made in 1986 and 1987 which declared Calco Superheat and Calco Cosycoke to be authorised fuels.

items.title: The Community Charges (Registration) (Scotland) (No.2) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations correct drafting errors in the Community Charges (Registration) (Scotland) (No.2) Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”).

items.title: The Tyne and Wear Residuary Body (Winding Up) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order inserts the date 31st October 1988 in article 11 (functions relating to accounts of the Residuary Body) of the Tyne and Wear Residuary Body (Winding Up) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1590), making good the omission of a date from that article.

items.title: The Teachers' Superannuation (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Teachers' Superannuation (Scotland) Regulations 1977.

items.title: The Estate Duty (Interest on Unpaid Duty) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: Estate Duty is chargeable in relation to deaths before 13th March 1975; it was replaced for deaths on or after that date by capital transfer tax, now known as inheritance tax. Article 2 of this Order provides that interest on unpaid estate duty will run at 9 per cent (instead of at the previous rate of 8 per cent as imposed by S.I. 1988/1276) under the various provisions which impose a fixed rate of interest. By Article 3 the limit on the discretionary rate of interest which may be charged where payment of estate duty is postponed on grounds of hardship is correspondingly altered by substituting 9 per cent for 8 per cent. The effect of this Order in conjunction with section 48(1) of the Finance Act 1975 (c. 7) is that from 6th October 1988 interest will also run at 9 per cent when overpaid estate duty is repaid.

items.title: The Estate Duty (Northern Ireland) (Interest on Unpaid Duty) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: Estate Duty is chargeable in relation to deaths before 13th March 1975; it was replaced for deaths on or after that date by capital transfer tax, now known as inheritance tax. Article 2 of this Order provides that interest on unpaid estate duty will run at 9 per cent. (instead of at the previous rate of 8 per cent. as imposed by S.I. 1988/1277) under the various provisions which impose a fixed rate of interest. By Article 3 the limit on the discretionary rate of interest which may be charged where payment of estate duty is postponed on grounds of hardship is correspondingly altered by substituting 9 per cent. for 8 per cent. The effect of this Order in conjunction with section 48(1) of the Finance Act 1975 (c. 7) is that from 6th October 1988 interest will also run at 9 per cent. when overpaid estate duty is repaid.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest on Unpaid Tax and Repayment Supplement) (No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: On and after 6th October 1988 this Order increases from 9.75 per cent. per annum to 10.75 per cent. per annum the rate of interest chargeable on unpaid income tax, surtax, capital gains tax, corporation tax (including advance corporation tax), development land tax, petroleum revenue tax, profits tax, excess profits tax, excess profits levy and on overpaid development land tax. The Order also provides that on and after 6th October 1988 supplement in respect of repayments of certain taxes will be at the rate of 10.75 per cent. per annum instead of 9.75 per cent. per annum.

items.title: The Income Tax (Official Rate of Interest on Beneficial Loans) (No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: By this Order the official rate of interest for the purposes of section 160 of, and Schedule 7 to, the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (charge to income tax on beneficial loans) is prescribed to be 13.5 per cent. per annum on and after 6th October 1988.

items.title: The Inheritance Tax and Capital Transfer Tax (Interest on Unpaid Tax) (No. 2) Order 1988
The Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Interest on Tax Repaid) (No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: Section 92 of the Finance Act 1986 provides that, if certain conditions have been fulfilled and a claim for repayment is made within the stipulated period, stamp duty reserve tax shall be repaid. Where the tax paid is not less than £25, it shall be repaid with interest at the appropriate rate. The section defines the “appropriate rate” as 11 per cent. per annum or such other rate as the Treasury may from time to time specify by order.

items.title: The County Council of the Royal County of Berkshire (Blakes Bridge Reconstruction Reading) Scheme 1987 Confirmation Instrument 1988
items.description: This version of the The County Council of the Royal County of Berkshire (Blakes Bridge Reconstruction Reading) Scheme 1987 Confirmation Instrument 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Port of London (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7 (5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a competent harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the Authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the Authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Portsmouth (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a competent harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Silloth (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the Authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisidiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Workington (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Beverages of an Alcoholic Strength not exceeding 5.5 per cent. (Provisions) (Appointed Day) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for the introduction of new duty bands for wines and made-wines exceeding 1.2 per cent. but not exceeding 5.5 per cent. alcohol by volume, as laid down in section 1(5) of the Finance Act 1988 (with Part II of Schedule 1 to that Act), to come into force from 1st October 1988.

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) (No. 3) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Crown Court Rules 1982. They make provision for the procedure to be adopted when bringing or abandoning an appeal by an accused or the prosecution, under section 22 of the Prosecution of Offences Act 1985 (c. 23), against a decision of a magistrates` court to extend or further extend a time limit imposed under regulations made under that section or to refuse such an extension. The Rules enable applications for extensions or further extensions before a Crown Court or appeals from decisions of magistrates` courts relating to time limits to be heard by a judge of the Crown Court sitting in chambers.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Guarding of Machinery and Safety of Electrical Equipment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations give effect in part to the Merchant Shipping (Minimum Standards) Convention 1976 (International Labour Organisation Convention 147) (Cmnd. 7163), laid before Parliament on 24th April 1978 and ratified by the United Kingdom on 28th November 1980 which is in force internationally and requires provisions to be made substantially equivalent to those of (amongst other Conventions) the Prevention of Accidents (Seafarers) Convention 1970 (International Labour Organisation Convention 134, Article 4) (Cmnd. 4800) which is in force but has not been ratified by the United Kingdom. The Regulations, with other provisions, will also allow ratification by the United Kingdom of the Convention and a recommendation concerning occupational safety and health in dock work (International Labour Organisation Convention 152) (Cmnd. 8118) laid before Parliament in December 1980 and in force internationally.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Means of Access) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace, and re-enact with amendments, the Merchant Shipping (Means of Access) Regulations 1981 as amended, which laid down requirements for the safe access to ships.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Entry into Dangerous Spaces) Regulations 1988
items.description: The note is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Hatches and Lifting Plant) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations give effect in part to the Merchant Shipping (Minimum Standards) Convention 1976 (International Labour Organisation Convention 147) (Cmnd 7163), laid before Parliament on 24th April 1978 and ratified by the United Kingdom on 28th November 1980 which is in force internationally and requires provisions to be made substantially equivalent to those of (amongst other Conventions) the Prevention of Accidents (Seafarers) Convention 1970 (International Labour Organisation Convention 134, Article 4) (Cmnd 4800) which is in force but has not been ratified by the United Kingdom. The Regulations, with other provisions, will also allow ratification by the UK of the Convention and a recommendation concerning occupational safety and health in dock work (International Labour Organisation Convention 152) (Cmnd 8118) laid before Parliament in December 1980 and in force internationally.

items.title: The Prosecution of Offences (Custody Time Limits) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: Regulation 3 of the Prosecution of Offences (Custody Time Limits) Regulations 1987 (the“principal regulations”) restricts the application of the regulations to proceedings instituted in certain counties (namely, Avon, Kent, Somerset and West Midlands); and regulation 5 restricts the application of the provisions dealing with custody time limits in the Crown Court to certain Crown Court centres (namely, Birmingham, Bristol and Maidstone). Those regulations are amended so as to include Greater Manchester and all the counties in the Wales and Chester and Western circuits and all the Crown Court centres in those areas and West Midlands.

items.title: The Motorcycles (Sound Level Measurement Certificates) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Motorcycles (Sound Level Measurement Certificates) Regulations 1980 made provision as required under Council Directive 78/1015/EEC of the 23rd November 1978 (on the approximation of the laws of the Member States on the permissible sound level and the exhaust system of motorcycles) for the issue of sound level measurement certificates to signify whether or not certain types of motorcycles complied with the harmonised requirements specified in that Directive.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Safe Movement on Board Ship) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations give effect in part to the Merchant Shipping (Minimum Standards) Convention 1976 (International Labour Organisation Convention 147) (Cmnd 7163), laid before Parliament on 24 April 1978 and ratified by the United Kingdom on 28 November 1980 which is in force internationally and requires provisions to be made substantially equivalent to those of (amongst other Conventions) the Prevention of Accidents (Seafarers) Convention 1970 (International Labour Organisation Convention 134, Article 4) (Cmnd 4800) which is in force but has not been ratified by the United Kingdom. The Regulations with other provisions will allow ratification by the UK of the Convention and a recommendation concerning occupational safety and health in dock work (International Labour Organisation Convention 152) (Cmnd 8118) laid before Parliament in December 1980 and in force internationally. These Regulations will with others also allow the repeal of the Docks Regulations 1934 (S.R. & O. 1934 No. 279) in respect of those provisions relating to dock operations by ship’s crews.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Breckland and Suffolk River Valleys) Designation (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends Orders previously made by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food which designated areas in Breckland and the Suffolk River Valleys as environmentally sensitive areas. The Order removes the requirement that land which is to revert to grassland or heathland in Breckland or the Suffolk River Valleys, or which is to be used for uncropped wildlife strips or conservation headlands in Breckland, should formerly have been cropped in rotation (articles 2 and 3).

items.title: The Assured Tenancies (Approved Bodies) (No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: Assured tenancies are tenancies which, but for section 56 of the Housing Act 1980, would have been protected tenancies or housing association tenancies under the Rent Act 1977 (c. 42), and which meet certain other conditions. Such tenancies are subject to Part II of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954 (c. 56), as modified by Schedule 5 to the 1980 Act. They can only be granted by bodies approved under section 56 of the 1980 Act.

items.title: The Ceramic Ware (Safety) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations impose revised requirements set out in BS 6748: 1986 relating to the permissible release of lead and cadmium from ceramic ware for use with food (regulation 4).

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Cordless Telephone Apparatus) (Exemption) Regulations 1988
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Regulations)

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies the fees payable for the year beginning 1st April 1988 under the Acts relating to the registration of births, deaths and marriages and associated matters.

items.title: The Teachers' Superannuation (Consolidation) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations consolidate the Regulations revoked by regulation H8 and Schedule 14, comprising the bulk of the Regulations applying to England and Wales made under section 9 of the Superannuation Act 1972.

items.title: The Lyon Court and Office Fees (Variation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the fees for the matters listed in Schedule B to the Lyon King of Arms Act 1867, other than for the appointment of a herald or pursuivant. The fees were last increased in 1986. These fees are exclusive of stamp duty when stamp duty is exigible.

items.title: The Transfer of Offenders (Designation of Equivalent Sentences) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Transfer of Offenders (Designation of Equivalent Sentences) Order 1983, which designates equivalent sentences for the purpose of transfer of offenders between different parts of the United Kingdom, the Channel Islands and the Isle of Man. The amendments reflect the introduction in England and Wales of a new custodial sentence for young offenders of detention in a young offender institution by section 123 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988, which comes into force on 1st October 1988 (S.I. 1988/1408), and, with effect from the coming into force of section 124 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988, the abolition of the sentence of detention in a detention centre in Scotland. They also reflect the abolition of the sentence of Borstal training in Scotland, and the introduction of youth custody sentences in the Isle of Man.

items.title: The Docks Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations impose health, safety and welfare requirements with respect to dock operations (as defined in regulation 2).

items.title: The Loading and Unloading of Fishing Vessels Regulations 1988 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations impose health and safety requirements with respect to fish loading processes, that is to say, the loading, unloading, moving or handling of wet fish at or near a quay or on a fishing vessel when moored at the quay, and incidental activities. For this purpose the handling of wet fish does not include tinning, freezing solid in blocks, curing, freeze-drying or other means of processing.

items.title: The Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations 1988
items.description: The Regulations impose duties on employers to protect employees and other persons who may be exposed to substances hazardous to health and also impose certain duties on employees.

items.title: The London Traffic Control System (Transfer) Order 1988
items.description: Article 3 of this Order comes into force on 7th March 1988 and the remainder comes into force on 1st April 1988.

items.title: The London-Fishguard Trunk Road (Haverfordwest Eastern By-pass) Order 1988
items.description: This Order revokes an Order made under section 1 of the Trunk Roads Act 1936 (c. 5) which provided for a By-pass of the London-Fishguard Trunk Road at Haverfordwest, and also an Order made under section 4 of the Trunk Roads Act 1946 (c. 30), which provided for a junction improvement at Cardigan Road on the London-Fishguard Trunk Road at Haverfordwest. In its place the Order lays down the centre line of a proposed trunk road which if constructed would allow for an Eastern By-pass of Haverfordwest.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Oxfordshire) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Oxfordshire and provides for the petty sessional divisions of Moreton and Wallingford and Wantage and Faringdon to be combined to form a new petty sessional division of Didcot and Wantage.

items.title: The Northern Ireland (Share of United Kingdom Taxes) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Northern Ireland (Share of United Kingdom Taxes) Regulations 1975 (“the principal Regulations”) by changing the method by which the proceeds of petroleum revenue tax (as reduced by repayments of advance petroleum revenue tax) are to be attributed to Northern Ireland. The method of attribution previously applicable to these taxes (being taxes adminstered by the Commissioners of Inland Revenue and charged on corporations, thus falling under regulation 4(1)(b) of the principal Regulations) was based on the total amount of emoluments paid in any financial year by corporations to individuals resident in Northern Ireland. These Regulations provide that the method now applicable to such taxes is based on the size of the population of Northern Ireland, as determined by the most recent estimates published by the Registrars General.

items.title: The Welsh Water Authority (Fixed Engine) Order 1988
items.description: This Order enables the Welsh Water Authority, with the approval of the Secretary of State, to erect and work a fixed engine at Gelli Goch, Gwynedd for catching salmon and migratory trout for scientific purposes and also for the purpose of improving stocks of fish. A definitive plan describing the situation of the fixed engine may be inspected during office hours at the offices of the Authority at Penrhosgarnedd, Bangor, Gwynedd LL57 2DJ

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Motor Vehicles (Type Approval) Regulations 1980 so that—the type approval requirements relating to the measures to be taken against air pollution by gases from positive ignition and diesel engines of motor vehicles are, as an alternative to the existing requirements, the requirements of Council Directive 70/220/EEC as amended by Council Directive 74/290/EEC, Commission Directives 77/102/EEC and 78/665/EEC, and Council Directives 83/351/EEC, 88/76/EEC and 88/436/EEC;the type approval requirements relating to the rear view mirrors of motor vehicles are, as an alternative to the existing requirements, the requirements of Council Directive 71/127/EEC as amended by Commission Directives 79/795/EEC, 85/205/EEC, 86/562/EEC and 88/321/EEC; andthe type approval requirements relating to the field of vision of motor vehicle drivers are, as an alternative to the existing requirements, the requirements of Council Directive 77/649/EEC as amended by Commission Directives 81/643/EEC and 88/366/EEC.

items.title: The Merseyside Traffic Control System (Transfer) Order 1988
items.description: Article 3 of this Order comes into force on 7th March and the remainder comes into force on 1st April 1988.

items.title: The Licensing (Retail Sales) Act 1988 (Commencement) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings the Licensing (Retail Sales) Act 1988 into force on 1st November 1988.

items.title: The Registered Establishments (Fees) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order sets the maximum levels for the fees chargeable by local authorities to applicants for registration, annual continuation of a registration, variation of conditions of registration, and for issuing a new certificate of registration under Part IV of the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968, and the times at which such fees are to be paid.

items.title: The Registration of Establishments (Appeal Tribunal) (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules extend the definitions of “appeal” in the Registration of Establishments (Appeal Tribunal) (Scotland) Rules 1983 to include reference to the new provisions in section 63A of the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (inserted by section 4 of the Registered Establishments (Scotland) Act 1987), and of “appellant” to include the new grounds for appeal with regard to the imposition or variation of conditions of registration.

items.title: The Registration of Establishments (Application Form) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes the form in which an application for registration in respect of any establishment to which section 61 or 61A of the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 applies is to be made and the information which is to be provided to the registration authority by the applicant.

items.title: The Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Regulations 1983 (“the 1983 Regulations”). Apart from regulations 1, 2 and 3(5) of these Regulations, the remainder are made in consequence of amendments made to the Social Security Act 1975 by the Social Security Act 1986.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Amendment No.3 Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Amendment No.3 Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 12th October 1988 sections 33, 34, 37—39, 40—42, 49, 51—57, 59, 63, 70, 96, 97, 104—107, 121, 122, 131—133, 145, 147, 148, 155—157, 163—165 and 170 of, and Schedule 11, so far as it is not already in force, and Schedule 12 to, the Criminal Justice Act 1988, and associated amendments and repeals. These provisions amend the law on miscellaneous aspects of evidence and procedure in criminal cases, and on the level and enforcement of fines and other pecuniary orders, and introduce a statutory right to apply for compensation for miscarriages of justice.

items.title: The Mostyn Docks Harbour Empowerment Order 1988
items.description: This Order establishes Mostyn Docks Limited as a statutory harbour authority, defines the limits of jurisdiction of the Company and confers upon it certain incidental powers in connection with the dock undertaking at Mostyn.

items.title: The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment No. 3 Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment No. 3 Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Amendment No. 4 Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Order 1987 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent the human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Amendment No. 4 Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Order 1987, as amended by S.I. 1988/9, 951 and 1329, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Protected Tenancies (Exceptions) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
The Alcoholic Liquor Duties (Beer-based Beverages) Order 1988
The A106 Trunk Road (Church Lane, Waltham Forest) (Restriction of Use of Gap in Central Reservation) Order 1988
The Criminal Justice Act 1987 (Notice of Transfer) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide for the form of notice of transfer under section 4 of the Criminal Justice Act 1987. That is the notice which is sent to a magistrates' court before the start of committal proceedings by or on behalf of a designated authority (as defined in that section, for example, the Director of the Serious Fraud Office) and which has the effect of transferring the case to the Crown Court. The Regulations require copies of the notice to be sent to the defendant, the Crown Court and (if the defendant is in custody) the prison governor.

items.title: The District of South Somerset (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The District of South Somerset (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Stability of Passenger Ships) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Merchant Shipping (Passenger Ship Construction) Regulations 1980 and the Merchant Shipping (Passenger Ship Construction and Survey) Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1987 (Dismissal of Transferred Charges) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules make provision for the purposes of section 6 of the Criminal Justice Act 1987 (c. 38), which permits a person charged with an offence or offences of fraud whose case has been transferred to the Crown Court under section 4 of that Act to apply to a Crown Court judge for the charge or charges to be dismissed.

items.title: The Petty Sessions Areas (Divisions and Names) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace the Petty Sessional Divisions (Review) Regulations 1952, and prescribe, in relation to reports and draft orders under section 23 of the Justices of the Peace Act 1979, with respect to the division of a non-metropolitan county, metropolitan district or outer London borough into petty sessional divisions, and in relation to draft orders under section 24A of that Act, with respect to the name of a petty sessions, requirements as to notice and as to the manner in which, and the times within which, objections may be made.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1987 (Preparatory Hearings) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules are made for the purposes of the holding by Crown Court judges of preparatory hearings under Part I of the Criminal Justice Act 1987 (c. 38) in cases of serious and complex fraud. By virtue of rule 2 they come into force on 31st October 1988.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1987 (Preparatory Hearings) (Interlocutory Appeals) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules make provision for regulating the practice and procedure of the criminal division of the Court of Appeal for the purposes of appeals against orders or rulings made at preparatory hearings in cases of fraud held under section 9 of the Criminal Justice Act 1987 (c. 38).

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Notices of Transfer) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules make provision for the procedure to be followed in magistrates' courts where a notice of transfer is given under section 4 of the Criminal Justice Act 1987, which comes into force on 31st October 1988 (S.I. 1988/397). Under section 4 of that Act, a prosecutor may give to a magistrates' court a notice of transfer in respect of a case proceeding in that court if the offence charged is an indictable offence and reveals a case of serious and complex fraud. The effect of a notice of transfer is to avoid committal proceedings in such cases, but the magistrates' court to which a notice of transfer is given under section 4 retains certain functions, set out in section 5, in respect of the grant or withholding of bail, legal aid and the making of witness orders pending the Crown Court trial.

items.title: The Bideford (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Cattewater (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a competent harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Lowestoft (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the Authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Port of Wisbech (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987, a direction given by a competent harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Weymouth and Portland (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
The M66 Motorway (Manchester Outer Ring Road, Denton to Middleton Section) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1988
items.description: This version of the The M66 Motorway (Manchester Outer Ring Road, Denton to Middleton Section) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 1987 (Commencement No. 6) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 12th October 1988 the remaining provisions of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 1987.

items.title: The Caernarfon (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the Authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Central Institutions (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Central Institutions (Scotland) Regulations 1972 and 1974 and related amendment regulations of general application. The principal change which the Regulations make is to introduce new uniform rules in respect of the composition of the governing bodies of all central institutions grant-aided under section 73(c) of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980. In addition, the Regulations amend the provisions of the enactments, schemes, articles of association and other instruments relating to individual central institutions to take account of the recomposition of their governing bodies.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Operations Book) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to United Kingdom passenger ships on short sea trades, require all such ships to carry a book (called the operations book) containing instructions and information for safe and efficient operation. Matters to be covered are listed in the Schedule, but provision is made for items adequately covered by Master’s Standing Orders to be omitted.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Kent) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Kent and provides for the petty sessional divisions of Faversham and Sittingbourne to be combined to form a new petty sessional division of Faversham and Sittingbourne.

items.title: The Home Purchase Assistance (Recognised Lending Institutions) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order adds three additional bodies to the list in section 447(1) of the Housing Act 1985. (Other bodies have been added to this list by previous orders.) Bodies which are so listed are lending institutions recognised for the purposes of section 445 of the Act and may receive advances from the Secretary of State under that section to enable them to provide assistance to first-time purchasers of house property (the“Homeloan” Scheme).

items.title: The Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations make provision for a payment to be made out of the social fund for each period of cold weather (7 days), if the claimant has been awarded income support for at least one day of that period; his applicable amount includes a disability or pensioner premium or a member of his family is under 2; and an average temperature of 0° Celsius or less is recorded for that period at a climatological station designated for the area (regulation 2).

items.title: The Social Security (Common Provisions) Miscellaneous Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Adjudication) Regulations 1986 (the Adjudication Regulations), the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 (the Claims and Payments Regulations) and the Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Regulations 1988 (the Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery Regulations) (collectively the Common Provisions Regulations).

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Priority of Charges) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: Under the Housing Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”) liability to repay discount following the exercise of the right to buy is secured by a charge on the dwelling-house. Such a charge does not have priority over certain charges securing advances by bodies which are approved lending institutions for the purposes of section 156 of that Act.

items.title: The Mortgage Indemnities (Recognised Bodies) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies three additional bodies as recognised bodies for the purposes of sections 442 and 443 of the Housing Act 1985. Local authorities may, with the approval of the Secretary of State, enter into agreements with recognised bodies providing for indemnities, where such bodies make advances secured by way of mortgage on dwelling-houses bought from the public sector landlords listed in section 444(4) of the Act.

items.title: The M66 Motorway (Middleton to the Lancashire/Yorkshire Motorway (M62) Section) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1988
items.description: This version of the The M66 Motorway (Middleton to the Lancashire/Yorkshire Motorway (M62) Section) and Connecting Roads Scheme 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Mines (Safety of Exit) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Regulations apply below ground to mines (regulation 2), and provide for safe ways of leaving a mine.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas Designation (Wales) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends a series of Orders previously made by the Secretary of State for Wales, namely the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Cambrian Mountains) Designation Order 1986, the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Cambrian Mountains—Extension) Designation Order 1987 and the Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Lleyn Peninsula) Designation Order 1987, which designated areas in the Cambrian Mountains and the Lleyn Peninsula as environmentally sensitive areas. The Order inserts a definition of “cow” and for clarification substitutes a new definition of “livestock unit” (articles 3and 4).

items.title: The River Tummel Catchment Area Protection (Renewal) Order 1988
items.description: This Order renews for the second time the River Tummel Catchment Area Protection Order 1983 which prohibited fishing for or taking freshwater fish in inland waters in the area prescribed in that Order without legal right or without written permission from someone having such a right. The effect of the renewal is that the prohibition has effect from 7th October 1988 to 6th October 1991.

items.title: The River Lunan Catchment Area Protection (Renewal) Order 1988
items.description: This Order renews for the second time the River Lunan Catchment Area Protection Order 1983 which prohibited fishing for or taking freshwater fish in inland waters in the area prescribed in that Order without legal right or without written permission from someone having such a right. The effect of the renewal is that the prohibition has effect from 7th October 1988 to 6th October 1991.

items.title: The Self-Propelled Industrial Trucks (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Self-Propelled Industrial Trucks (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Borough of Blyth Valley (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Borough of Blyth Valley (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Indictments (Procedure) (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules provide that the period of 28 days in which a bill of indictment is to be prepared is to begin on the date when the defendant is committed for trial or when a notice of transfer is given under section 4 of the Criminal Justice Act 1987, whichever is appropriate.

items.title: The Insolvency (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Insolvency (Amendment) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas Designation (England) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends a series of Orders previously made by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food which designated areas in the South Downs, the North Peak and the Test Valley as environmentally sensitive areas. The Order inserts a definition of “cow” and, for clarification, substitutes a new definition of “livestock unit” (articles 3 and 4).

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 28) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of that part of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule which lies in controlled waters, that is, the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29). The pipe-line lies between the Sean gasfield, situated east of the coast of Lincolnshire, and the Shell terminal at Bacton in Norfolk.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 29) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of that part of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule which lies in controlled waters, that is, the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29). The pipe-line lies between the Leman Bank gas field, situated north-east of the coast of Norfolk, and the Amoco Terminal at Bacton in Norfolk.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 30) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of that part of each of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule which lies in controlled waters, that is, the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29). The pipe-lines lie between the Hewett gas field off the Norfolk coast and Phillip’s terminal at Bacton in Norfolk.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 31) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of that part of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule which lies in controlled waters, that is, the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29). The pipe-line lies between the Esmond gasfield, situated east of the North Yorkshire coast, and the Amoco Terminal at Bacton in Norfolk.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 32) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property) (No.2) (West Yorkshire) Order 1988
items.description: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property) (West Yorkshire) Orders 1987 and 1988 (S.I. 1987/15, 1451 and 1988/751) transferred specified property formerly vested in West Yorkshire County Council and in the West Yorkshire Residuary Body to certain of the district councils in that county. This Order makes further transfers of the same kind.

items.title: The Employment Protection (Medical Suspension) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st October 1989, removes provisions from, and adds one provision to, the list of specified provisions in Schedule 1 to the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 (“the 1978 Act”). The Order is consequential upon the Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations 1988 which revoke the provisions removed from the list. It ensures, by adding regulation 11 of those Regulations to the list, that an employee who is suspended from work on medical grounds in consequence of that regulation is entitled to be paid remuneration by his employer under section 19 of the 1978 Act, where that section allows.

items.title: The London Government Reorganisation (Mortgages) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers to the Council of the London borough of Richmond-upon-Thames (“Richmond”) a variety of interests, functions, rights and liabilities held by the London Residuary Body in relation to mortgages of residential property.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (The Broads) Designation (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends an Order previously made by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food which designated an area in the Broads as an environmentally sensitive area. The Order inserts a definition of “cow” and, for clarification, substitutes a new definition of “livestock unit” (articles 3 and 4).

items.title: The Rickmansworth Water Company (Repeal of Local Enactments) Order 1988
items.description: This Order repeals local enactments enabling the Rickmansworth Water Company to abstract water by means of their pumping stations at Amersham, Batchworth, Berkhamsted, Blackford, Bulstrode, Chalfont St. Giles, Chartridge, Chorleywood, Deep Mill, Gerrards Cross, Hughenden, Hunton Bridge, Iver, Mill End, Springwell, Stockers, Uxbridge, West Drayton, West Hyde and Western Avenue.

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Great Britain, further amend the Seed Potatoes Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Somerset Levels and Moors) Designation (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends an Order previously made by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food which designated an area in the Somerset Levels and Moors as an environmentally sensitive area. The Order inserts a definition of “cow” and, for clarification, substitutes a new definition of “livestock unit” (articles 3 and 4).

items.title: The Spirits (Rectifying, Compounding and Drawback) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations come into force on 1st December 1988. They replace Parts II, III, V, VI and regulation 76(2) of the Spirits Regulations 1952, the Spirits (Removal and Stock Books) Regulations 1967 and the Spirits (Removal and Stock Books) Regulations 1967, etc (Amendment) Regulations 1981 with simpler and up-dated provisions.

items.title: The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) (Variation) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) (Variation) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Police (Complaints) (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police (Complaints) (General) Regulations 1985 to enable the Commissioner of Police for the Metropolis to delegate certain functions and duties in relation to police complaints under Part IX of the Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 to an officer of the rank of commander as well as to an assistant commissioner or a deputy assistant commissioner.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (Cotswold Cable Television Company Limited) Order 1988
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to Cotswold Cable Television Company Limited on26th August 1988 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Cotswold area. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 19th October 1988. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, Cotswold Cable Television Company Limited will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The A13 Trunk Road (New Road, Havering) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A13 Trunk Road (New Road, Havering) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Folkestone—Honiton Trunk Road (A31) (Picket Post Junction Improvement Slip Roads) Order 1987
The Trade Descriptions (Place of Production) (Marking) Order 1988
items.description: This Order requires goods, when supplied or exposed or offered for supply in the course of a trade or business, and presented in a way which gives a misleading indication as to where they were manufactured or produced, to be marked with or accompanied by a corrective statement of their origin. Section 36(1) of the Trade Descriptions Act 1968 (which provides that goods are deemed to have been manufactured or produced in the country in which they last underwent a treatment or process resulting in a substantial change) applies for the purposes of the Order. Breach of the Order is a criminal offence under section 8(2) of that Act.

items.title: The Foreign Prison-made Goods Act 1897 (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Regulations amend the Foreign Prison-made Goods Act 1897 to accord with Article 30 of the EEC Treaty. Article 30 prohibits quantitative restrictions on imports of goods from other Member States and all measures having equivalent effect. Prison-made goods originating or in free circulation in another Member State are expressly removed from the prohibition in section 1 of the 1897 Act.

items.title: The A11 Trunk Road (High Road, Leytonstone, Waltham Forest) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A11 Trunk Road (High Road, Leytonstone, Waltham Forest) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1985 (Police and Fire and Civil Defence Authorities) Precepts Limitation Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes maximum precepts, in respect of the financial year beginning on 1st April 1988, for such of the police and fire and civil defence authorities established under Part IV of the Local Government Act 1985 as are listed in the Schedule to the Order. In each case the maximum is shown against the name of the authority (in respect of the current financial year, precepts were set by section 7 of and Schedule 2 to the Local Government Finance Act 1987 (c. 6), obviating the need for an Order in 1987).

items.title: The Housing and Planning Act 1986 (Commencement No. 12) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Housing and Planning Act 1986 (Commencement No. 12) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Fees for Inquiries (Standard Daily Amount) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England and Wales, apply where a Minister is authorised under or by virtue of specified provisions to recover his costs in connection with the holding of an inquiry.

items.title: The Local Government (Council of the Borough of Newport, Gwent, Library Authority) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision for the constitution of Newport Borough Council in the County of Gwent as the library authority for the area of the Borough from 1st April 1989. As a consequence provision is made for the transfer on that date from Gwent County Council to the Borough Council of relevant property, liabilities, contracts, notices and staff.

items.title: The Finance Act 1987 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: Section 40(4) of the Finance Act 1987 (“section 40(4)”) adds new subsections (2) and (3) at the end of section 92 of the Capital Gains Tax Act 1979 (c. 14) authorising the Treasury to make regulations providing that any scheme of a description specified in the regulations is to be treated as not being a unit trust scheme for the purposes of that Act and containing such supplementary and transitional provisions as appear to the Treasury to be necessary or expedient.

items.title: The Control of Pollution (Special Waste) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England, Wales and Scotland, amend the Transfrontier Shipment of Hazardous Waste Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”) and the Control of Pollution (Special Waste) Regulations 1980 (“the 1980 Regulations”).

items.title: The Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st November 1988 sections 131 to 134, 136 and 220 of and, so far as it is not already in force, Schedule 8 to the Education Reform Act 1988. These provisions relate to the establishment of the Universities Funding Council and the Polytechnics and Colleges Funding Council and supplementary provisions and extension of the functions of the Audit Commission. Also brought into force on the same date are sections 17 to 19 which relate to exceptions and modification of the National Curriculum in certain cases or circumstances and paragraphs 83, 84 and 85 of Schedule 12 which make provision for amendments of the Education Act 1981 relating to statements of special educational needs.

items.title: The Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors (Entry to Training Requirements) Amendment Rules Approval Order 1988
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order, and made by the United Kingdom Central Council for Nursing, Midwifery and Health Visiting, further amend the Rules approved by the Nurses, Midwives and Health Visitors Rules Approval Order 1983 as they relate to the minimum educational requirements for entry to professional training.

items.title: The Education (Higher Education Corporations) Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies those local education authority maintained institutions which appear to the Secretary of State to satisfy the criteria set out in section 121(2) of the Education Reform Act 1988. The Southampton Institute of Higher Education is omitted from the list of specified institutions. The Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No.2) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1794) excludes the same institution when bringing into force section 121 of that Act. By virtue of section 121(2), paragraph 1 of Schedule 7 to that Act and the Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No.2) Order 1988 bodies corporate by the names of those specified institutions are established on 21st November 1988 for the purpose of conducting those institutions as from the transfer date. 1st April 1989 is specified as the transfer date, defined in section 123(2) of the 1988 Act as the date for transfer of local education authority property, rights and liabilities to those corporations. The Order also specifies the local authority or authorities able to nominate local authority nominees to become members of each such corporation.

items.title: The A40 London—Fishguard Trunk Road (Long Lane Junction Improvement Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A40 London—Fishguard Trunk Road (Long Lane Junction Improvement Trunk Road and Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Higher Education Corporations) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision for the establishment of a body corporate to conduct the Winchester School of Art. That body which, by virtue of paragraph 1(2) of Schedule 7 to the Education Reform Act 1988 will have the name of the Winchester School of Art, is established on 21st November 1988 to prepare itself for the conduct of that institution from 1st April 1989 which is appointed as the transfer date for the purposes of section 126 of that Act. It also specifies the County Council of Hampshire and the Council of the City of Winchester as local authorities each able to nominate a local authority nominee to become a member of that body corporate.

items.title: The Education (Higher Education Corporations) (No.3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision for the establishment of a body corporate to conduct the Falmouth School of Art and Design. That body which, by virtue of paragraph 1(2) of Schedule 7 to the Education Reform Act 1988 will have the name of the Falmouth School of Art and Design, is established on 21st November 1988 to prepare itself for the conduct of that institution from 1st April 1989 which is appointed as the transfer date for the purposes of section 126 of that Act. It also specifies the County Council of Cornwall as the local authority able to nominate two local authority nominees to become members of that body corporate.

items.title: The Importation of Milk Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales only, re-enact with amendments the Importation of Milk Regulations 1983, as amended. They make provision for the implementation of Council Directive No. 85/397/EEC (OJ No. L226, 24.8.85, p.13) on health and animal health problems affecting intra-Community trade in heat-treated milk.

items.title: The Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations apply to England and Wales only and come into force on 16th November 1988. They amend the Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) Regulations 1986 by making provision for the further heat treatment of bulk semi-skimmed milk and bulk skimmed milk, including any such milk imported under the Importation of Milk Regulations 1988, which has already been heat treated for the purposes of transportation in another heat treatment establishment.

items.title: The Milk (Special Designation) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations apply to England and Wales only and come into force on 16th November 1988. They amend the Milk (Special Designation) Regulations 1986 by making provision for the further heat treatment of bulk milk, including any such milk imported under the Importation of Milk Regulations 1988, which has already been heat treated for the purposes of transportation in another heat treatment establishment.

items.title: The Fire Safety and Safety of Places of Sport Act 1987 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st January 1989 the provisions of Part III of the Fire Safety and Safety of Places of Sport Act 1987 and the remainder of section 50(4) to (7) of that Act.

items.title: The Safety of Places of Sport Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout Great Britain (except the Isles of Scilly), concern certificates under Part III of the Fire Safety and Safety of Places of Sport Act 1987 (by virtue of sections 26(2) and 41 of that Act referred to as “safety certificates”). Such a certificate which is issued by a local authority (as defined by section 41) is required in respect of the use, at a sports ground which is not a designated sports ground under the Safety of Sports Grounds Act 1975 (c. 52), of each stand which provides covered accommodation for 500 or more spectators to view activities at the ground. By virtue of sections 26(5) and 41 of the 1987 Act such a stand is referred to as a “regulated stand”.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Goods Vehicles (Authorisation of International Journeys) (Fees) Regulations 1983.

items.title: The Road Transport (International Passenger Services) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Transport (International Passenger Services) Regulations 1984 so as to implement with respect to Great Britain and in relation to the transport of passengers the provisions of the agreement between the United Kingdom and the Soviet Union concerning international road transport signed on the 22nd January 1988 (Cmd. 421.). This agreement comes into force on a date to be notified in the London Gazette.

items.title: The Customs and Excise (Deferred Payment) (RAF Airfields and Offshore Installations) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 21st November 1988 are made under section 45(1) and section 127A(2) of the Customs and Excise Management Act 1979.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences) (Temporary Use in Great Britain) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences) (Temporary Use in Great Britain) Regulations 1980. Regulation 3 introduces a new Regulation to implement the Agreement between the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics concerning international road transport signed on the 22nd January 1988 (Cmd. 421). This agreement comes into force for the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland on a date to be notified in the London Gazette. Regulation 4 makes it clear that paragraph 2 of Schedule 2 applies whether or not the vehicle is empty.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Applications) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations specify the requirements for applications for planning permission under the Town and Country Planning Act 1971. They also provide for the giving of directions by a local planning authority about further information and the supply of evidence.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning General Development Order 1988
