items.description: This Order consolidates with amendments the Town and Country Planning General Development Order 1977 and subsequent amending instruments, except the provisions relating to planning applications which are reproduced in the Town and Country Planning (Applications) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1812). The consolidation also incorporates the provision previously made by the special development orders listed in Schedule 7.

items.title: The Importation of Milk (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, re-enact with amendments the Importation of Milk (Scotland) Regulations 1983, as amended.

items.title: The Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 by making provision, in relation to the production of semi-skimmed and skimmed milk, for the further heat treatment of bulk milk (including any such milk imported under the Importation of Milk (Scotland) Regulations 1988) which has already been heat treated for the purposes of transportation in another heat treatment establishment. These Regulations also revoke the existing provisions which banned semi-skimmed and skimmed milk from being heat treated for a second time.

items.title: The Milk (Special Designations) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Milk (Special Designations) (Scotland) Order 1980 by making provision for the further heat treatment of bulk milk (including any such milk imported under the Importation of Milk (Scotland) Regulations 1988) which has already been heat treated for the purposes of transportation in another heat treatment establishment. This Order also revokes the existing provisions which banned milk being heat treated for a second time.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No.3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st November 1988 section 124 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 and associated amendments and repeals. These provisions abolish the sentence of detention in a detention centre, but leave unchanged the sentence of detention in a young offenders institution. The Order does not affect England and Wales.

items.title: The Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987 (Commencement No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 30th November 1988 the provisions of the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987 not so far implemented.

items.title: The Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1985 (Commencement No.4) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 30th November 1988 section 18 of the Law Reform (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Scotland) Act 1985, which introduces a procedure for pursuing small claims in the sheriff court as part of the summary cause procedure. Section 18 is the last provision of the 1985 Act to be brought into force.

items.title: The Scottish Milk Marketing Schemes (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Scottish Milk Marketing Schemes (Amendment) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Police Cadets (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Cadets Regulations 1979 to increase the pay of cadets attached to the Metropolitan Police force from 1st July 1987 and again from 1st September 1987 (retrospective effect is authorised by section 33(4), as applied by section 35(2), of the Police Act 1964).

items.title: The Police (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Regulations 1987. Regulation 3(a) increases London weighting with effect from 1st July 1987 and again from 1st July 1988; regulation 3(c) increases pay and regulation 3(d) increases the dog handler’s allowance, both with effect from 1st September 1988 (retrospection is authorised by section 33(4) of the Police Act 1964). Regulation 3(b) provides for records of punishments to be removed from a police officer’s personal record at the expiration of a specified period automatically instead of at the officer’s request where the specified period ends on or after 1st January 1989.

items.title: The European Parliamentary Elections (Day of Election) Order 1988
items.description: This Order appoints 15th June 1989 as the day on which the next general election of United Kingdom representatives to the European Parliament shall be held.

items.title: The European Parliamentary Elections (Day of By-election) (Hampshire Central Constituency) Order 1988
items.description: This Order appoints 15th December 1988 as the day on which the by-election to fill the vacancy in the European Parliamentary constituency of Hampshire Central shall be held.

items.title: The Patents, Designs and Marks Act 1986 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st January 1989 the remaining provisions of the Patents, Designs and Marks Act 1986. They (1) substitute a new section 17 of the Registered Designs Act 1949 and repeal section 24 of that Act and (2) substitute a new section 32 of the Patents Act 1977 and repeal section 35 of that Act.

items.title: The Land Registration (District Registries) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Land Registration (District Registries) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The (A650) North-West of Doncaster—Kendal Trunk Road (Drighlington Bypass) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A650) North-West of Doncaster—Kendal Trunk Road (Drighlington Bypass) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The (A650) North-West of Doncaster—Kendal Trunk Road (Bradford District Boundary to Gildersome Interchange De-Trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A650) North-West of Doncaster—Kendal Trunk Road (Bradford District Boundary to Gildersome Interchange De-Trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A12) (Cambridge Park Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A12) (Cambridge Park Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A12) (Eastway to Eastern Avenue and Slip Roads at Wanstead) Variation Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that the new trunk road which the Secretary of State is authorised to construct in the London Boroughs of Newham, Waltham Forest and Redbridge shall be 25 metres shorter than previously authorised. The shortening of the trunk road is a consequence of the redesign of the Lea interchange. This has resulted in a slight shift in the point at which the slip roads join the Link Road and hence a change in the point at which non-motorway traffic may join or leave the Link Road.

items.title: The London—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A12) (Eastway to Eastern Avenue Section) (Green Man Interchange Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A12) (Eastway to Eastern Avenue Section) (Green Man Interchange Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A12) (Eastway to Eastern Avenue Section) (Lea Interchange Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A12) (Eastway to Eastern Avenue Section) (Lea Interchange Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The London—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A106) Detrunking Order 1988
The London—Norwich Trunk Road (A11) (Leytonstone Road and High Road Leytonstone Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The London—Norwich Trunk Road (A11) (Leytonstone Road and High Road Leytonstone Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A102(M) Motorway (Eastway Section) No. 2 Scheme 1988
items.description: This Scheme authorises the Secretary of State to provide a 25 metre length of special road which shall add to the special road which he is authorised to construct by the A102(M) Motorway (Eastway Section) Scheme 1986. The additional length of special road is required as a consequence of the proposed redesign of the Lea interchange which has resulted in a slight shift in the point at which the slip roads join the Link Road and hence a change in the point at which non-motorway traffic may join or leave the Link Road.

items.title: The A102(M) Motorway (Eastway Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1988
items.description: This version of the The A102(M) Motorway (Eastway Section) Connecting Roads Scheme 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (British Museum (Natural History)) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers to the Lord President of the Council the power under section 8(2)(b) of the British Museum Act 1963 to appoint one of the Natural History Trustees, and the power under section 10(2) to amend Part II of Schedule 3 to that Act, presently exercised by the Secretary of State. The buildings for the time being described in Part II of that Schedule are the authorised repositories for the collections of the British Museum (Natural History).

items.title: The Local Authorities (Armorial Bearings) Order 1988
items.description: This Order authorises Faversham town council to bear and use the armorial bearings of the former Faversham borough council.

items.title: The Bermuda (Territorial Sea) Order in Council 1988
items.description: This Order extends the boundaries of Bermuda, so as to include, as territorial sea, the sea within twelve nautical miles of the baselines, together with its seabed and subsoil, and makes other provisions in this connection. In particular, it defines the baseline from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured as generally the low water line, except that, between points at 32°15.2'N64°52.3'W and 32°22.7'N64°39.7'W on the coast inshore of Hogfish Cut and Town Cut respectively, the baseline follows the seaward limit of the main reef as shown on Admiralty Chart 334 to the west and north of the main group of the islands of Bermuda. Where there is a break or passage in that reef, the baseline is a straight line joining the seaward entry points of that break or passage.

items.title: The Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) (Amendment) (No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) Order 1986. It adds Austria to the list of the Contracting States to the Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction, The Hague, 25th October 1980 (Cm.33).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Oil Pollution) (Hong Kong) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Oil Pollution) (Hong Kong) Order 1984 to enable the Governor of Hong Kong to make regulations to give effect in Hong Kong to the amendments to the Annex to the Protocol of 1978 relating to the International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships 1973 adopted on 7th September 1984 by the Marine Environment Protection Committee (“MEPC”) of the International Maritime Organization at its 20th Session. The amendments adopted by MEPC are contained in resolution MEPC 14(20), which may be obtained from the International Maritime Organization, 4 Albert Embankment, London SE1 7SR.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (Cayman Islands) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends to the Cayman Islands certain provisions of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988, as amended. Its main purpose is to make fresh provision for the registration of entitled vessels in the Cayman Islands, and it also makes provision with respect to the safety of navigation (sections 30—32, 35 and 50), prevention of oil pollution (sections 30, 31, 34, 35 and 50), inquiries (section 44) and crew agreements (section 46). Schedule 2 sets out Part I of the Merchant Shipping Act 1894 as it now applies in the Cayman Islands.

items.title: The St. Helena Constitution Order 1988
items.description: This Order confers a new Constitution on St. Helena and its Dependencies. In particular it provides for a Governor appointed by Her Majesty, and for St. Helena an Executive Council, a Legislative Council, and Council Committees. Provision is also made regarding a Supreme Court and Court of Appeal for the territories.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Health and Social Security) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision consequent on the establishment of the Department of Health and the Department of Social Security and the entrusting to the Secretaries of State for those Departments of functions formerly carried out by the Secretary of State for Social Services.

items.title: The Fuel and Electricity (Control) Act 1973 (Continuation) (Jersey) Order 1988
items.description: This Order continues provisions of the Fuel and Electricity (Control) Act 1973 in force in the Bailiwick of Jersey for one year from 30th November 1988 (being the date to which those provisions were continued in force by the Fuel and Electricity (Control) Act 1973 (Continuation) (Jersey) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/1834)).

items.title: The Customs Duties (ECSC) (Quota and Other Reliefs) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Customs Duties (ECSC) (Quota and Other Reliefs) Order 1987, which provides for exemption from customs duty for certain iron and steel products, so that it no longer applies to goods originating in the Republic of Korea.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1965 (Guernsey) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends to the Bailiwick of Guernsey the Merchant Shipping Act 1965, with the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule to the Order. It revokes and replaces the Merchant Shipping Act 1965 (Guernsey) Order 1969; its effect is to extend to Guernsey amendments to the 1965 Act made subsequent to the 1969 Order.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1979 (Guernsey) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends to the Bailiwick of Guernsey section 31(1) of the Merchant Shipping Act 1979. That provision amends section 85 of the Merchant Shipping Act 1894 (c. 60) (which relates to dues for space occupied by deck cargo) by substituting a newsubsection (3).

items.title: The Reciprocal Enforcement of Foreign Judgments (Canada) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends article 3 of the Reciprocal Enforcement of Foreign Judgments (Canada) Order 1987 so as to include the courts of the Province of Saskatchewan as recognised courts.

items.title: The Registration of Title (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends the system of compulsory registration of title on sale of land to the districts mentioned in article 2(1)(a) with effect from 1st February 1989 and, to those mentioned in article 2(1)(b) with effect from 1st December 1989.

items.title: The Copyright (International Conventions) (Amendment No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Copyright (International Conventions) (Amendment No. 3) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Patents, Designs and Marks (Guinea-Bissau and Malaysia) (Convention and Relevant Countries) Order 1988
items.description: The Governments of Guinea-Bissau and Malaysia have given notice of their respective accessions to the International Convention for the Protection of Industrial Property (as revised at Stockholm in 1967) (Cmnd. 4431). This Order declares each of Guinea-Bissau and Malaysia to be a convention country for the purposes of the Registered Designs Act 1949 and the Patents Act 1977 and amends the Trade Marks and Service Marks (Relevant Countries) Order 1986 to take account of the accessions.

items.title: The Social Security Act 1988 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Social Security Act 1988 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Road Transport) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st August 1989, gives effect to proposals of the Road Transport Industry Training Board which were submitted to and approved by the Training Commission, and thereafter submitted by the Training Commission to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the road transport industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1988 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations replace the Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1980 as amended. The Regulations implement Council Directive No. 71/316/EEC (O.J. L202, 6.9.71, p.1, O.J./S.E. 1971(II) p.707) relating to measuring instruments and methods of metrological control, as amended by Council Directives No. 72/427/EEC (O.J. L291, 28.12.72, p.156, O.J./S.E.1972, 28–30 Dec., p.71), No. 83/575/EEC (O.J. L332, 28.11.83, p.43), No. 87/354/EEC (O.J. L192, 11.7.87, p.43) and No. 87/355/EEC (O.J. L192, 11.7.87, p.46). They also implement the Council Directives relating to particular categories of instruments, which are mentioned in Regulation 2(1). Together these Directives provide procedures and requirements for instruments to receive EEC pattern approval and EEC initial verification as an alternative to complying with national law in each of the member States.

items.title: The Nursing Homes Registration (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide for the registration of persons in respect of nursing homes under the Registration of Nursing Homes (Scotland) Act 1938 (“the 1938 Act”). They supersede previous Regulations governing registration.

items.title: The Crown Prosecution Service (Witnesses' etc. Allowances) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide for the entitlement to costs and expenses of witnesses, interpreters and other persons who attend court or elsewhere at the instance of the Crown Prosecution Service in proceedings conducted by the Director of Public Prosecutions under Part I of the Prosecution of Offences Act 1985.

items.title: The Serious Fraud Office (Witnesses' etc. Allowances) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide for the entitlement to costs and expenses of witnesses, interpreters and other persons who attend court or elsewhere at the instance of the Serious Fraud Office in proceedings conducted by the Director of the Serious Fraud Office under Part I of the Criminal Justice Act 1987.

items.title: The Exeter — Launceston — Bodmin Trunk Road A30 (Plusha to Bolventor Improvement) (Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Exeter — Launceston — Bodmin Trunk Road A30 (Plusha to Bolventor Improvement) (Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Exeter—Launceston—Bodmin Trunk Road A30 (Plusha to Bolventor Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Exeter—Launceston—Bodmin Trunk Road A30 (Plusha to Bolventor Improvement and Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Commissioner for Local Administration in Scotland (Expenses) Regulations 1988
items.description: Schedule 4 to the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1975 provides for the remuneration of, and other expenses incurred by, the Commissioner for Local Administration in Scotland and his officers being met by “the body designated for Scotland” . The Secretary of State has designated the Commission for Local Authority Accounts in Scotland as that body.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No. 7) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 8 of the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 (“the 1986 Regulations”) so that the permitted maximum overall width of a refrigerated vehicle is increased from 2·58 to 2·60 metres.

items.title: The Renfrew and Cunninghame Districts (Whitehouse) Boundaries Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers an area of approximately 29 hectares at Whitehouse from Renfrew District to Cunninghame District and an area of approximately 2.6 hectares from Cunninghame District to Renfrew District and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Three Valleys Water Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Three Valleys Water Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) Regulations 1986 in the following respects.1Regulation 6, which prescribes a 21 day period of notice for applications to vary timetables for all or part of a week which includes 24th December, Christmas Day, Good Friday or any bank holiday, is omitted. Instead regulation 9(2) is amended so that services may be varied without variation of registration in England and Wales in respect of journeys taking place in such weeks and in Scotland in respect of journeys taking place in a week which includes Christmas Day, 26th December, New Year’s Day, any bank holiday or a local holiday. In addition operators are required to send notice of such variations to the relevant authorities and to the traffic commissioner no later than 21 days before the variations take effect (regulation 9(3)).2One of the cases where the traffic commissioner may substitute a different period of notice from that prescribed — the case specified in regulation 7(2)(a) — is amended so that it covers operators who apply to register or vary a registration with respect to a service or part of a service which will be substantially similar to a service which he has ceased or is ceasing to operate. In addition a new case is added to regulation 7(2) — where an operator applies to register or vary a registration with respect to a service or part of a service to meet an urgent and exceptional public passenger transport requirement (regulation 7(2)(h)).3A new case is added to regulation 9(2) (variations of registered services without variation of registration). This is where a service is varied by the provision of additional vehicles which are operated over any part of the route and as closely as possible to the timings in the timetable (regulation 9(2)(g)).4A new regulation, regulation 9A, is inserted which makes provision for the cancellations of registrations relating to discontinued services.5The fee for an application to register particulars of a local service is increased from £27 to £30, and the fee for an application to vary the registered particulars of a service is increased from £15 to £27.6A prescribed particular of a registered service, the largest category of vehicle to be used (paragraph 10 of the Schedule), is omitted.7Regulation 5(2), which provided for an extension of periods of notice (save as specified) to the 25th January 1987 if they expired before that date, is omitted as the paragraph is now spent.

items.title: The Community Charges (Levying, Collection and Payment) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe matters relating to the levy and payment of community charges and community water charges under the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc. (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Amendment No. 4 Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Order 1988 (“the principal Order”) contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the Ukraine, USSR.

items.title: The Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which applies to Scotland only, and which revokes and replaces the Import and Export (Plant Health Fees) (Scotland) Order 1987, prescribes fees in respect of the services listed in Schedules 1 and 2 to the Order (articles 3 and 4).

items.title: The Family Law (Scotland) Act 1985 (Commencement No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 30th November 1988 the remaining provision of the Family Law (Scotland) Act 1985 which is not already in force. This provision, section 25, provides for the presumption of equal shares in household goods.

items.title: The Education (Higher Education Corporations) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Education (Higher Education Corporations) (No. 2) Order 1988 and the Education (Higher Education Corporations) (No. 3) Order 1988 by giving the names under which the new corporations referred to in those Orders shall be established.

items.title: Community Charges (Information Concerning Social Security) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the Community Charges (Information Concerning Social Security) (Scotland) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Community Charge Rebates) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations have effect to provide for matters concerning entitlement to, the amount of and the claiming and payment of housing benefit in the form of community charge rebates in Scotland in respect of a person’s liability for personal community charges or collective community charge contributions under the Abolition of Domestic Rates Etc (Scotland) Act 1987.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Amendment (No.3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Civil Legal Aid (Scotland) Regulations 1987 in the following respects.

items.title: The Court of Session Etc. Fees Amendment (No.3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order reduces the fee payable to the Principal Clerk of Session and Justiciary for defending a consistorial action to the same level as that prescribed for commencing the action.

items.title: The Sheriff Court Fees Amendment (No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order imposes two new fees relating to new legislation. Item 16.3 of the Table of Fees is for citations or intimations by sheriff officer in cases heard under the Small Claims procedure. Item 27A is for the inspection of reports of sales and auditor of court’s reports under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987. Fees prescribed under item 27A are the only court fees payable in relation to proceedings under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations firstly replace article 6 of the Transport Act 1985 (Commencement No.6) Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1794) and article 3 of the Transport Act 1985 (Commencement No.7) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/1228) which purported to amend the Motor Vehicles (Tests) Regulations 1981 (“the 1981 Regulations”). As a result of the amendments buses being used to provide a community bus service and small buses constructed or adapted to carry more than 12 seated passengers used under a permit granted under section 19 of the Transport Act 1985 are included in Class V for the purposes of the 1981 Regulations.

items.title: The Customs and Excise (Deferred Payment) (RAF Airfields and Offshore Installations) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 21st November 1988 are made under section 45(1) and section 127A of the Customs and Excise Management Act 1979.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Protection of Shipping and Trading Interests) (USSR) (Revocation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order revokes the Merchant Shipping (Protection of Shipping and Trading Interests) (USSR) Order 1986, which required certain persons in the United Kingdom connected with Soviet shipping lines to provide information to the Secretary of State concerning carryings by Soviet lines in the trades between the United Kingdom and East Africa, the United Kingdom and the Far East and the United Kingdom and Central America.

items.title: The Local Government (Rate Product) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Local Government (Rate Product) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 33) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie above and between two offshore gasfields situated east of the coast of Lincolnshire.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 34) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule which lie above and between two offshore gasfields situated east of the coast of Lincolnshire.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Orders) (No.35) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie above and between two offshore gasfields situated east of the coast of Lincolnshire.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 36) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies specified in Part I of the Schedule as owners of those parts of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule which lie in controlled waters, that is the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the maximum non-domestic rate which a local authority may determine for the financial year 1989-90 (regulation 4 and the Schedule).

items.title: Training Commission (Incidental and Transitional Provisions) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes incidental and transitional provisions for the purposes of, and in connection with, the revocation by the Secretary of State, subject to certain savings, of all authorisations or directions given to the Training Commission under the Employment and Training Act 1973. The Order provides for the transfer of property (other than land) and of rights and liabilities from the Training Commission to the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Penalty Points (Alteration) Order 1988
items.description: This Order alters the range of penalty points applicable to various offences under the Road Traffic Act 1972.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 58(3) of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988, Part II thereof comes into force when the Merchant Shipping (Registration of Fishing Vessels) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1926) come into force which is on 1st December 1988. This Order brings into force on the same date related provisions of the Act which are set out in the Schedule to the Order. It also effects the repeals set out in the Appendix to the Schedule which are consequential on the coming into force of Part II.

items.title: The Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend in two respects the Social Fund Cold Weather Payments (General) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Fishing Vessels—Tonnage) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations continue the provisions for tonnage measurement laid down in the Merchant Shipping (Fishing Boats Registry) Order 1981 (S.I. 1981/740) for vessels under 24 metres in length.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Passenger Boarding Cards) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations apply to United Kingdom passenger ships operating as passenger ships of Classes II and II(A), and require the operation of a boarding card system for all voyages on which passengers are carried. They require documentation to be retained for future inspection by persons duly authorised by the Secretary of State, and supplementary arrangements to be made for the determination of the numbers of passengers who remain on board the ship from a previous voyage. It is to be an offence under the Regulations for a ship to leave its berth before the total number of passengers on board has been determined by means of the boarding card system and the master informed. It is also to be an offence for any person to make a false statement in connection with the boarding card system, or to falsify the documentation.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Tonnage) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Tonnage) Regulations 1982 (“the 1982 Regulations”) which give effect to the International Tonnage Convention 1969.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Transitional Provisions — Fishing Vessels) Order 1988
items.description: Part II of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988, which establishes a new system for the registration of fishing vessels, comes into force on 1st December 1988.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Warwickshire) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Warwickshire and provides for the petty sessional divisions of Alcester, Kineton, Shipston on Stour and Stratford-upon-Avon to be combined to form a new petty sessional division of South Warwickshire, and for the petty sessional divisions of Southam and Warwick to be combined to form a new petty sessional division of Mid-Warwickshire.

items.title: The Rivers Tweed and Eye Protection (Renewal) Order 1988
items.description: This Order renews for the third time the Rivers Tweed and Eye Protection Order 1980 which prohibited fishing for or taking freshwater fish in inland waters in the area prescribed in that Order without legal right or without written permission from someone having such a right. The effect of the renewal is that the prohibition has effect from 8th November 1988 to 6th October 1991.

items.title: The Precept Limitation (Prescribed Maximum) (Greater Manchester Passenger Transport Authority) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes a maximum precept of 15.58 pence in the pound for the Greater Manchester Passenger Transport Authority for the financial year beginning on 1st April 1988.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Registration of Fishing Vessels) Regulations 1988
items.description: Part II of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988, which comes into force on the making of these Regulations, provides that there shall be a new registration system for fishing vessels. These Regulations provide for the establishment of one central register for the whole of the United Kingdom which will be at the offices of the Registrar General of Shipping and Seamen in Cardiff. However notwithstanding that registration is centralised vessels will be registered as belonging to a particular port of registry and the existing system of marking vessels with port letters and numbers will continue.

items.title: The Sutton Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: This Order authorises the Sutton Harbour Company to construct a sea wall adjacent to the East Pier of the seaward entrance to the harbour of Sutton Pool in the port of Plymouth and to reclaim land as requisite for these works. The Order also makes further provision as to the fish market of the Company at the harbour and for the repeal of certain provisions of the Sutton Harbour Acts and Orders 1847 to 1986.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Merchant Shipping (Fees) Regulations 1988 by inserting a Part prescribing fees payable in respect of registration of fishing vessels in the new register established under Part II of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988

items.title: The Precept Limitation (Prescribed Maximum) (Merseyside Passenger Transport Authority) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes a maximum precept of 27 pence in the pound for the Merseyside Passenger Transport Authority for the financial year beginning on 1st April 1988.

items.title: The Quarries (Explosives) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations apply to the keeping, handling and use of explosives (including detonators) at quarries (regulation 3), subject to the exceptions specified in that regulation.

items.title: The Scottish Land Court (Fees) Amendment Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Table of Fees in the Scottish Land Court Rules 1979 for two purposes—1to increase (with some exceptions) the fees payable in respect of applications to the Scottish Land Court; and2to impose, at paragraphs (1)(e), (2)(i), (3)(c) and (d) for the first time, fees in respect of Hearings and ddditional fees where an application is dismissed or withdrawn.

items.title: The National Health Service (Payments for Optical Appliances) Amendment (No. 4) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Payments for Optical Appliances) Regulations 1986 which provide for payments to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of the cost of optical appliances supplied to certain categories of persons following a sight test under the National Health Service in England and Wales.

items.title: The Police Pensions (Lump Sum Payments to Widows) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Police Pensions Regulations 1971 (which apply in relation to policemen who ceased to serve before 1st April 1972) provide for discretionary increases in police widows' pensions where the widow would have been entitled to widow’s benefit or a retirement pension under the Social Security Acts had her husband satisfied the contribution conditions therefor or where he died before 5th July 1948 (when the National Insurance Act 1946 (c. 67) came into operation).

items.title: The Royal Irish Constabulary (Lump Sum Payments to Widows) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide for the payment of an additional £10 in the case of a widow in receipt of an allowance or pension under the Royal Irish Constabulary (Widows' Pensions) Regulations 1971 equivalent to the Christmas bonus for pensioners payable under Schedule 6 to the Social Security Act 1986 if she is not entitled to a payment by virtue of that Schedule.

items.title: The Legal Aid (General) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Legal Aid (General) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Precept Limitation (Prescribed Maximum) (West Yorkshire Passenger Transport Authority) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes a maximum precept of 24.82 pence in the pound for the West Yorkshire Passenger Transport Authority for the financial year beginning on 1st April 1988.

items.title: The Southampton (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a competent harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the Authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Port of Heysham (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7 (5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a competent harbour authority for the purposes of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the Authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Falmouth (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy Apparatus (Approval) (Test Fees) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for the charging of fees for tests of wireless telegraphy apparatus to MPT Performance Specifications conducted on behalf of the Secretary of State for Trade and Industry for the purposes of determining applications for type approval under section 84 of the Telecommunications Act 1984.

items.title: The Diseases of Fish (Definition of Infected) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the list of diseases to which the provisions of the Diseases of Fish Act 1937, as amended, apply.

items.title: The National Health Service (Payments for Optical Appliances) (Scotland) Amendment (No.4) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Payments for Optical Appliances) (Scotland) Regulations 1986 which provide for payments to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of the cost of optical appliances supplied to certain categories of persons following a sight test under the National Health Service in Scotland.

items.title: The Rate Support Grant (Specified Bodies) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 2(7) of the Local Government Act 1974 and section 56(9) of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 empower the Secretary of State to defray expenditure incurred in the provision of services for local authorities by any body specified by him in regulations.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Miscellaneous Provision) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision consequential on the abolition of the Greater London Council and metropolitan county councils by section 1 of the Local Government Act 1985.

items.title: The National Health Service (Superannuation) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Service (Superannuation) (Scotland) Regulations 1980 (

items.title: The Friendly Societies (Valuation) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Friendly Societies (Valuation) Regulations 1985.

items.title: The County Council of the Royal County of Berkshire A329(M) Relief Road Special Road Variation Scheme 1987 Confirmation Instrument 1988
The Financial Services Act 1986 (Commencement) (No. 10) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Financial Services Act 1986 (Commencement) (No. 10) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Financial Services (Schemes Authorised in Designated Countries or Territories) (Notification) Regulations 1988
items.description: These regulations prescribe the period of two months as the period within which the Securities and Investments Board may notify the operator of a scheme which is managed in and authorised under the law of a country or territory designated under section 87 of the Financial Services Act 1986 that the scheme is not to be recognised.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest Relief) (Qualifying Lenders) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: By article 2, the bodies named in article 3 are prescribed as qualifying lenders so that interest on qualifying loans made by them may be paid to them under deduction of tax under the tax deduction scheme (introduced by the Finance Act 1982 (c. 39)) from the dates specified in the Order.

items.title: The Local Government (Non-Domestic District Rates and District Community Charges) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to how there is to be ascertained the amount which a regional council is liable to pay to a district council in respect of the non-domestic district rate and the district community charges for the financial year 1989-90 and each subsequent financial year (regulations 3 to 5 and 7 to 16).

items.title: The Insurance Brokers Registration Council (Registration and Enrolment) (Amendment) Rules Approval Order 1988
items.description: This Order approves Rules made by the Insurance Brokers Registration Council in exercise of their powers under section 8(2) of the Insurance Brokers (Registration) Act 1977.

items.title: The Merger Reference (Elders IXL Limited and Scottish & Newcastle Breweries plc) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prohibits Elders IXL Limited and its subsidiaries from acquiring, directly or indirectly, shares or interests in shares of Scottish & Newcastle Breweries plc or any of its subsidiaries if, after the acquisition, they and their associates would have more than 15% of any class of the shares; but this does not apply to existing acquisitions. They are also prohibited from exercising more than 15% of voting rights. The proposed merger of Elders and Scottish & Newcastle has been referred to the Monopolies and Mergers Commission.

items.title: The County of Gwent (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The County of Gwent (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Liverpool and Wirral Urban Development Area Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates three areas totalling about 610 hectares in the city of Liverpool and the borough of Wirral and in the vicinity of the Merseyside urban development area, as an urban development area. It provides for the functions of an urban development corporation to be performed in the designated area by the Merseyside Development Corporation. The three areas are shown hatched black on the map forming part of this note.

items.title: The Merseyside Development Corporation (Planning Functions) (Liverpool and Wirral) Order 1988
items.description: The Liverpool and Wirral Urban Development Area Order 1988 designates the Liverpool and Wirral urban development area and provides for the functions of an urban development corporation for the area to be performed by the Merseyside Development

items.title: The Data Protection (Fees) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase from £40 to £56 the fee which is to accompany an application to the Data Protection Registrar for registration or for renewal of registration under the Data Protection Act 1984.

items.title: The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Woodfarm Camp Main) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Woodfarm Camp Main) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Family Credit (General) Amendment No. 4 Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) Amendment No. 4 Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The A40 Trunk Road (Western Avenue, Ealing) (Prohibition of Left Turn) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A40 Trunk Road (Western Avenue, Ealing) (Prohibition of Left Turn) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Mersey Docks and Harbour Revision (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends for one year the time for the completion of the construction of a deep water river berth in the river Mersey to the south of Seacombe Landing Stage adjacent to the Morpeth and Wallasey Docks authorised by section 4 of the Mersey Docks and Harbour Board (Ore Berth) Act 1971 (as extended).

items.title: The Safety of Sports Grounds (Designation) Order 1988
items.description: Article 2 of this Order designates the sports grounds specified therein as sports grounds requiring safety certificates under the Safety of Sports Grounds Act 1975. That Act was amended by Schedule 2 to the Fire Safety and Safety of Places of Sport Act 1987 (which was brought into force on 1st January 1988 by S.I. 1987/1762) so that references to sports grounds were substituted for the references to sports stadia.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Small Claim Rules) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes rules of procedure in the sheriff court for small claims.

items.title: The Non-Domestic District Rates (Timetable) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe, in relation to the financial year 1989-90 and each subsequent financial year, 15th December in the immediately preceding financial year as the date before which a regional council is required to notify a district council of its estimate of the amount which it is liable to pay to the district council in respect of the non-domestic district rate for the following financial year.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Amendment of Sheriff Court Ordinary Cause, and Summary Cause, Rules) 1988
items.description: This version of the Act of Sederunt (Amendment of Sheriff Court Ordinary Cause, and Summary Cause, Rules) 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Legal Officers (Annual Fees) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the fees fixed by The Legal Officers (Annual Fees) Order 1987 and adjusts some of the fees to take account of increases or decreases in the number of parishes within particular dioceses. It also amends the provisions in the 1987 Order as to the scope of diocesan registrars' annual fees to take account of new duties under the Patronage (Benefices) Measure 1986, and clarifies the provisions as regards supplementary payments made by agreement by diocesan boards of finance.

items.title: The Anatomy (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Anatomy Regulations 1988 so that the requirement in those Regulations to keep records relating to anatomical examinations and anatomical specimens in a particular place and the requirement to keep those records and records relating to parts of bodies at all times available for inspection by an inspector are omitted.

items.title: Patronage (Appeals) Rules 1988
items.description: This version of the Patronage (Appeals) Rules 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Grant-maintained Schools) (Termination of Power to Determine a Period of Suspension) Order 1988
items.description: Section 73 of the Education Reform Act 1988 confers power on the Secretary of State to suspend the consideration and determination of statutory proposals published under section 12 or 13 of the Education Act 1980 (c. 20) in relation to the establishment, discontinuance and alteration of a school so as to allow time for the publication of proposals for acquisition of grant-maintained status for the school. By virtue of subsection (11) of section 73 of the 1988 Act that power does not apply in relation to proposals under section 12 or 13 of the 1980 Act published on or after a date appointed for the purposes of section 73 by order made by the Secretary of State. This Order appoints 31st December 1988 for the purposes of that section.

items.title: The Agriculture Improvement (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Agriculture Improvement Regulations 1985 (“the principal Regulations”), implementing in part the provisions of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (OJ No. L93, 30.3.85, p.1) on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1760/87 (OJ No. L167, 26.6.87, p.1), and Council Directive No. 75/268/EEC (OJ No. L128, 19.5.75, p.1) on mountain and hill farming and farming in certain less-favoured areas.

items.title: The Agriculture Improvement (Variation) ( No. 2) Scheme 1988 (revoked)
items.description: This Scheme further varies the Agriculture Improvement Scheme 1985 (“the principal Scheme”) and complies with Article 8 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (OJ No. L93, 30.3.85, p.1) on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1760/87 (OJ No. L167, 26.6.87, p.1), and Council Directive No. 75/268/EEC (OJ No. L128, 19.5.75, p.1) on mountain and hill farming and farming in certain less-favoured areas.

items.title: The Transfer of Functions (Government Shareholding in British Airways Plc) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers to the Treasury, so as to be exercisable concurrently with the Secretary of State, his function of holding and disposing of the shares in and securities of British Airways Plc and associated functions in relation to those shares and securities under the Civil Aviation Act 1980.

items.title: The Parliamentary Commissioner (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of paragraph 2 of Schedule 3 to the Parliamentary Commissioner Act 1967, as amended by the Parliamentary Commissioner Order 1979, the jurisdiction of the Parliamentary Commissioner for Administration extends to action taken in the exercise of a consular function by a career consular officer on condition that the action complained of was taken in relation to a United Kingdom citizen with the right of abode in the United Kingdom. This Order removes this condition, thereby extending the jurisdiction of the Parliamentary Commissioner to the generality of actions taken in the exercise of consular functions by such officers.

items.title: The Consular Fees (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Consular Fees Order 1987 so as to prescribe a fee for the replacement of an existing passport in order to amend the holder’s name (fee 18), to add a fresh photograph or to amend the holder’s description (fee 19). The Order also introduces a new fee for receiving in the United Kingdom an application made in the United Kingdom for a certificate of entitlement to the right of abode (fee 22A).

items.title: The Precept Limitation (Prescribed Maximum) (Inner London Education Authority) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prescribes a maximum precept of 81.8 pence in the pound for the Inner London Education Authority for the financial year beginning on 1st April 1988.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Certification of Deck Officers and Marine Engineer Officers) (Guernsey) Order 1988
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Merchant Shipping (Certification of Deck Officers and Marine Engineer Officers) (Guernsey) Order 1981. Subject to the exceptions, adaptations and modifications specified in the Schedule thereto, the Order extends to the Bailiwick of Guernsey as part of its law, and applies to ships registered there and to masters and seamen employed in them, the Merchant Shipping (Certification of Deck Officers) Regulations 1985 and the Merchant Shipping (Certification of Marine Engineer Officers and Licensing of Marine Engine Operators) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Parliamentary Constituencies (Scotland) (Miscellaneous Changes) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect withOut modification to the recommendations contained in the report of the Boundary Commission for Scotland dated 24th June 1988. The report contains proposals for changes to the areas of certain parliamentary constituencies where local government area changes have resulted in inconsistencies between local government boundaries and the boundaries of parliamentary constituencies. Articles 2 to 9 of, and the Schedule to, this Order set out and describe the new constituencies, the boundaries of which will be consistent with the revised local government boundaries at 24th June 1988.

items.title: The Sheriff Courts (Scotland) Act 1971 (Privative Jurisdiction and Summary Cause) Order 1988
items.description: This Order raises the limit of the privative (or exclusive) jurisdiction of the sheriff court and the limit of the summary cause from £500 and £1,000 respectively to £1,500. The new limits do not apply to proceedings commenced before the Order comes into force.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Aeroplane and Aeroplane Engine Emission of Unburned Hydrocarbons) Order 1988
items.description: The Order applies to subsonic and supersonic aeroplanes, wherever registered, which are propelled by turbojet or turbofan engines manufactured after 1st January 1989 and which in the case of subsonic aeroplanes are powered by such engines which have a rated output greater than 26.7 kN. It prohibits such aeroplanes from landing or taking-off in the United Kingdom unless their engines are of a type which has been certified as complying with the requirements for the emission of unburned hydrocarbons (article 4).

items.title: Payments to Redundant Churches Fund Order 1988
items.description: This version of the Payments to Redundant Churches Fund Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Benefices (Institution Appeals) Rules 1988
items.description: This version of the Benefices (Institution Appeals) Rules 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Small Claims (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision in connection with the establishing of a small claims procedure.

items.title: The Highland Regional Council (Abhainn Shlatach) Water Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Highland Regional Council (Abhainn Shlatach) Water Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Noise Insulation (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These regulations amend the Noise Insulation Regulations 1975.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Ships' Names) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (c. 12) excluded fishing vessels from registry under Part I of the Merchant Shipping Act 1894 and set up a separate register for fishing vessels under Part II of the 1988 Act.

items.title: The Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 30th November 1988 sections 214 to 216 of the Education Reform Act 1988. These provisions relate to the new offence of granting awards which may be taken to be degrees of U.K. institutions where the award is not a recognised award for the purposes of those provisions.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Fishing Vessels' Names) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations require persons intending to apply for the registration of a fishing vessel under Part II of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988 (c. l2) to obtain the approval of the Registrar General and Shipping and Seamen to the proposed name. The Registrar General may refuse approval if there is already a vessel of that name registered at the intended port of registry or if the name is so similar to another vessel already registered at that port as to be likely to deceive. Approval is to last for 3 months.

items.title: The Petty Sessional Divisions (Berkshire) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect to a draft order submitted by the magistrates' courts committee for the county of Berkshire and provides for the petty sessional divisions of Bradfield and Sonning and Reading to be combined to form a new petty sessional division of Reading and Sonning.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Proceedings in the Sheriff Court under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987) 1988
items.description: This version of the Act of Sederunt (Proceedings in the Sheriff Court under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987) 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Scotland) (Prospective Cost) (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Advice and Assistance (Scotland) (Prospective Cost) (No. 3) Regulations 1988 provide that in certain specified cases solicitors may, without obtaining the prior approval of the Scottish Legal Aid Board, exceed the limit of £60 prescribed for legal advice and assistance and assistance by way of representation. These Regulations provide that, when that limit is thus exceeded, the solicitor must notify the Board within 14 days. Failure so to notify shall render that part of the solicitor’s account in excess of £60 ineligible for payment, unless the Board considers that there is special reason for it to excuse such failure.

items.title: The Financial Services (Designated Countries and Territories) (Overseas Collective Investment Schemes) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the Isle of Man for the purposes of section 87 of the Financial Services Act 1986 in respect of schemes authorised in and managed under the law of the Isle of Man which are of a class falling within the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Order 1983 (the principal Order) which specifies descriptions and classes of prescription only medicines, that is to say medicinal products which (subject to exemptions) may be sold or supplied by retail only in accordance with a prescription given by an appropriate practitioner and which may be administered only by or in accordance with the directions of such a practitioner.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies (Approved Bodies)(No. 4) Order 1988
items.description: Assured tenancies are tenancies which, but for section 56 of the Housing Act 1980, would have been protected tenancies or housing association tenancies under the Rent Act 1977 (c. 42), and which meet certain other conditions. Such tenancies are subject to Part II of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954 (c. 56), as modified by Schedule 5 to the 1980 Act. They can only be granted by bodies approved under section 56 of the 1980 Act.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Offensive Weapons) Order 1988
items.description: Section 141 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 provides that any person who manufactures, sells or hires or offers for sale or hire, exposes or has in his possession for the purpose of sale or hire, or lends or gives to any other person, a weapon to which that section applies shall be guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to a fine not exceeding level 5 on the standard scale or both. The importation of any such weapon is prohibited.

items.title: The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 5 Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 5 Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Conservation of Seals (Common Seals) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prohibits for two years with effect from the 19th December 1988 the killing, injuring or taking of common seals in Great Britain and adjacent territorial waters. It also revokes an earlier Order which had prohibited the killing, injuring or taking of common seals only in the County of Zetland and adjacent territorial waters and which is made redundant by this Order.

items.title: The Conservation of Seals (England and Wales) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prohibits for two years with effect from 19th December 1988 the killing, injuring or taking of grey seals in England and Wales and adjacent territorial waters.

items.title: The Education (Listed Bodies) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Education (Listed Bodies) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Education (Recognised Awards) Order 1988
items.description: This Order lists particular awards granted by certain persons which are recognised awards for the purposes of section 214 of the Education Reform Act 1988. As a recognised award these particular awards are not awards of the kind referred to in section 214(1), which makes it an offence to grant, offer to grant, or issue any invitation relating to certain unrecognised degrees and awards.

items.title: The Education (Recognised Bodies) Order 1988
items.description: This Order lists all those bodies which appear to the Secretary of State to be recognised bodies within section 214(2)(a) or (b) of the Education Reform Act 1988. An award granted by such a body is not an award of a kind referred to in section 214(1), which makes it an offence to grant, offer to grant, or issue any invitation relating to certain unrecognised degrees and awards.

items.title: The Education Support Grants (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Education Support Grants Regulations 1984 by substituting a new regulation 4 (rate of grant) and a new Schedule listing the purposes for or in connection with which grants are payable.

items.title: The Housing (Scotland) Act 1988 Commencement Order 1988
items.description: This Order appoints the days on which the provisions of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988 (“the Act”) are to come into force.

items.title: The Weights and Measures (Intoxicating Liquor) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Weights and Measures (Intoxicating Liquor) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The West of Southampton—Bath Trunk Road A36 (Codford Bypass) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The West of Southampton—Bath Trunk Road A36 (Codford Bypass) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Weights and Measures (Miscellaneous Foods) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Weights and Measures (Miscellaneous Foods) Order 1988Approved by both Houses of ParliamentJ is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Consumer Protection Act 1987 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 31st December 1988 the provisions of the Consumer Protection Act 1987 under which the Trade Descriptions Act 1972 (which required certain names and marks applied to imported goods to be accompanied by an indication of origin) is repealed.

items.title: The Employment Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings section 8 of the Employment Act 1988 into force on 5th December 1988. The section makes it unlawful for union property to be applied towards making any payment to any individual or indemnifying him in respect of a penalty imposed for an offence or for contempt of court.

items.title: The Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) (Approval of Fitters and Workshops) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Passenger and Goods Vehicles (Recording Equipment) (Approval of Fitters and Workshops) (Fees) Regulations 1986 to increase the fees for the approval of fitters or workshops for the installation or repair of recording equipment (Tachographs).

items.title: North Devon Link Road (Little Gornhay Junction Slip Roads) (Trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the North Devon Link Road (Little Gornhay Junction Slip Roads) (Trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A1079 Trunk Road (Hull City Boundary to Barmston Drain) (De-Trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A1079 Trunk Road (Hull City Boundary to Barmston Drain) (De-Trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Shoreham Port Authority Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: This Order contains provisions for—

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Agreements and Cancellation Notices and Copies of Documents) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend The Consumer Credit (Cancellation Notices and Copies of Documents) Regulations 1983 by substituting, for the forms numbered 2 to 8 inclusive, 10 to 14 inclusive and 18 to 22 inclusive set out in the Schedule thereto, revised forms numbered correspondingly. The effect of these revisions is threefold. They allow the omission of any reference to (i) goods given in part-exchange, (ii) property given as security, or (iii) goods in the possession of the debtor or hirer by virtue of the agreement, where any of these would be inappropriate. Second, they allow a notice to be inserted in appropriate cases warning the debtor or hirer that any attempt by him to disconnect and return the goods personally would be dangerous and could contravene Health and Safety legislation, in substitution for the words of express permission to return the goods in his possession. Third, they allow the inclusion of a cross-reference to the place where the debtor or hirer will find set out his financial obligations arising under any linked transaction which is exempted from the effect of a cancellation notice by virtue of regulation 2(1)(b) of the Consumer Credit (Linked Transactions) (Exemptions) Regulations 1983 (S.I. 1983/1560).

items.title: The West of Southampton—Bath Trunk Road A36 (Codford Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The West of Southampton—Bath Trunk Road A36 (Codford Bypass) (Detrunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Distress for Rent Rules 1988
items.description: This version of the The Distress for Rent Rules 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Customs Duties (ECSC) (Amendment No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 25th November 1988, reimposes duties on certain categories of iron or steel products originating in Yugoslavia.

items.title: The Housing Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st December 1988 the provisions of the Housing Act 1988 which relate to the establishment of Housing for Wales.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Designated Rural Areas and Designated Region) (England) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Housing (Right to Buy) (Designated Rural Areas and Designated Region) (England) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Merger Reference (Strong & Fisher (Holdings) plc and Pittard Garnar plc) Order 1988
items.description: Strong & Fisher (Holdings) plc, its subsidiaries and its associates are prohibited by this Order from acquiring any shares or interests in shares in Pittard Garnar plc if together they would have 15% or more of any class of voting shares, from exercising 15% or more of voting rights and from making agreements with others to secure control of Pittard Garnar.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Form of charge for payment) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for the form of charge for payment of money to be used by a messenger-at-arms or a sheriff officer under section 90 of the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.5) (Time to pay directions) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session to make provision in the rules in relation to time to pay directions under section 1 of the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Seamen’s Wages and Accounts) (Fishing Vessels) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend The Merchant Shipping (Seamen’s Wages and Accounts) (Fishing Vessels) Regulations 1972 so as to increase from £50 to £200 the maximum contribution that an employer can recover from a fisherman who is in breach of his contractual obligations.

items.title: The Agriculture Improvement (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Agriculture Improvement Regulations 1985 (“the principal Regulations”), implementing in part the provisions of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (OJ No. L93, 30.3.85, p.1) on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1760/87 (OJ No. L167, 26.6.87, p.1) and Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1137/88 (OJ No. L108, 29.4.88, p.1), and Council Directive No. 75/268/EEC (OJ No. L128, 19.5.75, p.1) on mountain and hill farming and farming in certain less-favoured areas.

items.title: The Agriculture Improvement (Variation) (No. 3) Scheme 1988
items.description: This Scheme further varies the Agriculture Improvement Scheme 1985 (“the principal Scheme”) and complies with Article 8 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 797/85 (OJ No. L93, 30.3.85, p. 1) on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures, as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1760/87 (OJ No. L167, 26.6.87, p. 1).

items.title: The Assured Tenancies (Notices to Quit Prescribed Information) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the information to be contained in a Notice to Quit given by a landlord to terminate a tenancy which is an assured tenancy under the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988. Failure to include such information will in terms of section 112 of the Rent (Scotland) Act 1984 render the Notice to Quit invalid.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies (Exceptions) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 12(2) of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988 provides that if and so long as a tenancy falls within any paragraph of Schedule 4 to the Act, it cannot be an assured tenancy. Paragraph 7(1) of that Schedule provides that a tenancy which is granted to a person who is pursuing or intends to pursue a course of study provided by a specified educational institution and is so granted by that institution or by another specified institution or body of persons is such a tenancy.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies (Tenancies at a Low Rent) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: Section 12(2) of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988 provides that if and so long as a tenancy falls within any paragraph of Schedule 4 to the Act, it cannot be an assured tenancy.
