Paragraph 2(1) of that Schedule provides that a tenancy under which either no rent is payable or the rent payable is less than that specified by order is such a tenancy.

items.title: The Public Lending Right Scheme 1982 (Commencement of Variations) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 22nd December 1988 variations to the Public Lending Right Scheme 1982.

items.title: The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) (Revocation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order revokes the Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1988 as varied, which prohibited fishing for cod (Gadus morhua) by any British fishing boat registered in the United Kingdom, the Isle of Man or the Channel Islands within any part of a sea area specified in that Order with effect from 11th April 1988.

items.title: The Employment Appeal Tribunal (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Employment Appeal Tribunal Rules 1980, as amended by the Employment Appeal Tribunal (Amendment) Rules 1985, principally to make provision for the instituting of, and the entering of appearances to, applications for awards of compensation and orders under section 5 of the Employment Act 1988 (remedies for infringement of the right not to be unjustifiably disciplined) and for the registration and proof of any such awards and orders. The Rules also increase from three to five days the time allowed for giving notice of an interlocutory appeal from a decision of the Registrar, prescribe an amended version of Form 1 (Notice of appeal from decision of Industrial Tribunal) and make two minor corrections to the 1980 Rules.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Commencement No.4) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 5th January 1989 section 32, in part, sections 50, 60, 61, 62, 118, 120, 130, 152, 153 and 154 and section 170, in part, of the Criminal Justice Act 1988, and associated amendments and repeals in Schedules 15 and 16 to that Act. These provisions make miscellaneous changes to the law on criminal procedure and evidence. In relation to trial on indictment, they allow for the discretionary deferral of jury service, abolish the right of peremptory challenge of jurors, and enable evidence to be given by live television link by witnesses under the age of 14 in trials for certain offences. They also amend the law relating to bail applications and remands, computation of sentence where time has been spent in local authority care, and fine enforcement, and provide for the application of the law on suspended and partly suspended sentences in courts-martial and Standing Civilian Courts.

items.title: The Education (Amendment of the Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1987) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends section 3(5)(a) of the Teachers' Pay and Conditions Act 1987 to enable any Order made under that section to provide for any discretion conferred on a local education authority by virtue of that paragraph to be exercisable instead by the governing body of any school to which section 44 or 45 of the Education Reform Act 1988 (staff employed in schools with a delegated budget) for the time being applies.

items.title: The Consumer Protection Act 1987 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1 March 1989 those provisions of the Consumer Protection Act 1987 which are not already in force save that transitional provision is made in article 3 for the application of section 20(1) and (2) of the Act (which relate to misleading price indications) to indications given in certain catalogues, reference works, periodicals, direct mail advertisements, circulars, price lists, films, cable programmes and radio and television broadcasts.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies the fees payable for the year beginning 1st April 1989 under the Acts relating to the registration of births, deaths and marriages and associated matters.

items.title: The Consumer Protection (Code of Practice for Traders on Price Indications) Approval Order 1988
items.description: Section 25 of the Consumer Protection Act 1987 provides for the approval by the Secretary of State of a code of practice issued for the purpose of giving practical guidance with respect to the requirements of section 20 of the Act (which is concerned with misleading price indications) and promoting desirable practices in relation to the giving of price indications. A contravention of an approved code does not of itself give rise to civil or criminal liability but Section 25 of the Act provides that in proceedings for an offence under section 20(1) or (2) a contravention of a provision in an approved code may be relied on in relation to any matter for the purpose of showing that the person committed the offence or of negativing any defence and compliance with a provision in an approved code may be relied on in relation to any matter for the purpose of showing that the commission of the offence has not been established or that the person concerned has a defence.

items.title: The Price Marking (Bargain Offers) (Revocation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order revokes the Price Marking (Bargain Offers) Order 1979, as amended, which, except in the cases set out in that Order, prohibits the indication of a price for the retail sale of goods or a charge for the provision of services to non-business users which includes an express or an implied statement that the price or charge is lower than another price or charge for, or a value ascribed to, goods or services of the same description.

items.title: The Housing Revenue Account General Fund Contribution Limits (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order limits the amount of contribution from their general fund to the housing revenue account which local authorities may include in their estimates for the year 1989-90

items.title: The Car Tax (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 1st December 1988 amend regulation 27 of the Car Tax Regulations 1985.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 1st December 1988 amend regulation 66 of the Value Added Tax (General) Regulations 1985.

items.title: The Sweeteners in Food (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only, amend the Sweeteners in Food (Scotland) Regulations 1983 by adding Lactitol to the list of permitted sweeteners (regulation 3(a)). Specific purity criteria are prescribed for Lactitol (regulation 3(b)).

items.title: The Assured Tenancies (Rent Book) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 30(4) provides that where under an assured tenancy rent is payable weekly, it shall be the duty of the landlord to provide a rent book. Section 30(5) provides that a rent book shall contain such notices which shall be in such form and shall relate to such matters as may be prescribed and otherwise shall comply with such requirements as may be prescribed.

items.title: The Landlord’s Repairing Obligations (Specified Rent) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: Paragraph 1 of Schedule 10 to the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 as amended by paragraph 9 of Schedule 8 to the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988 provides that in any contract to which the paragraph applies there shall, notwithstanding any stipulations to the contrary, be implied a condition that the house is at the commencement of the tenancy and an undertaking that the house will be kept by the landlord during the tenancy in all respects reasonably fit for human habitation.

items.title: The Designs (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules substitute a new rule 51 and introduce a new rule 51A in the Designs Rules 1984. The new rules relate to certificates and copies supplied by the Registrar of Designs. The amendments are consequential upon the coming into force on 1st January 1989 of the remaining provisions of the Patents, Designs and Marks Act 1986. Amendments consequential on the amendments to the Designs Rules are made to the Registered Designs (Fees) Rules 1988. A fee is introduced for the supply by the Registrar of uncertified copies of entries in or extracts from the Register of Designs and of copies of and extracts from documents kept in the Patent Office. Form Designs No.23 is revised.

items.title: The Patents (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules substitute in the Patents Rules 1982 a new rule 52 for the present rules 52 and 93(3). The new rule 52 relates to certificates and copies supplied by the Comptroller-General of Patents, Designs and Trade Marks. The amendments are consequential on the coming into force on 1st January 1989 of the remaining provisions of the Patents, Designs and Marks Act 1986. Patents Forms Nos. 23/77 and 24/77 are revised.

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Reciprocal Exemption of European Radio Amateurs) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations permit radio amateurs from certain other European countries who are visiting the United Kingdom (including the Isle of Man and the Channel Islands) to use certain amateur radio stations without obtaining a licence under section 1 of the Wireless Telegraphy Act 1949.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning General Development (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning General Development Order 1988.

items.title: The Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase from 9¼ per cent. to 11⅞ per cent. per annum, in respect of any period after the coming into force of these Regulations, the rate of interest payable where entry is made, before payment of compensation, on land in England and Wales which is being purchased compulsorily, and revoke the Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase from 9¼ per cent. to 11⅞ per cent. per annum, in respect of any period after the coming into force of these Regulations, the rate of interest payable where entry is made, before payment of compensation, on land in Scotland which is being purchased compulsorily, and revoke the Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (Scotland) Regulations 1988.

items.title: The Opencast Coal (Rate of Interest on Compensation) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: Section 35 of the Opencast Coal Act 1958 provides that interest shall be payable in addition to compensation in certain circumstances. This Order increases the rate of interest from 9¼ per cent. to 11⅞ per cent. per annum and revokes the Opencast Coal (Rate of Interest on Compensation) Order 1988.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Messengers-at-Arms and Sheriff Officers Rules) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes provision for rules for messengers-at-arms and sheriff officers under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987.

items.title: The Veterinary Surgeons and Veterinary Practitioners (Registration)(Amendment) Regulations Order of Council 1988
items.description: The Regulations approved by this Order further amend the Veterinary Surgeons and Veterinary Practitioners Registration Regulations 1967 to take account of the position, under the 1988 Agreement with the Republic of Ireland (scheduled to S.I. 1988/784), of veterinary surgeons residing in the Republic, and to remove provisions rendered obsolete by professional mobility within the European Economic Community.

items.title: The Highland Regional Council (Loch an t-Seana Bhaile) (Amendment) Water Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Highland Regional Council (Loch an t-Seana Bhaile) (Amendment) Water Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Lands Tribunal for Scotland (Amendment) (Fees) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Lands Tribunal for Scotland Rules 1971 for the purpose of increasing the fees payable to the Tribunal.

items.title: The Local Statutory Provisions (Postponement of Repeal) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: Section 225(6) of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1973 as amended provides that all local statutory provisions to which the subsection applies shall cease to have effect at the end of 1984. Previous Orders (S.I. 1984/1926, 1985/1629. 1986/2034 and 1987/2090) have postponed until the end of 1988 the date on which such local statutory provisions applying to the local government areas or parts thereof listed in the Schedules to those Orders were to cease to have effect. This Order further postpones that date until the end of 1989 in respect of those local government areas listed in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Health and Medicines Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st January 1989 those provisions of the Health and Medicines Act 1988 which remove the prohibition on charges for clinical examinations as part of general dental services in the National Health Service in Great Britain and reports on those examinations.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (General) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 1st January 1989 amend and consolidate various provisions of the Value Added Tax (General) Regulations 1985. In the main they reflect amendments to Schedule 1 of the Value Added Tax Act 1983 introduced by section 14 of the Finance Act 1988. However, there is a substantive amendment to regulation 46.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies (Forms) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the forms which are to be used in connection with assured tenancies and short assured tenancies under the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988 in the cases where these forms are applicable.

items.title: The Butter (EEC Special Sale) (Revocation) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Butter (EEC Special Sale) Regulations 1986 as amended by the Butter (EEC Special Sale) (Amendment) Regulations 1986 which prescribed, in addition to requirements as to packaging, maximum retail prices for concentrated butter (in the form of butter oil or butter ghee) produced from butter sold out of intervention stocks at a reduced price, pursuant to Article 8 of Commission Regulation (EEC) No 3143/85 (OJ No. L298, 12.11.85, p.9). Article 8 was deleted by Commission Regulation (EEC) No. 3157/86 (OJ No. L294, 17.10.86, p.8).

items.title: The Spring Traps Approval (Variation) Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 8 of the Pests Act 1954 it is an offence to use in England and Wales for the purposes of killing or taking animals a spring trap other than one approved by an Order of the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food and the Secretary of State for Wales.

items.title: The Sweeteners in Food (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 31st December 1988, amend the Sweeteners in Food Regulations 1983 by adding Lactitol to the list of permitted sweeteners (regulation 3(a)). Specific purity criteria are prescribed for Lactitol (regulation 3(b)).

items.title: The Medicines (Pharmacies) (Applications for Registration and Fees) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Medicines (Pharmacies) (Applications for Registration and Fees) Regulations 1973. They increase the fees for registration of premises at which a retail pharmacy business is or is to be carried on and the subsequent annual fees (retention fees).

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property, etc.) (Greater Manchester) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect, with a modification, to proposals of the Greater Manchester Residuary Body for the transfer of certain functions, property, rights and liabilities. The effect of the modification is that the monies referred to in article 3(2) may be used only for the purpose there stated and not to defray expenditure referred to in article 3(1) of the Local Government Reorganisation (Pensions etc.) (Greater Manchester and Merseyside) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/1579).

items.title: The Birmingham—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A47) (Eye Bypass) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Birmingham—Great Yarmouth Trunk Road (A47) (Eye Bypass) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Funds for Trade Union Ballots (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the scheme under which the Certification Officer makes payments to independent trade unions in respect of postal, printing and stationery expenses which they have incurred in holding secret postal ballots for certain purposes.

items.title: The Trade Union Ballots and Elections (Independent Scrutineer Qualifications) Order 1988
items.description: Section 15 of the Employment Act 1988 requires certain ballots and elections held by trade unions to be supervised by a qualified independent person (known as a “scrutineer”) appoin- ted by the trade union. This Order specifies the conditions which a person must meet to be eligible for appointment as a scrutineer and specifies three bodies by name as being eligible for appointment as scrutineers.

items.title: The Occupational Pensions (Revaluation) Order 1988
items.description: Section 52A of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (inserted by section 2 of, and Schedule 1 to, the Social Security Act 1985) defines the terms “revaluation period” and “revaluation year” and requires the Secretary of State to specify by Order, in each revaluation year, a “revaluation percentage” for each revaluation period.

items.title: The Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions, contained in the Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Regulations 1983, for the disregard of certain pension payments and for the calculation of the weekly amount of pension payments made other than weekly.

items.title: The Weighing Equipment (Non-automatic Weighing Machines) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Weighing Equipment (Non-automatic Weighing Machines) (Amendment) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Cosmetic Products (Safety) (Amendment No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 1984 (“the principal Regulations”) and implement Commission Directive No. 88/233/EEC (O.J. No. L105, 26.4.88, p. 11) on cosmetic products.

items.title: The Three-Wheeled All-Terrain Motor Vehicles (Safety)Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prohibit the supply of any vehicle which has three wheels, is propelled by an internal combustion engine and is designed or constructed primarily for use in leisure activities on all types of land surface or all types of land surface other than highways or roads to which the public has access except for vehicles for disabled persons and any two-wheeled motor cycle with a side-car. The Regulations come into force on the 7th December 1988 for a period of twelve months.

items.title: The Mersey Docks and Harbour (Princes River Berth) Revision Order 1988
items.description: This Order empowers The Mersey Docks and Harbour Company to construct in the City of Liverpool an in-river berth comprising an adjustable bridge, a line of mooring fenders and an access gangway for foot passengers, and to carry out subsidiary works.

items.title: The (A43) Oxford—Market Deeping Trunk Road (Blisworth and Milton Malsor Bypass and Slip Roads to Rothersthorpe Service Area) Order 1988
The (A43) Oxford—Market Deeping Trunk Road(Blisworth and Milton Malsor Bypass and Slip Roadsto Rothersthorpe Service Area) Detrunking Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A43) Oxford—Market Deeping Trunk Road(Blisworth and Milton Malsor Bypass and Slip Roadsto Rothersthorpe Service Area) Detrunking Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The M1 Motorway(Rothersthorpe Service Area Connecting Roads)Scheme 1988
items.description: This version of the The M1 Motorway(Rothersthorpe Service Area Connecting Roads)Scheme 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences, Qualifications and Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Goods Vehicles (Operators' Licences, Qualifications and Fees) Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Education (NAB Staff) Order 1988
items.description: This order designates certain staff of the National Advisory Body for Public Sector Higher Education as staff who shall, on 31st December 1988, transfer to the employment of the Polytechnics and Colleges Funding Council.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) (Fifth Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) Regulations 1984 as follows:—

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) (No. 4) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend Rule 25 of the Crown Court Rules 1982, which makes provision for an appeal against a refusal of the appropriate officer of the Crown Court to excuse a person from jury service. The amendment enables a person to appeal against a refusal of the appropriate officer to defer his attendance for jury service under section 9A(1) of the Juries act 1974 as inserted by section 120 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Criminal Justice Act 1988) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts Rules 1981, the Magistrates' Courts (Forms) Rules 1981, the Magistrates' Courts (Children and Young Persons) Rules 1988 and the Magistrates' Courts (Attendance Centre) Rules 1958, pursuant to certain provisions of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (the 1988 Act).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Dangerous Goods) (Second Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Air Navigation (Dangerous Goods) Regulations 1985. In addition to some minor and drafting amendments the following changes are made:1The definition of “Technical Instructions” now refers to the 1989—90 English language edition, approved and published by decision of the Council of the International Civil Aviation Organization (regulation 2(1)).2Regulation 4(2) of the Regulations is amended such that it now applies to any dangerous goods which a person knows or ought to know or suspect to be goods capable of posing significant risk to health, safety or property when carried by air (regulation 2(2)).3The notices required under regulation 9(2) of the Regulations must be displayed at each of the places at an airport specified and must be sufficient in number and prominence for the purpose specified (regulation 2(8)).

items.title: The Agricultural Levies (Export Control) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace the Agricultural Levies (Export Control) Regulations 1983 (“the 1983 Regulations”), which are revoked. They are made in implementation of the obligation to protect funds of the European Agricultural Guidance and Guarantee Fund contained in article 8 of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 729/70 (OJ No. L94, 28.4.70, p.13; OJ/SE 1970(I) p.218) on the financing of the common agricultural policy.

items.title: The Medicines (Exemptions from Restrictions on the Retail Sale or Supply of Veterinary Drugs) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Exemptions from Restrictions on the Retail Sale or Supply of Veterinary Drugs) Order 1985 (“the 1985 Order”) which provides for exemptions from the restrictions imposed by section 52 of the Medicines Act 1968.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 2 Order 1984) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 2) Order 1984 by redefining the centre of the site of an historic wreck in Poole Bay near Poole, Dorset and by increasing the restricted area to one of 75 (instead of 50) metres of site. The amendment is made in order to include in the restricted area an additional part of the wreck recently discovered just outside the one originally designated. No part of the restricted area lies above high water mark of ordinary spring tides.

items.title: The Protection of Wrecks (Designation No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates as a restricted area for the purposes of the Protection of Wrecks Act 1973 an area off Rame Head, Plymouth round the site of what is thought to be the wreck of a vessel which is of historical and archaeological importance. No part of the restricted area lies above high water mark of ordinary spring tides.

items.title: The National Savings Bank (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which amend the National Savings Bank Regulations 1972, raise the minimum ordinary deposit from £1 to £5. They also give to the Director of Savings a discretion to accept ordinary deposits or investment deposits of less than that amount which are made at any savings bank office where previously such deposits could only be made if presented directly to the principal office of the National Savings Bank.

items.title: The Income Tax (Reduced and Composite Rate) Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of the Income Tax (Building Societies) Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/482, amended by S.I. 1987/844 and 1988/1011) a building society is required to pay to the Inland Revenue in respect of payments of dividends and interest an amount representing income tax on certain sums calculated in part at the basic rate and in part at “the reduced rate”.

items.title: The Rating Lists (Valuation Date) Order 1988
items.description: The first non-domestic rating lists under Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 are, by virtue of section 41(2) and 52(2) of the Act, to be compiled on 1st April 1990. Article 2 of the Order provides that 1st April 1988 is to be the day by reference to which determinations of rateable value in accordance with paragraph 2 of Schedule 6 to the Act are to be made in relation to those lists.

items.title: The Financial Services (Designated Countries and Territories) (Overseas Collective Investment Schemes) (Guernsey) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the Bailiwick of Guernsey for the purposes of section 87 of the Financial Services Act 1986 in respect of schemes managed in and authorised under the law of the Bailiwick which are of a class falling within the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Financial Services (Designated Countries and Territories) (Overseas Collective Investment Schemes) (Jersey) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the Bailiwick of Jersey for the purposes of section 87 of the Financial Services Act 1986 in respect of schemes managed in and authorised under the law of the Bailiwick which are of a class falling within the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Cycle Racing on Highways (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Cycle Racing on Highways Regulations 1960, as amended, which apply to England and Wales, authorise certain races or trials of speed between bicycles or tricycles, not being motor vehicles, (described in those Regulations as bicycle races) to be held on public highways subject to certain conditions including conditions as to the number of competitors taking part.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Joint Financing) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations consolidate the Civil Aviation (Joint Financing) Regulations 1982, as amended.

items.title: The Housing Act 1988 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 2nd January 1989 section 140 of the Housing Act 1988 in relation to certain amendments to the Housing (Scotland) Acts 1987 and 1988. It brings into force on 15th January 1989 provisions of the Housing Act 1988 mentioned below —

items.title: The London Regional Transport Levy (General Rate Act 1967) (Modification) Order 1988
items.description: Section 13 of the London Regional Transport Act 1984 provides that the Secretary of State may, in respect of any year, make a levy on the rating authorities for all the rating areas in Greater London to recover a contribution from Greater London ratepayers towards his estimated expenditure in that year on grants to London Regional Transport, and for the purpose of raising the levy the Secretary of State may issue a demand for payments to the rating authorities. Such a demand shall require the rating authorities to levy as part, or as an additional item of the rate, a rate of an amount in the pound specified by Order in respect of the year to which the demand relates.

items.title: The Education (Schools and Further Education) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 10 of the Education (Schools and Further Education) Regulations 1981, which relates to the duration of the school year and day. They provide that where, in the academic years 1988/89 and 1989/90 but before 1st January 1990 a school session is devoted to the training of teachers at the school for the introduction of the National Curriculum (as defined in section 2(1)(6) of the Education Reform Act 1988 (c. 40)), it shall be treated for the purposes of the regulations as a session on which the school has met. The requirement in regulation 10(3) as to minimum hours of secular instruction per day is disapplied to the extent that such sessions of training take place.

items.title: The Landlord’s Repairing Obligations (Specified Rent) (Scotland) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: Paragraph 1 of Schedule 10 to the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 as amended by paragraph 9 of Schedule 8 to the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988 provides that in any contract to which the paragraph applies there shall, notwithstanding any stipulations to the contrary, be implied a condition that the house is at the commencement of the tenancy and an undertaking that the house will be kept by the landlord during the tenancy in all respects reasonably fit for human habitation.

items.title: The Isles of Scilly (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Littlehampton (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7(5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Wells (Pilotage) Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: By virtue of section 7 (5) of the Pilotage Act 1987 a direction given by a harbour authority for the purpose of making pilotage compulsory for ships navigating in a specified area shall not apply to any area which is outside the authority’s harbour unless the limits of jurisdiction of the authority for pilotage purposes have been extended to cover that area.

items.title: The Criminal Appeal (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Criminal Appeal Rules 1968 so as to provide for applications under section 32(1)(b) of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (evidence through television links of witness under 14).

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) (No. 5) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Crown Court Rules 1982 so as to provide for applications under section 32(1)(b) of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 (evidence through television links witness under 14).

items.title: The Lotteries (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Lotteries Regulations 1977, which relate to lotteries promoted on behalf of societies registered under Schedule 1 to the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976 and lotteries promoted by local authorities under that Act. Regulation 2 introduces an additional restriction as to the sale of tickets or chances in a lottery where there is a set of lotteries in which the determination of winners in those lotteries is combined to secure the winning of several prizes by the holder of a group of winning tickets or chances. The restriction does not apply to any lottery in which tickets or chances have already been sold when these Regulations come into force (regulation 1(2)).

items.title: The Police (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Regulations 1987. They correct an error in an amending instrument, the Police (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/1821), in relation to the annual pay of constables after 8 years of service and before 12 years of service.

items.title: The Police Cadets (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Cadets Regulations 1979. Regulation 3(a) and (b) increase the pay of cadets attached to the Metropolitan Police force from 1st July 1988 and again from 1st September 1988 and of cadets elsewhere from 1st September 1988 (retrospective effect is authorised by section 33(4), as applied by section 35(2), of the Police Act 1964). Regulation 3(c) increases charges payable by cadets for board and lodging provided by police authorities.

items.title: The Civil Courts (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Civil Courts (Amendment) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) (No. 2) 1988'
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1978 to make provision for fees in respect of work under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) (No.2) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1978 to make provision for fees in respect of work under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987.

items.title: The Pensions Increase (Review) Order 1988
items.description: Under section 59 of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 as amended by section 11 of the Social Security Act 1979 and as modified by section 59A of the 1975 Act (introduced by section 11(4) of the 1979 Act) the Treasury (in whom the functions conferred by those provisions are now vested) are required to provide by order for the increase in the rates of public service pensions. The increase is the percentage (or in some circumstances a fraction of the percentage) by which the Secretary of State for Social Services has, by directions given under the provisions of section 23(2) of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (as amended by Schedule 10, paragraph 91 of the Social Security Act 1986), increased the sums referred to in section 63(1)(b) of the Social Security Act 1986. These are the sums which are the additional components in the rates of long term benefits, namely the additional pension entitlements accruing to employees in respect of earnings after 5th April 1978.

items.title: The School Curriculum Development Committee and Secondary Examinations Council (Designation of Staff) Order 1988
items.description: This Order concerns certain staff of the School Curriculum Development Committee and the Secondary Examinations Council. Those bodies are to be wound up in consequence of the establishment (under section 14 of the Education Reform Act 1988) of the Curriculum Council for Wales, the National Curriculum Council, and the School Examinations and Assessment Council. Under section 15 of that Act, the Secretary of State may designate (either individually or by class or description) employees of the existing bodies for automatic transfer to the new Councils. Where an individual is so designated there is no termination of, or change in the terms of, his contract of employment. This Order lists those staff so designated.

items.title: The School Curriculum Development Committee and Secondary Examinations Council (Transfer of Property) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers property, rights and liabilities of the School Curriculum Development Committee and the Secondary Examinations Council to the Curriculum Council for Wales, the National Curriculum Council, and the School Examinations and Assessment Council, which were established under section 14 of the Education Reform Act 1988.

items.title: The Valuation Timetable (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the entry in the Schedule to the Valuation Timetable (Scotland) Order 1984 which contains the timetable for the disposal by valuation appeal committees of appeals and complaints against entries in a valuation roll. That Order was most recently amended by the Valuation Timetable (Scotland) Amendment Order 1988, which extended the period for disposal of such appeals and complaints from 31st March 1988 to 31st December 1988.

items.title: The Iron Casting (Scientific Research Levy)(Abolition) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that the Iron Casting (Scientific Research Levy) Order 1971, as amended, shall cease to have effect as respects the imposition of the levy for any period after 30th June 1990.

items.title: The Merger Reference (Hillsdown Holdings plc and Pittard Garnar plc) Order 1988
items.description: Hillsdown Holdings plc, its subsidiaries and its associates are prohibited by this Order from acquiring any shares or interests in shares in Pittard Garnar plc if together they would have 15% or more of any class of voting shares, from exercising 15% or more of voting rights and from making agreements with others to secure control of Pittard Garnar.

items.title: The Cumbria and Lancashire (County Boundaries) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers areas between the county of Cumbria and district of South Lakeland and the county of Lancashire and City of Lancaster. Consequential changes to parishes and local government electoral areas are made. The areas and population affected are small.

items.title: The Fife and Tayside Regions and North East Fife and Perth and Kinross Districts (Duncrievie) Boundaries Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers an area of approximately 10.5 acres at Duncrievie from North East Fife District in Fife Region to Perth and Kinross District in Tayside Region and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Cleveland and Durham (County Boundaries) Order 1988
The Accommodation of Children (Charge and Control)Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide for the accommodation, under the charge and control of a parent, guardian, relative or friend, of children in the care of a local authority.

items.title: The Boarding-out of Children (Foster Placement) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace the Boarding-out of Children Regulations 1955, which are revoked by regulation 20.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest on Unpaid Tax and Repayment Supplement) (No. 4) Order 1988
items.description: On and after 6th January 1989 this Order increases from 10.75 per cent. per annum to 11.5 per cent. per annum the rate of interest chargeable on unpaid income tax, surtax, capital gains tax, corporation tax (including advance corporation tax), development land tax, petroleum revenue tax, profits tax, excess profits tax, excess profits levy and on overpaid development land tax. The Order also provides that on and after 6th January 1989 supplement in respect of repayments of certain taxes will be at the rate of 11.5 per cent. per annum instead of 10.75 per cent. per annum.

items.title: The Income Tax (Official Rate of Interest on Beneficial Loans) (No. 4) Order 1988
items.description: By this Order the official rate of interest for the purposes of section 160 of, and Schedule 7 to, the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (charge to income tax on beneficial loans) is prescribed to be 14.5 per cent. per annum on and after 6th January 1989.

items.title: The Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Interest on Tax Repaid) (No.4) Order 1988
items.description: Section 92 of the Finance Act 1986 provides that, if certain conditions have been fulfilled and a claim for repayment is made within the stipulated period, stamp duty reserve tax shall be repaid. Where the tax paid is not less than £25, it shall be repaid with interest at the appropriate rate. The section defines the “appropriate rate” as 11 per cent. per annum or such other rate as the Treasury may from time to time specify by order.

items.title: The Children and Young Persons (Amendment) Act 1986 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings section 1 of the Children and Young Persons (Amendment) Act 1986 into force on 1st June 1989. Section 1 makes provision for regulations to be made as to the accommodation under the charge and control of a parent, guardian, relative or friend of children who are in the care of a local authority.

items.title: The Housing (Prescribed Forms) (Repair Notices etc.) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe new forms for use by local housing authorities for the purposes of Part VI of the Housing Act 1985 (repair notices) as amended by Schedule 15 to the Housing Act 1988. These amendments do not have effect in relation to any repair notices, within the meaning of Part VI, served before 15th January 1989.

items.title: The East Kilbride and City of Glasgow Districts (Carmunnock) Boundaries Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers two areas of ground extending to approximately 67 acres at Carmunnock from East Kilbride District to City of Glasgow District and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations re-enact with modifications the Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) (Scotland) Regulations 1974, as amended, and make provision for the implementation of Council Directive 85/397/EEC (OJ No. L226, 24.8.1985, p.13) on health and animal health problems affecting intra-Community trade in heat-treated milk.

items.title: The Milk (Special Designations) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order re-enacts with modifications the Milk (Special Designations) (Scotland) Order 1980 (“the 1980 Order”), as amended, and makes provision for the implementation of Council Directive 85/397/EEC (OJ No. L226, 24.8.1985, p.13) on health and animal health problems affecting intra-Community trade in heat-treated milk.

items.title: The Housing (Scotland) Act 1988 (Specified Date) Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies 1st April 1989 as the specified date for the purposes of section 3(1) of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1988.

items.title: The Lotteries (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Lotteries (Scotland) Regulations 1977, which relate to lotteries promoted on behalf of societies registered under Schedule 1 to the Lotteries and Amusements Act 1976 and lotteries promoted by local authorities under that Act. Regulation 2 introduces an additional restriction as to the sale of tickets or chances in a lottery where there is a set of lotteries in which the determination of winners in those lotteries is combined to secure the winning of several prizes by the holder of a group of winning tickets or chances. The restriction does not apply to any lottery in which tickets or chances have already been sold when these Regulations come into force (regulation 1(2)).

items.title: The Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace and simplify the scale of building control fees in the Building (Procedure) (Scotland) Regulations 1981. The average increase is 20%. Flat rate and minimum fees are increased to £12.

items.title: The Rent Act 1977 (Forms etc.) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations make an amendment to form No. 15 (application for cancellation of a rent registered by a rent tribunal where the dwelling is not subject to a restricted contract) in the Rent Act 1977 (Forms etc.) Regulations 1980, consequential on provisions in the Housing Act 1988 (c. 50).

items.title: The Cod (Specified Sea Area) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Cod (Specified Sea Area) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Plaice (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Plaice (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Rent Book (Forms of Notice) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Rent Book (Forms of Notice) Regulations 1982 by adding a form of notice which (under section 5 of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1985 as amended by the Housing Act 1988) is to be inserted in rent books or other similar documents. The new form of notice relates to premises let on or subject to an assured tenancy or an assured agricultural occupancy.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Rent Information) Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies the information on rents of assured tenancies and assured agricultural occupancies which is to be made publicly available by the President of each rent assessment panel, the manner in which it is to be made available and the fee to be charged for the supply of a certified copy of such information.

items.title: The Building Societies (Designated Capital Resources) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that building societies can count as part of their capital subordinated debt which meets certain conditions specified in the Order.

items.title: The Strathclyde and Central Regions and Monklands and Falkirk Districts (Black Loch) Boundaries Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers an area of approximately 42 hectares at Black Loch from Monklands District in Strathclyde Region to Falkirk District in Central Region and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Rent Assessment Committees (England and Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Rent Assessment Committees (England and Wales) Regulations 1971 (which regulate the procedure to be followed by rent assessment committees) to take account of the functions of rent assessment committees under Part I of the Housing Act 1988 in relation to assured tenancies and assured agricultural occupancies.

items.title: The Notices to Quit etc. (Prescribed Information) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the information to be contained in a landlord’s notice to quit given on or after the 15th January 1989 to determine a tenancy of premises let as a dwelling, or a licensor’s notice given on or after that date to determine a periodic licence to occupy premises as a dwelling. They do not apply to the premises specified in section 5(1B) of the Protection from Eviction Act 1977 (premises subject to excluded licences or certain excluded tenancies). These Regulations replace the Notices to Quit (Prescribed Information) Regulations 1980, which applied only to tenancies.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Capital Money) (Greater London) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Local Government Reorganisation (Capital Money) (Greater London) Order 1988. The effect of the amendments is to require the London Residuary Body to deduct an additional £50 millions from the amounts, both notional and actual, that they would otherwise be required to distribute to the rating authorities in London in respect of the financial year ending 31st March 1989.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies and Agricultural Occupancies (Forms) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the forms to be used for the purposes of various provisions of Part I of the Housing Act 1988 relating to assured tenancies and assured agricultural occupancies.

items.title: The Milk (Special Designation) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations re-enact with modifications the Milk (Special Designation) Regulations 1986, as amended, and make provision for the implementation of Council Directive 85/397/EEC (OJ No. L226, 24.8.1985, p. 13) on health and animal health problems affecting intra-Community trade in heat-treated milk.

items.title: The Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations re-enact with modifications the Milk and Dairies (Semi-skimmed and Skimmed Milk) (Heat Treatment and Labelling) Regulations 1986, as amended, and make provision for the implementation of Council Directive 85/397/EEC (OJ No. L226, 24.8.1985, p.13) on health and animal health problems affecting intra-Community trade in heat-treated milk.

items.title: The Ministry of Defence Police (Defence Police Federation) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations relate to the organisation, financial arrangements and rights of consultation of the Ministry of Defence Police Federation.

items.title: The Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st February 1989 the provisions of the Firearms (Amendment) Act 1988 specified in the Schedule to the Order. Article 4 postpones the coming into force of two provisions of that Act which extend the classes of firearms that are prohibited weapons under the Firearms Act 1968 (c. 27), so that persons who are in lawful possession of certain firearms prior to that date may hold and dispose of the firearms until the end of April 1989 and registered firearms dealers may acquire, hold and dispose of such firearms until the end of October 1989.

items.title: The City of Glasgow and Strathkelvin Districts (Easter Balmuildy Farm and Laigh Kenmure Farm) Boundaries Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers areas of approximately 83.5 acres at Easter Balmuildy Farm and Laigh Kenmure Farm between City of Glasgow District and Strathkelvin District and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Community Charges (Notification of Deaths) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the particulars of death of persons over the age of 16 years usually resident in the United Kingdom which the district registrar for each registration district shall furnish to the appropriate Community Charges Registration Officer (regulation 3).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Imported Goods) Relief (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Value Added Tax (Imported Goods) Relief Order 1984 which provides for relief from value added tax chargeable on the permanent importation of certain goods into the United Kingdom. The amendments give effect to Council Directive 88/331/EEC which amends Council Directive 83/181/EEC. Comparable provisions for relief from customs duty are conferred by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 918/83 which is similarly amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No. 1315/88.

items.title: The Spring Traps Approval (Scotland) Variation Order 1988
items.description: It is an offence under section 50 of the Agriculture (Scotland) Act 1948 as amended by section 10 of the Pests Act 1954 to use for the purpose of killing or taking animals any spring trap other than one of the type and kind approved by the Secretary of State. This Order adds the Springer No. 4 Multi-purpose (Heavy Duty) and the Springer No. 6 Multi-purpose to those traps already approved in the Spring Traps Approval (Scotland) Order 1975 and specifies the animals for which and the circumstances in which each may be used.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths, Marriages and Divorces (Fees) (Scotland) (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe, with effect from 1st April 1989, the fees payable to the Registrar General and district registrars for services provided under the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Scotland) Act 1965. The fees for the first to seventh and tenth to sixteenth services listed in Schedule 1 and for the first to ninth services listed in Schedule 2 are increased. The fees for the first, fourth, eighth and ninth services introduce a two tier system of fees for personal or postal applications. The fees for the other services are not changed. The Registration of Births, Deaths, Marriages and Divorces (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 1988 are revoked.

items.title: The Marriage Fees (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations set out, with effect from 1st April 1989, the fees payable under sections 3(1) and 19(2) of the Marriage (Scotland) Act 1977 (fees for the preliminaries to marriage and for the solemnisation of a civil marriage respectively). The fee under section 3(1) is increased from £5 to £5.75, and the fee under section 19(2) is increased from £12 to £13. The Marriage Fees (Scotland) Regulations 1986 are revoked.

items.title: The Statutory Harbour Undertakings (Pilotage Accounts) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 42 of the Harbours Act 1964, as amended, specifies requirements as to accounts and reports relating to the activities of statutory harbour undertakings (as defined in the section) and enables Regulations to be made relating to their pilotage activities. Section 14 of the Pilotage Act 1987 enables Regulations to be made providing for the publication of these accounts. These Regulations make provision as to the information in relation to its pilotage activities to be included in a competent harbour authority’s statement of accounts, and for the publication of that information.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Repayment to Community Traders) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations which come into force on 1st February 1989, amend the Value Added Tax (Repayment to Community Traders) Regulations 1980 by extending the repayment provisions to importations of goods into the United Kingdom by persons carrying on a business in a member state other than the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Central Regional Council (Kirkton Burn, Balquhidder) Water Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Central Regional Council (Kirkton Burn, Balquhidder) Water Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Price Marking (Petrol) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Price Marking (Petrol) Order 1980, as amended, by removing the requirement that, where petrol is sold by reference to the litre on price display premises, the equivalent price per gallon shall also be indicated on the roadside price display; and by permitting the use of a price equivalent chart at other premises where petrol is sold by the litre as an alternative to marking the gallon equivalent price on the pump.

items.title: The Employment Subsidies Act 1978 (Renewal) (Great Britain) Order 1988
items.description: This Order renews for Great Britain until 30th June 1990 the powers of section 1 of the Employment Subsidies Act 1978, which would otherwise not be exercisable after the end of December 1988, the section having been renewed until that date by the Employment Subsidies Act 1978 (Renewal) (Great Britain) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/1124). Section 1 of the Employment Subsidies Act 1978 authorises the Secretary of State to make payments to employers enabling them to retain in employment persons who might otherwise become unemployed, to take on new employees and generally to maintain or enlarge their labour force.

items.title: The Superannuation (Children’s Pensions) (Earnings Limit) Order 1988
items.description: Subject to certain conditions section 21 of the Judicial Pensions Act 1981 permits the payment of a children’s pension under that Act to continue after the child reaches the age of 16 if the child is undergoing training for a trade, profession or vocation. One of the conditions is that the emoluments received during training, excluding any emoluments receivable or payable by way of return of any premium paid in respect of the training, do not exceed £1178 a year. This Order revokes the Superannuation (Children’s Pensions) (Earnings Limit) Order 1987 and increases the limit on such emoluments from £1178 to £1227 a year.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Berkshire) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 to take account of the abolition of the petty sessional divisions of Bradfield and Sonning and Reading and their reconstitution as the new division of Reading and Sonning effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Berkshire) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/2008) which comes into force on 1st January 1989. It also increases from two to three the number of justices who may be appointed to the Berkshire Probation Committee by the justices for the petty sessional division of Slough.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Oxfordshire) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 to take account of the combination of the petty sessional divisions of Moreton and Wallingford and of Wantage and Faringdon in the new petty sessional division of Didcot and Wantage, effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Oxfordshire) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1665) which comes into force on 1st January 1989.

items.title: The Combined Probation Areas (Warwickshire) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Combined Probation Areas Order 1986 to take account of the abolition of the petty sessional divisions of Alcester, Kineton, Shipston on Stour, Southam, Stratford-upon-Avon and Warwick and their reconstitution into the new divisions of Mid-Warwickshire and South Warwickshire, effected by the Petty Sessional Divisions (Warwickshire) Order 1988 (S.I. 1988/1912) which comes into force on1st January 1989.

items.title: The Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions of the Fire Services (Appointments and Promotion) Regulations 1978 relating to the qualifications for promotion to, or appointment in, the rank of fireman or firewoman in a fire brigade maintained under the Fire Services Act 1947.

items.title: The Assured and Protected Tenancies (Lettings to Students) Regulations 1988
items.description: Section 8 of the Rent Act 1977 and paragraph 8 of Schedule 1 to the Housing Act 1988 excepts from the definition of “protected tenancy” and “assured tenancy” in section 1 of the respective Acts a tenancy granted to a student, or prospective student, at an educational institution specified for the purposes of the said section 8 or paragraph 8 by regulations if the tenancy is granted by such an institution or by a body so specified.

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Incentive Payments) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: Regulation 3 of the Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Incentive Payments) Regulations 1987 specifies those cases in which an incentive payment under section 7 of the Social Security Act 1986 is not to be made. Paragraph (2) of that regulation provides that an incentive payment is not to be made if the earner in question or another earner has during any part of the period from 1st January 1986 to 5th April 1993 been in contracted-out employment by reference to another scheme, which employment is indistinguishable from the employment of the earner in question except by reference to the identity of the employer or employee or both.

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Compensation) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide that for a personal pension scheme which is not within the investor protection provisions of one or other of the Policyholders Protection Act 1975, the Building Societies Act 1986, the Banking Act 1987, the Financial Services (Compensation of Investors) Rules 1988 or the Friendly Societies Protection Scheme to be an appropriate scheme under Part I of the Social Security Act 1986, it must secure that any part of its liability for protected rights which it is unable to meet from its own resources are, at least to the extent that they would be met if one or other of those provisions did apply, met from some other source.

items.title: The Clergy Pensions (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These regulations are made under section 6 of the Clergy Pensions (Amendment) Measure 1972, in preparation for the consolidation of the law relating to pensions for the clergy and church workers and for their widows, widowers and dependants in the Church of England Pensions Regulations 1988. They incorporate into the Clergy Pensions Measure 1961 and the Clergy Pensions (Amendment) Measure 1967 certain provisions at present contained in regulations which cannot appropriately be included in the consolidated Regulations.

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Order 1978 by substituting new schedules for Schedules 1 and 2 to that Order (article 2(a) and (b)). Schedule 1, which lists approved disinfectants, includes newly approved disinfectants, and Schedule 2 lists disinfectants which are now omitted from Schedule 1 but which may nevertheless continue to be used as approved disinfectants until 30 June 1988 (article 2(c)).

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) (No.2) Order 1988 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series of Orders designating the Ministers who, and departments which, may exercise powers to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communites Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which these powers may be exercised.

items.title: The Architects' Qualifications (EC Recognition) Order 1988
items.description: This Order under section 2 of the European Communities Act 1972 comes into force on 1st February 1989.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Torture) (Overseas Territories) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends sections 134 and 135 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988, subject to modifications, to the territories specified in Schedule 2 hereto.

items.title: The Extradition (Hijacking) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Extradition (Hijacking) Order 1971 (which among its provisions names States party to the Convention for the Suppression of Unlawful Seizure of Aircraft signed at The Hague on 16th December 1970) by adding an entry for Congo.

items.title: The Extradition (Internationally Protected Persons) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Extradition (Internationally Protected Persons) Order 1979 (which among its provisions names States party to the Convention on the Prevention and Punishment of Crimes against Internationally Protected Persons, including Diplomatic Agents, signed at New York on 14th December 1973) by adding entries for Oman and Syria.

items.title: The Extradition (Protection of Aircraft) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Extradition (Protection of Aircraft) Order 1973 (which among its provisions names States party to the Convention for the Suppression of Unlawful Acts against the Safety of Civil Aviation signed at Montreal on 23rd September 1971) by adding an entry for Congo.

items.title: The Extradition (Taking of Hostages) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Extradition (Taking of Hostages) Order 1985 by adding to Schedule 2 (which names the foreign states with which the United Kingdom has extradition treaties in force and which are parties to the International Convention against the Taking of Hostages opened for signature at New York on 18th December 1979) entries for Czechoslovakia and Ecuador, and by adding to Part I of Schedule 3 (which names the foreign states with which the United Kingdom has no extradition treaties in force and which are parties to the Convention) entries for Bulgaria, Cameroon and the German Democratic Republic.

items.title: The Extradition (Torture) Order 1988
items.description: This Order applies the Extradition Acts 1870 to 1895 so as to make extraditable the offence described in section 134 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 and an attempt to commit such an offence, in the case of States Parties to the United Nations Convention Against Torture and other Cruel, Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment adopted by the General Assembly of the United Nations on 10th December 1984 with which extradition treaties are in force.

items.title: The Naval, Military and Air Forces etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Amendment (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which takes effect on 1st February 1989, further amends the Naval, Military and Air Forces etc. (Disablement and Death) Service Pensions Order 1983 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Hartlebury Borehole) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Severn-Trent Water Authority (Hartlebury Borehole) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Fourth Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Air Navigation Order 1985.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Categorisation of Registries of Overseas Territories) Order 1988
items.description: Section 11 of the Merchant Shipping Act 1988 enables Orders in Council to establish categories of registry by reference to which the registration of ships in the relevant overseas territories — ie Crown Dependencies and dependent territories — may be regulated. This Order establishes the first such category, the “unlimited tonnage and type” category, to which Bermuda and the Isle of Man are assigned under which there is no restriction as to the ships which may be registered.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Pollution by Garbage) Order 1988
items.description: This Order enables effect to be given to Annex V to the International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships 1973 as amended by the Protocol (Cmnd 7347) thereto of 1978.

items.title: The Ministerial and other Salaries Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases salaries payable under the Ministerial and other Salaries Act 1975 to Ministers, to salaried Members of the Opposition, to the Speaker of the House of Commons and to other paid office holders. The amounts specified in this Order are the maximum salaries payable. The actual salaries in payment may therefore be below these levels.

items.title: The Trustee Investments (Additional Powers) Order 1988
items.description: This Order adds National Savings Bonds to the list of narrower-range investments not requiring advice, set out in Part I of the First Schedule to the Trustee Investment Act 1961.

items.title: The General Medical Council Preliminary Proceedings Committee and Professional Conduct Committee (Procedure) Rules Order of Council 1988
items.description: The Rules approved by this Order revoke and replace the General Medical Council Preliminary Proceedings Committee and Professional Conduct Committee (Procedure) Rules 1980 (approved by S.I. 1980/858).

items.title: The Church of England Pensions Regulations 1988
items.description: These regulations consolidate with minor amendments the law relating to pensions for the clergy and church workers and for their widows, widowers and dependants which is at present contained in the Church of England (Pensions) Measures 1961 to 1988 and in regulations made under section 6 of the Clergy Pensions (Amendment) Measure 1972. The amendments are made solely for administrative purposes and do not prejudice any entitlement to pension benefits. The regulations, being made in exercise of the power contained in section 6 of the 1972 Measure, do not affect the financial and constitutional provisions of the Clergy Pensions Measure 1961.

items.title: The Customs Duties (ECSC) (Quota and Other Reliefs) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 1st January 1989, provides for reliefs from customs duty (in so far as it is charged) on certain iron and steel products originating in the developing countries named in Schedule 2 to the Order in accordance with a Decision of the representatives of the governments of the Member States of the European Coal and Steel Community meeting in Council of [19/20 December 1988]. The reliefs are provided under the Community’s Generalised Tariff Preference Scheme for Developing Countries.

items.title: The Financial Services (Recognised Collective Investment Schemes from Other Member States) (Luxembourg) Order 1988
items.description: This Order directs that schemes constituted in the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg which meet the requirements specified in article 3 of the Order are recognised schemes for the purposes of the Financial Services Act 1986. Under section 86 of the Act, as it applies by virtue of paragraph 10 of Schedule 15, such schemes are required to give written notice to the Securities and Investments Board two months before inviting persons in the United Kingdom to become participants. The notice must specify the manner in which the invitation is to be made and a scheme will be not a recognised scheme if, within the two month period, the Securities and Investments Board gives notice that the manner in which the invitation is to be made does not comply with the law in force in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Amendment (No.3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which doctors and chemists provide general medical services and pharmaceutical services under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The Matrimonial Causes (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: (This Note is not part of the Rules)

items.title: The Personal Injuries (Civilians) Amendment (No. 2) Scheme 1988
items.description: This Scheme, which takes effect on 1st February 1989, further amends the Personal Injuries (Civilians) Scheme 1983 (“the principal Scheme”).

items.title: The Zoonoses Order 1988
items.description: Section 29 of the Animal Health Act 1981 (“the Act”) empowers Ministers, with a view to reducing the risk to human health from any disease of animals, to make an order designating any such disease which, in their opinion, constitutes such a risk and applying any provision of the Act to that disease.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) Regulations 1973 which concern arrangements under which dentists provide general dental services under the National Health Service in England and Wales by providing for the Statement of Dental Remuneration (distributed to those providing general dental services) to include partial reimbursement of expenses of setting up or expanding a practice in an area where there is a shortage of dentists.

items.title: The County of Dyfed (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The County of Dyfed (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Use of Invalid Carriages on Highways Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Use of Invalid Carriages on Highways Regulations 1970 (S.I. 1970/1391) (“the 1970 Regulations”). The 1970 Regulations however continue to apply to invalid carriages manufactured before 30th January 1989. These Regulations make provision for 3 classes of invalid carriages which, if they meet the prescribed conditions and requirements, may be used under the modifications of the law provided by s.20 of the Chronically Sick and Disabled Persons Act 1970.

items.title: The Artificial Insemination (Cattle and Pigs) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Artificial Insemination (Cattle and Pigs) (Fees) Regulations 1987 by revising the fees payable in connection with the issue of licences and approvals under certain Regulations relating to the artificial insemination of cattle and pigs and in connection with tests and examinations carried out for the purposes of these Regulations (regulations 2, 4 and 5 and Schedules). Old fees are shown in the revised Schedules in italics.

items.title: The Education Reform Act 1988 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st January 1989 section 152(10) of the Education Reform Act 1988, which requires local education authorities to take into account guidance issued by the Secretary of State as to the content of instruments and articles of government for institutions of further or higher education which they maintain. Also brought into force on that date are section 219 (to the extent not already in force) - the section extends the provisions of sections 67, 68 and 99 of the Education Act 1944(c. 31), which gave powers to the Secretary of State to determine disputes, to prevent unreasonable exercise of powers, and to act in default - paragraphs 63 and 67 of Schedule 12 (amendment of the Charities Act 1960(c. 58) and the Parliamentary Commissioner Act 1967(c. 13)) and the provisions of Schedule 13 repealing various provisions of the Education Acts 1944 and 1946(c. 50) relating to religious education and worship, and repealing the Education (No. 2) Act 1968.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Emergency Equipment Lockers for Ro/Ro Passenger Ships) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations introduce requirements for the provision on United Kingdom ro/ro passenger ships of Classes I, II, II(A) and IV of lockers containing equipment for use in emergency situations including a situation when the ship is at a large angle of heel.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Safety at Work Regulations) (Non-UK Ships) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Merchant Shipping (Safety at Work Regulations) (Non-UK Ships) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) Order 1988
items.description: Section 11 of the Clean Air Act 1956 empowers local authorities to declare the whole or any part of their district to be a smoke control area in which the emission of smoke is generally prohibited. Specified classes of fireplace may be exempted from control by order.

items.title: The Education (Higher Education Corporations) (No. 4) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision for the establishment of bodies corporate to conduct the institutions specified in column 2 of the Schedule hereto. The bodies corporate are established by virtue of paragraph 1(2) of Schedule 7 to the Education Reform Act 1988 by the names given in column 1 on 24th January 1989 to prepare themselves for the conduct of those institutions from 1st April 1989 which is appointed as the transfer date, defined in section 123(2) of that Act as the date for transfer of local education authority property, rights and liabilities to those corporations. The Order also specifies, in relation to each such corporation, the local authority or authorities able to nominate one or more local authority nominees to become members of the corporation.

items.title: The Financial Services (Designated Countries and Territories) (Overseas Collective Investment Schemes) (Bermuda) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates Bermuda for the purposes of section 87 of the Financial Services Act 1986 in respect of schemes managed in and authorised under the law of Bermuda which are of a class falling within the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Commencement) (No. 11) Order 1988
items.description: This Order postpones, until 28th February 1989, the coming into force of the restriction on the promotion of certain collective investment schemes, namely open-ended investment companies which are managed in and authorised under the law of Bermuda and units in which are included in the Official List on 31st December 1988 or are promoted by a complying prospectus.
