items.description: This version of the The (A1) London—Edinburgh—Thurso Trunk Road (Newcastle Western Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1986 (Variation) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Profit-Related Pay (Shortfall Recovery) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Profit-Related Pay (Shortfall Recovery) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Passenger Boarding Cards) (Application to non-UK Ships) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations apply the requirements of the Merchant Shipping (Passenger Boarding Cards) Regulations 1988 to non-United Kingdom passenger ships operating as passenger ships of Classes II and II(A) in respect of voyages departing from United Kingdom ports. (Those Regulations require the operation of a boarding card system for all voyages on which passengers are carried.)

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Closing of Openings in Enclosed Superstructures and in Bulkheads above the Bulkhead Deck) (Application to Non-United Kingdom ships) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations apply to non-United Kingdom ro/ro passenger ships the requirements of the Merchant Shipping (Closing of Openings in Enclosed Superstructures and in Bulkheads above the Bulkhead Deck) Regulations 1988 while they are within a port in the United Kingdom. These Regulations include such requirements as appear necessary to implement and impose additional, more precise requirements, for the control of watertight and weathertight doors fitted in the hull and enclosed superstructure above the bulkhead deck and for their closing and for relevant entries in the official log book, but only so long as the ship is within such a port.

items.title: The Department of Transport (Fees) Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies functions and matters which are to be taken into account in the determination of fees to be fixed by the Secretary of State for Transport in respect of certain activities concerning road vehicles, road transport, drivers, goods vehicle operators, public service vehicle operators and bus services.

items.title: The Banking Act 1987 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 29th April 1988 all the provisions of the Banking Act 1987 which are not already in force.

items.title: The Banking Act 1987 (Advertisements) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations re-enact the Banking Act 1979 (Advertisements) Regulations 1985 (S.I. 1985/220, amended by S.I. 1987/64). They apply to deposit advertisements (which are defined in section 32(5) of the Banking Act 1987 (c. 22)) subject to the exclusions specified in regulation 2. In particular, the Regulations do not apply where the deposits are to be made only with offices in the United Kingdom or another member State of the European Communities, or, if there is no indication of the offices with which the deposits are to be made, if the deposit-taker carries on business there. The one change of substance from the previous Regulations is that these Regulations exclude advertisements which invite only such deposits as are incidental to investment business and which are regulated by rules made in the context of the Financial Services Act 1986. In addition there are minor and drafting amendments.

items.title: The Banking Act 1987 (Exempt Transactions) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe certain transactions as transactions to which the prohibition on deposit-taking imposed by section 3 of the Banking Act 1987 does not apply. They re-enact the Banking Act 1979 (Exempt Transactions) Regulations 1986 with amendments. In addition to minor and drafting amendments they make the following three changes of substance.

items.title: The State Scheme Premiums (Actuarial Tables) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These regulations amend regulations 6 and 7 of and the second table of Schedule 5 to the State Scheme Premiums (Actuarial Tables) Regulations 1987 so as to enable the Secretary of State to calculate the amount of any pensioner’s rights premium payable or, in the case of a personal pension protected rights premium or contracted-out protected rights premium, the amount needed to restore fully rights in the State Scheme in respect of a widower. Hitherto the provisions for buying-back applied only to members and their widows. From 6th April 1989, under the provisions of the Social Security Act 1986, they will apply also to widowers of women members in respect of rights attributable to pensionable service after 5th April 1988 if both husband and wife are over pensionable age when the wife dies.

items.title: The Inner London Education Area (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Inner London Education Area (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The (A1) London—Edinburgh—Thurso Trunk Road (Newcastle Western Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1986 Variation Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A1) London—Edinburgh—Thurso Trunk Road (Newcastle Western Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1986 Variation Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) (Amendment) Order 1988 (revoked)
items.description: This Order further amends the Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) Order 1986, which prescribes the quota per hectare and the average yield per hectare to be taken into account in determining the standard quota for the purposes of calculating the payment which a tenant in England and Wales is entitled, in certain circumstances, by Schedule 1 to the Agriculture Act 1986 to obtain from his landlord in respect of milk quota on the termination of the tenancy of the land.

items.title: The Finance Act 1986 (Stamp Duty and Stamp Duty Reserve Tax) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: Sections 81(3) and 89(3) of the Finance Act 1986 contain definitions of “market maker” for the purposes of exemption from stamp duty and stamp duty reserve tax respectively. Sections 81(5) and 89(5) provide that the Treasury may by regulations provide that for sections 81(3) and 89(3) there shall be substituted subsections containing different definitions of “market maker” for the purposes of those sections. These Regulations substitute such subsections for sections 81(3) and 89(3).

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Barnet Way, Barnet) (Box Junction) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Barnet Way, Barnet) (Box Junction) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Personal Equity Plan (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Personal Equity Plan Regulations 1986 (“the Principal Regulations”). First, they limit the type of authorised unit trusts which, following the coming into force of the relevant part of the Financial Services Act 1986 on 29th April 1988, will be qualifying investments for the purposes of regulation 6 of the Principal Regulations. Secondly, they increase certain monetary limits in the Principal Regulations. Thirdly, they revoke certain of the qualifications for plan managers which will no longer be relevant on and after 29th April 1988.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Barnet Way, Barnet) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A1 Trunk Road (Barnet Way, Barnet) (Prescribed Routes) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Local Government (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1982 (Repeal of Local Acts) Order 1988
items.description: This Order repeals the provisions of local Acts passed before or in the same Session as the Local Government (Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 1982 which have become unnecessary in consequence of the provisions of that Act.

items.title: The Family Credit (General) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 with effect from 11 April 1988.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987 to provide for the disregard, in the calculation of income and capital, of charitable payments made to haemophiliacs under the Macfarlane Trust; and exempt a person from the requirement to furnish any information relating to such payments (regulations 1 to 6, 9 and 10). They also make provision for payments to be made on account of rent and rate rebates for a period before 30th June 1988 where it has been impracticable to determine a claim (regulation 8); and for notifications on determinations made on claims before that date to be in a shortened form (regulation 7).

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Supply of Information) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the information which the Secretary of State may supply to rating authorities, housing authorities or local authorities, which he has obtained by reason of the exercise of any of his functions under the benefit Acts relating to social security benefits, industrial injuries benefits and child benefit and which is required by those authorities in connection with their functions relating to housing benefit. They also prescribe the information obtained by authorities in exercise of their housing benefit functions, which is to be supplied to the Secretary of State, where required in connection with his functions under the benefit Acts.

items.title: The Income Support (General) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 with effect from 11th April 1988.

items.title: The Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, a draft of which has been laid before and approved by resolution of each House of Parliament, come into force on 11th April 1988 and replace, with amendments, the Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Regulations 1987 (S.I. 1987/491) and Part IV of the Supplementary Benefit (Trade Disputes and Recovery from Earnings) Regulations 1980 (S.I. 1980/1641) which cease to have effect.

items.title: The Land Registration Fee Order 1988
items.description: The Order, which supersedes the Land Registration Fee Order 1986 (“the 1986 Order”), restructures the land registration fee scales and makes certain other changes.

items.title: The Legal Advice and Assistance (Financial Conditions) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase the disposable income limit for the availability of legal advice and assistance under Part I of the Legal Aid Act 1974 from £118 to £122 a week and increase the disposable capital limit from £825 to £850.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Financial Conditions) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase the financial limits of eligibility for legal aid under Part I of the Legal Aid Act 1974.

items.title: The Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers' Compensation) (Payment of Claims) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 11th April 1988, prescribe the amount of payments to be made under the Pneumoconiosis etc. (Workers' Compensation) Act 1979 (“the Act”), as amended by the Social Security Act 1985 and the Social Security Act 1986, to persons disabled by a disease to which the Act applies (namely pneumoconiosis, bysinnosis, diffuse mesothelioma, primary carcinoma of the lung (where accompanied by asbestosis or bilateral diffuse pleural thickening) and bilateral diffuse pleural thickening) or to dependants of persons who immediately before they died were disabled by such a disease. All such persons should satisfy the conditions of entitlement prescribed in section 2 of the Act.

items.title: The Gipsy Encampments (Metropolitan Borough of Gateshead) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the Metropolitan Borough of Gateshead as an area to which section 10 of the Caravan Sites Act 1968 applies.

items.title: The Channel Tunnel Act (Competition) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision in connection with the approval by the Secretary of State by order under section 29 of the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 (as amended by section 33 of the Channel Tunnel Act 1987) of the channel tunnel agreement providing for the corporate structure of the Concessionaires.

items.title: The Income Support (Transitional) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (Transitional) Regulations 1987 in preparation for the introduction of income support. They provide for a person’s transitional addition, special transitional addition and personal expenses addition not to be reduced where there has been an increase in his income attributable to the receipt of a training allowance.

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Scotland only and which revoke and supersede the Seed Potatoes (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 1987, prescribe the fees payable in respect of certain matters arising under the Seed Potatoes Regulations 1984.

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Accounts and Statements) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insurance Companies (Accounts and Statements) Regulations 1983 by amending the definition of an authorised unit trust scheme (which referred to the Prevention of Fraud (Investments) Act 1958 (c. 45) and the Prevention of Fraud (Investments) Act (Northern Ireland) 1940 (c. 9 (N.I.)) which have been repealed by the Financial Services Act 1986) so that the expression now has the meaning given by the Financial Services Act 1986.

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the references in the Insurance Companies Regulations 1981 to unit trust schemes authorised under the Prevention of Fraud (Investments) Act 1958 (c. 45) and the Prevention of Fraud (Investments) Act (Northern Ireland) 1940 (c. 9 (N.I.)) and to a recognised stock exchange within the meaning of the companies legislation. These amendments are made in consequence of the repeal by the Financial Services Act 1986 of the 1958 and 1940 Acts and of the definition of a recognised stock exchange in the companies legislation and of the replacement of the repealed provisions by new provisions in the 1986 Act. The Insurance Companies Regulations 1981 as amended by these Regulations now refer to the equivalent new provisions in the 1986 Act.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations substitute regulation 89 of the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”), which modifies section 4(6) to 4(6E) (incidence of Class 1 contributions) of the Social Security Act 1975 (“the Act”) in relation to mariners.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions, Re-rating) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the amounts of weekly earnings specified in the earnings brackets in section 4(6B) and (6E) of the Social Security Act 1975, determining the appropriate percentage rate at which Class 1 contributions are payable under that Act (article 2). In both subsections, the increases in respect of Bracket 1 are from £64.99 to £69.99 and in respect of Bracket 2 from £65.00 and £99.99 to £70.00 and £104.99. In subsection (6B), the increase in respect of Bracket 3 is from £100 to £105. In subsection (6E), the increase in respect of Bracket 3 is from £100.00 and £149.99 to £105.00 and £154.99 and, in respect of Bracket 4, from £150.00 to £155.00.

items.title: The Social Security (Treasury Supplement to and Allocation of Contributions) (Re-rating) Order 1988
items.description: This Order decreases the Treasury supplement to contributions paid under the Social Security Act 1975 from 7 per cent. to 5 per cent. (article 2).

items.title: The Salmon and Freshwater Fisheries (Expenses) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales, provide for directions to be given by the Minister of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food or the Secretary of State for Wales, as appropriate, as to the payment of expenses of and incidental to public local inquiries held to consider applications for orders under section 28 of and Schedule 3 to the Salmon and Freshwater Fisheries Act 1975, and any other matters of procedure involved in making such orders.

items.title: The Channel Tunnel Act (Competition) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision in connection with the approval by the Secretary of State by order under section 29 of the Restrictive Trade Practices Act 1976 (as amended by section 33 of the Channel Tunnel Act 1987) of the channel tunnel agreement providing for the construction of the tunnel system.

items.title: The North West Water Authority (Knowsley Park Water Mains) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The North West Water Authority (Knowsley Park Water Mains) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Solicitors in the Sheriff Court) 1988
items.description: The Act of Sederunt revokes the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Solicitors in the Sheriff Court) 1986 as amended, re-enacts that Act with a number of minor amendments and increases the fees in the Table of Fees in the Schedule of the Act by 8 per cent overall.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Exchequer Causes) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt appoints Lord Coulsfield to be the Lord Ordinary in Exchequer Causes under section 2 of the Exchequer Court (Scotland) Act 1856.

items.title: The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Cod (Specified Sea Areas) (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No.2) (Solicitors' Fees) 1988
items.description: This Act of Sederunt substitutes new Chapters I and III of the Table in rule 347 of the Rules of the Court of Succession (fees of solicitors) with minor ammendments and increases the fees in these chapters by 8 per cent overall.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase the disposable income limit for eligibility for advice and assistance under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986 from £118 a week to £122 a week, the disposable capital limit from £825 to £850 and the weekly disposable income above which a person is required to pay a contribution from £56 to £58. They also prescribe the scale of contributions to be paid where the weekly disposable income exceeds £58 but does not exceed £122.

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase certain of the financial limits of eligibility for civil legal aid under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986.

items.title: The Registration of Births and Deaths (Welsh Language) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Registration of Births and Deaths (Welsh Language) Regulations 1987 (the main Regulations).

items.title: The Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Payments on account, Overpayments and Recovery) Regulations 1988. They correct minor errors in those Regulations and come into force on the same day as those Regulations.

items.title: The Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Social Security (Unemployment, Sickness and Invalidity Benefit) Amendment Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Reservoirs Act 1975 (Application Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Reservoirs Act 1975 (Supervising Engineers Panel) (Applications and Fees) Regulations 1984, the Reservoirs Act 1975 (All Reservoirs Panel) (Applications and Fees) Regulations 1985 and the Reservoirs Act 1975 (Non-Impounding and Service Reservoirs Panels) (Applications and Fees) Regulations 1985 prescribed the fees which are to accompany applications by civil engineers to become members of the panels constituted for the purposes of the Reservoirs Act 1975. Those fees have been increased by the Reservoirs Act 1975 (Application Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Order 1987 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Cheshire (District Boundaries) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers an area within the county of Cheshire from the district of Vale Royal and parish of Delamere to the City of Chester and parish of Kelsall. Consequential changes to local government electoral areas are made.

items.title: The Medicines (Hormone Growth Promoters) (Prohibition of Use) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations re-enact the Medicines (Hormone Growth Promoters) (Prohibition of Use) Regulations 1986 which were made to implement in part Council Directive 85/649/EEC which was declared void by the Court of Justice of the European Communities in Case 68/86 on 23 February 1988.

items.title: The Public Companies (Disclosure of Interests in Shares) (Investment Management Exclusion) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations specify an interest in shares which is to be disregarded for the purposes of sections 198 to 202 of the Companies Act 1985. Those sections require the disclosure in certain circumstances of interests in shares comprised in certain share capital of public companies. The effect of these Regulations is that an interest in shares listed on the Stock Exchange, designated on its Unlisted Securities Market or traded under Rule 535(3) of its Rules need not be disclosed if the interest is held by a person authorised to carry on investment business under the Financial Services Act 1986 (regulation 3(b)), and if it arises only as a result of disposal powers conferred on him by an investment management agreement (regulation 3(c)).

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) (No. 2) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) (No.2) Order 1985 (“the principal Order”) insofar as it provides for the exemption of certain consumer credit agreements secured on land where the creditor is a body specified in the Schedule to the principal Order, a building society authorised under the Building Societies Act 1986, or an authorised institution under the Banking Act 1987 or a wholly-owned subsidiary of such an institution. The Order provides for the exemption of debtor-creditor agreements secured by a land mortgage made by such a creditor to finance the alteration, enlarging, repair or improvement of a dwelling, where the agreement is made as a result of services certified as having been provided by certain bodies listed in the Order.

items.title: The Derbyshire (District Boundaries) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Derbyshire (District Boundaries) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Free Zone (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: The amendment to regulation 6 of the 1984 Regulations will permit the Commissioners to determine by directions the time limit in which goods moved into a free zone shall become “free zone goods” and to extend that period, by directions, in the circumstances of particular cases.

items.title: The Asbestos (Prohibitions) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations implement, as respects Great Britain, Council Directive No. 85/610/EEC (OJ No. L375, 31.12.85, p.1) which amends Council Directive No. 76/769/EEC (OJ No. L262, 27.9.76, p.201) on the marketing and use of asbestos. They do so by amending the Asbestos (Prohibitions) Regulations 1985 (S.I. 1985/910).

items.title: The Health and Safety (Fees) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations update and replace the Health and Safety (Fees) Regulations 1987 (Schedule 12). They fix or determine the fees payable by an applicant to the Health and Safety Executive in respect of an application made for—an approval under mines and quarries legislation (regulation 2 and Schedule 1);an approval of certain respiratory protective equipment, blasting helmets, and of automatic safe load indicators (regulation 3 and Schedule 2);an approval of plant or equipment under the Agriculture (Tractor Cabs) Regulations 1974 and the Poisonous Substances in Agriculture Regulations 1984 (regulation 4 and Schedule 3);an approval of a scheme or programme under the Freight Containers (Safety Convention) Regulations 1984 (regulation 5 and Schedule 4);a licence under the Asbestos (Licensing) Regulations 1983 (regulation 6 and Schedule 5);an approval of dosimetry services and for type approval of radiation generators or apparatus containing radioactive substances under the Ionising Radiations Regulations 1985 (regulation 9 and Schedule 8);an approval, authorisation or licence etc. under the Explosives Act 1875 and certain instruments thereunder, for a licence under the Petroleum (Consolidation) Act 1928, for the transfer of a licence under the Petroleum (Transfer of Licences) Act 1936, and for the purpose of each of the exemption orders made under the Gas Cylinders (Conveyance) Regulations 1931 (regulation 10 and Schedule 9). The Home Office Specifications referred to in Part V of Schedule 9 are obtainable from the Health and Safety Executive, Technical Division 2A, Mechanical Systems Unit, Magdalen House, Stanley Precinct, Bootle, Merseyside L20 3QZ;testing for the purpose of the classification of an article, substance, combination, or unit load under the Classification and Labelling of Explosives Regulations 1983 (regulation 12 and Schedule 10);an explosives licence under Part IX of the Dangerous Substances in Harbour Areas Regulations 1987 (regulation 13 and Schedule 11).

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property) (Greater Manchester) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers to the Manchester City Council the properties specified in its article 2 together with rights and liabilities ancillary to those properties. The properties were formerly owned by the Greater Manchester County Council and, on the abolition of that council by the Local Government Act 1985, were transferred to the Greater Manchester Residuary Body under that Act.

items.title: The Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Milk Quota (Calculation of Standard Quota) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency Act 1988 (Commencement) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 12th April 1988 the Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency Act 1988.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Investment Advertisements) (Exemptions) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order makes provision for certain exemptions from the requirement imposed by section 57 of the Financial Services Act 1986 that no person other than a person authorised under that Act may issue or cause to be issued an investment advertisement in the United Kingdom unless its contents have been approved by an authorised person.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Stabilisation) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends section 48(7) of the Financial Services Act 1986 so as to extend the scope of the exception to the offence in section 47(2) of that Act concerning market manipulation. As a result of the amendment, anything done for the purpose of stabilising the price of investments will not be a contravention of section 47(2) if it is done in conformity with rules made under section 48 of that Act, is in respect of an investment of the kind specified and is done during the specified period before or after an offer of those investments which is of the kind specified.

items.title: The Cowes Harbour Revision Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Cowes Harbour Revision Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Miscellaneous Exemptions) (No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for certain exemptions from the provisions of the Financial Services Act 1986 additional to those specified in Chapter IV of Part I of that Act and in the Financial Services Act 1986 (Miscellaneous Exemptions) Order 1988 (SI 1988/350). The persons specified in Schedule 1 to the Order are exempted from the requirement to obtain authorisation in respect of any investment business they carry on. The persons specified in Schedule 2 to the Order are granted more limited exemptions.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Occupational Pension Schemes) (No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order revokes and consolidates with certain amendments the Financial Services Act 1986 (Occupational Pension Schemes) Order 1988. The Order directs that no trustee of an occupational pension scheme of a kind described in article 3 is to be treated as carrying on investment business by virtue of section 191 of the Financial Services Act 1986 if either he is a beneficiary or potential beneficiary under the scheme in question or he takes no day to day decisions relating to the management of assets of the scheme which are investments other, in the case of a scheme of the kind described in article 3(3), than the selection of life insurance and annuity contracts. The Order also makes provision as to schemes where some of the members in respect of whom contributions have been made are no longer entitled to benefits under the scheme and schemes under which investment decisions are taken by a person of the kind described in section 191 of the Act acting jointly with all, or a majority of, relevant members.

items.title: The Kinneil and Manuel Light Railway Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Kinneil and Manuel Light Railway Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Compensation for Restrictions on Mineral Working) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: The Town and Country Planning (Compensation for Restrictions on Mineral Working) Regulations 1985 specify methods of calculating the annual value of the right to win and work minerals for the purpose of assessing compensation in certain situations where restrictions on permission for mining are imposed. The calculation includes a factor relating to the base of the Retail Price Index. The Index was rebased on 1st January 1987. These Regulations amend the factor to take account mathematically of the rebasing.

items.title: The Police (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”) with effect from 23rd May 1988.

items.title: The Police Cadets (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend Regulation 5 of the Police Cadets Regulations 1979 so as to provide that, in the case of the metropolitan police force, the chief officer’s power to terminate a police cadet’s service is also exercisable by an assistant commissioner of police and the function of personally interviewing a cadet prior to such termination (currently exercisable by the chief officer or an assistant commissioner of police) is also exercisable by a deputy commissioner of police or a commander. The reference to the City of London police force, which no longer has police cadets, is omitted.

items.title: The Rate Limitation (Designation of Authorities) (Exemption) Order 1988
items.description: Section 2(1) of the Rates Act 1984 provides that an authority in whose case a maximum rate or precept is to be prescribed in any financial year is to be designated by the Secretary of State in the preceding financial year.

items.title: The (A174) Parkway Interchange to ICI Westgate Roundabout (Trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A174) Parkway Interchange to ICI Westgate Roundabout (Trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A13 Trunk Road (Newham, Barking and Dagenham, and Havering) (Speed Limits) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A13 Trunk Road (Newham, Barking and Dagenham, and Havering) (Speed Limits) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Parsley (Temporary Prohibition on Landing) (Great Britain) Order 1988
items.description: This Order prohibits until 19th June 1988 the landing in Great Britain of parsley grown in Italy, on account of recent findings of Colorado Beetle.

items.title: The A406 Trunk Road (South Woodford to Barking Relief Road, Redbridge, Newham, Barking and Dagenham) (Speed Limits) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A406 Trunk Road (South Woodford to Barking Relief Road, Redbridge, Newham, Barking and Dagenham) (Speed Limits) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Delegation) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers certain of the Secretary of State’s functions under the Financial Services Act 1986 to the body known as The Securities and Investments Board. The functions in question are described in article 3 to the Order. The functions under section 94 of the Act are transferred to The Securities and Investments Board subject to a reservation that they are to be exercisable concurrently by the Secretary of State.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Commencement) (No. 8) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 29th April 1988 provisions of the Financial Services Act 1986 concerning the criminal and civil sanctions applicable if a person carries on investment business in the United Kingdom without authorisation or exemption under the Act. The Order also brings into force on the same date certain provisions of the Act concerning the regulation of investment business and of persons authorised to carry on that business.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates Texaco North Sea UK Company as the owner of the pipe-lines described in the Schedule, which lie above and between a number of offshore oilfields situated east of the Highland Region of Scotland.

items.title: House of Commons Member’s Fund Resolution 1988
The Commissioners for Oaths (Fees) Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the fees chargeable by commissioners for oaths and practising solicitors for taking affidavits and similar declarations from £3.00 to £3.50 and for marking exhibits from 75p to £1.00. The increased amounts include value added tax where payable.

items.title: The Finance (No. 2) Act 1987 (Commencement) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings section 81 of the Finance (No. 2) Act 1987 (“the section”) into force on 29th April 1988.

items.title: The Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (Appointed Day) Order 1988
items.description: This Order appoints 29th April 1988 as the day on which section 470(2) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988, which substitutes a definition of “authorised unit trust” for that contained in section 468(6) of that Act, and omits the definition of “unit trust scheme” from sections 468 and 832 of that Act, shall cease to have effect.

items.title: The Clydebank and City of Glasgow Districts (Yoker) Boundaries Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers areas in the vicinity of Yoker between Clydebank District and City of Glasgow District and makes consequential changes to electoral arrangements.

items.title: The Prison (Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules extend Part I of the Prison Rules 1964, so as to apply references in that Part to prison officers (other than governors) to constables or members of the armed forces when employed in an emergency to assist the governor of a prison in performing duties ordinarily performed by prison officers.

items.title: The International Development Association (Eighth Replenishment) Order 1988
items.description: The Overseas Development and Co-operation Act 1980, section 6(2), provides that if the Government of the United Kingdom becomes bound by arrangements for the making of additional payments to the International Development Association, the Secretary of State may, with the approval of the Treasury, provide by order for the payment out of money provided by Parliament of any sums required by him for any of the purposes specified in that subsection.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property) (West Yorkshire) Order 1988
items.description: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property) (West Yorkshire) Orders 1987 (S.I. 1987/15 and 1987/1451) transferred specified property formerly vested in West Yorkshire County Council and in the West Yorkshire Residuary Body to certain of the district councils in that County. This Order effects further transfers of the same kind.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest on Unpaid Tax and Repayment Supplement) Order 1988
items.description: On and after 6th May 1988 this Order reduces from 8.25 per cent. per annum to 7.75 per cent. per annum the rate of interest chargeable on unpaid income tax, surtax, capital gains tax, corporation tax (including advance corporation tax), development land tax, petroleum revenue tax, profits tax, excess profits tax, excess profits levy and on overpaid development land tax. The Order also provides that on and after 6th May 1988 supplement in respect of repayments of certain taxes will be at the rate of 7.75 per cent. per annum instead of 8.25 per cent. per annum.

items.title: The Income Tax (Official Rate of Interest on Beneficial Loans) Order 1988
items.description: By this Order the official rate of interest for the purposes of section 160 of, and Schedule 7 to, the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (charge to income tax on beneficial loans) is prescribed to be 9.5 per cent. per annum on and after 6th May 1988.

items.title: The Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Interest on Tax Repaid) Order 1988
items.description: Section 92 of the Finance Act 1986 provides that, if certain conditions have been fulfilled and a claim for repayment is made within the stipulated period, stamp duty reserve tax shall be repaid. Where the tax paid is not less than £25, it shall be repaid with interest at the appropriate rate. The section defines the “appropriate rate” as 11 per cent. per annum or such other rate as the Treasury may from time to time specify by order. This Order specifies that on and after 6th May 1988 the appropriate rate shall be 7.75 per cent. per annum. Prior to that date the rate was 9 per cent. on and after 6th April 1987 (S.I. 1987/514), 8.25 per cent. on and after 6th June 1987 (S.I. 1987/888), 9 per cent. on and after 6th September 1987 (S.I. 1987/1494) and 8.25 per cent. on and after 6th December 1987 (S.I. 1987/1990).

items.title: The Community Drivers' Hours and Recording Equipment (Exemptions and Supplementary Provisions) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Community Drivers' Hours and Recording Equipment (Exemptions and Supplementary Provisions) Regulations 1986 by providing for an additional exemption from the provisions (except Article 5) of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3820/85 of 20th December 1985 (O.J. No. L370, 31.12.85, p.1) on the harmonisation of certain social legislation relating to road transport (“the Community Drivers' Hours Regulation”) and from the provisions of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3821/85 of 20th December 1985 on recording equipment in road transport (“The Community Recording Equipment Regulation”).

items.title: The A405 North Orbital Road (Long Lane Roundabout — A412) and Connecting Roads Detrunking Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A405 North Orbital Road (Long Lane Roundabout — A412) and Connecting Roads Detrunking Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Chester-Bangor Trunk Road (Conwy Morfa to Llanfairfechan, Pen-y-Clip Section) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Chester-Bangor Trunk Road (Conwy Morfa to Llanfairfechan, Pen-y-Clip Section) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Cubic Measures (Ballast and Agricultural Materials) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Cubic Measures (Ballast and Agricultural Materials) (Amendment) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Classification, Packaging and Labelling of Dangerous Substances (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Classification, Packaging and Labelling of Dangerous Substances (Amendment) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Conservation Measures) Order 1986 (“the 1986 Order”), which makes provision for the enforcement of certain of the enforceable Community restrictions and obligations concerning technical measures for the conservation of fishery resources which are contained in Council Regulation (EEC) No 3094/86 (“the Council Regulation”).

items.title: The A6 Trunk Road (The Leicester City Boundary to the Southern District Distributor Road) Detrunking Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A6 Trunk Road (The Leicester City Boundary to the Southern District Distributor Road) Detrunking Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Canadian Navigation Services) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Aviation (Canadian Navigation Services) Regulations 1986, by increasing the charges for air navigation services provided by or on behalf of the Government of Canada as follows:

items.title: The (A49) Shrewsbury to Whitchurch to Warrington Trunk Road (Improvement at Holloway Mouth) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A49) Shrewsbury to Whitchurch to Warrington Trunk Road (Improvement at Holloway Mouth) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Building Societies (Supplementary Capital) Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies supplementary capital as a description of capital resource of building societies.

items.title: The Ionising Radiation (Protection of Persons Undergoing Medical Examination or Treatment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations implement, as respects Great Britain, the provisions of Council Directive 84/466/Euratom (OJ No. L265, 5.10.84, p.1) laying down basic measures for the radiation protection of persons undergoing medical examination or treatment.

items.title: The North West Water Authority (Wilmslow and Alderley Edge) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The North West Water Authority (Wilmslow and Alderley Edge) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Finance Act 1987 (Commencement No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 29th April 1988 the only provision of the Finance Act 1987 (section 49(1) ) which is not already in force or which has not been repealed.

items.title: The Income Tax (Interest Relief) (Qualifying Lenders) Order 1988
items.description: By article 2, the bodies named in article 3 are prescribed as qualifying lenders so that interest on qualifying loans made by them may be paid to them under deduction of tax under the tax deduction scheme (introduced by the Finance Act 1982 (c. 39)) from the dates specified in the Order.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Property) (West Midlands) Order 1988
items.description: This Order transfers property, rights and liabilities formerly vested in the West Midlands County Council to the councils of districts in that county and to the West Midlands Police Authority. The transfers effected are in addition to those effected on 22nd July 1987 by S.I. 1987/1077.

items.title: The Veterinary Surgeons (Agreement with the Republic of Ireland) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which is made under section 21 of the Veterinary Surgeons Act 1966 (“the Act”), gives effect to an Agreement dated 11th April 1988 (“the 1988 Agreement”) entered into between the Governments of the United Kingdom and the Republic of Ireland concerning in particular the payment of fees for membership of the Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons (“the Royal College”) by holders of certain veterinary qualifications obtained in the Irish Republic (article 2).

items.title: The European Communities (Designation) Order 1988 (revoked)
items.description: This is a further Order in the series of Orders designating the Minister who, and departments which, may exercise powers to make regulations conferred by section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and specifying the matters in relation to which those powers may be exercised.

items.title: The Antarctic Treaty (Contracting Parties) Order 1988
items.description: This Order adds Austria, Ecuador, Greece and North Korea to the list of Contracting Parties to the Antarctic Treaty, certified as such for the purposes of the Antarctic Treaty Act 1967.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Confirmation of Legislation) (Gibraltar) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, made under section 735(1) of the Merchant Shipping Act 1894, confirms an Ordinance enacted by the House of Assembly of Gibraltar which extends to Gibraltar the Merchant Shipping (Certification of Deck Officers) Regulation 1985 (S.I. 1985/1306), the Merchant Shipping (Certification of Marine Engineer Officers and Licensing of Marine Engine Operators) Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/1935), the Merchant Shipping (Certification and Watchkeeping) Regulations 1982 (S.I. 1982/1699) and the Merchant Shipping (Tankers—Officers and Ratings) Regulations 1984 (S.I. 1984/94), which also applies in relation to Gibraltar-registered tankers. The Ordinance also amends the Merchant Shipping Ordinance provision concerning the Safety of Life at Sea and extends to Gibraltar-registered ships and other ships in Gibraltar the provisions of the Merchant Shipping (Load Lines) Act 1967 (c. 27) and the load line rules and the Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Oil Pollution) Regulations 1983 (S.I. 1983/1398).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Oil Pollution) (Bermuda) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends to Bermuda certain provisions of the Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Oil Pollution) Order 1983. Its main purpose is to give the Governor of Bermuda power to make regulations to enable effect to be given in Bermuda to the International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships 1973 (Cmnd. 5748), the Protocol of 1978 (Cmnd. 7347) relating to the Convention, and amendments to the Protocol adopted by resolution MEPC 14(20) on 7th September 1984 by the Marine Environment Protection Committee of the International Maritime Organization at its 20th Session (which resolution may be obtained from IMO, 4 Albert Embankment, London SE1 7SR).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1974 (Cayman Islands) Order 1988
items.description: This Order permits the extension to the Cayman Islands of section 19 of the Merchant Shipping Act 1974, which amends the provisions of the Merchant Shipping Act 1970 (c. 36).

items.title: The South West Water Authority (River Wolf Discharge) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The South West Water Authority (River Wolf Discharge) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping Act 1979 (Cayman Islands) Order 1988
items.description: This Order permits the extension to the Cayman Islands of certain provisions of the Merchant Shipping Act 1979 which amend the provisions of the Merchant Shipping Act 1970 (c. 36). Level 5 of the standard scale of fines is at present five thousand Cayman dollars.

items.title: The Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency (Overseas Territories) Order 1988
items.description: This Order extends the provisions of sections 3, 4, 5 and 6 of the Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency Act, subject to modifications and exceptions, to the Territories specified in Schedule 2 hereto.

items.title: The Treaty on the Elimination of Intermediate-Range and Shorter-Range Missiles (Inspections) (Privileges and Immunities) Order 1988
items.description: This Order confers privileges and immunities on inspectors and aircrew members, as well as the inspection equipment and aircraft used by them, whilst they are present in the United Kingdom to carry out the necessary inspections to verify the implementation of the Treaty between the United States of America and the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics on the Elimination of their Intermediate-Range and Shorter-Range Missiles. These privileges and immunities are required for giving effect in the United Kingdom to the Agreement among the United States of America and the Kingdom of Belgium, the Federal Republic of Germany, the Republic of Italy, the Kingdom of the Netherlands and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland regarding Inspections relating to the aforementioned Treaty between the United States and the Soviet Union.

items.title: The Copyright (Sound Recordings) (Indonesia) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for the copyright protection in the United Kingdom of sound recordings originating in Indonesia.

items.title: The High Court of Justiciary Fees Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the fees payable to the Principal Clerk of Session and Justiciary or any officer acting for him in relation to proceedings in the High Court of Justiciary, other than the fees payable for copying and certain of the search fees. The previous Order increasing fees is revoked.

items.title: The Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) (No. 2) Regulations 1982 which prescribe charges for some overseas visitors for services forming part of the national health service.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths, Marriages and Divorces (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe, with effect from 1st April 1988, the fees payable to the Registrar General and district registrars for services provided under the Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Scotland) Act 1965. The fees for the first, second, fourth and thirteenth to sixteenth services listed in Schedule 1 and for the first to third and sixth services listed in Schedule 2 are increased. The fifth, sixth and seventh services in Schedule 1 and fifth, seventh, eighth and ninth services in Schedule 2 introduce for the first time reduced fees where abbreviated certificates are obtained in certain circumstances. The eleventh service in Schedule 2 has been changed from £7 per day or part to £2.50 per hour or part. The fees for the other services are not changed. The Registration of Births, Deaths, Marriages and Divorces (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 1986 are revoked.

items.title: The Cosmetic Products (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Cosmetic Products (Safety) Regulations 1984 as amended by the Cosmetic Products (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 1985 and 1987 (“the principal Regulations”) and implement Commission Directive No. 87/137/EEC (O.J. L56, 26.2.87, p.20) by extending the ban on the use of N—(Trichloromethylthio)—4—cyclohexene—1, 2—dicarboximide (captan); 2, 2'—Dihydroxy—3, 3', 5, 5', 6, 6'—hexachlorodiphenylmethane (hexachlorophene) and 6—(piperidinyl)—2,4—pyrimidinediamine—3—oxide (minoxidil) and its salts and derivatives to the substances listed in Schedule 1 to the principal Regulations which may not be contained in cosmetic product (regulation 3(b)).

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Restriction of Scope of Act and Meaning of Collective Investment Scheme) Order 1988
items.description: This Order restricts the scope of the Financial Services Act 1986 in certain respects. Article 2 restricts the scope of paragraph 12 of Schedule 1 to the Act by excluding from that paragraph the acceptance of instruments creating or acknowledging indebtedness in respect of certain financial transactions. Article 3 restricts the scope of paragraph 13 of Schedule 1 to the Act in several ways. First, it excludes arrangements with a view to a person accepting an instrument creating or acknowledging indebtedness in respect of certain financial transactions. Second, it excludes loans made for the specific purpose of financing investment transactions. Third, it excludes arrangements for the introduction of persons to certain other persons if the introduction is with a view to independent advice being given or independent discretion being exercised. Article 4 restricts the scope of the Act by extending the exemption provided by paragraph 24 of Schedule 1 so that it covers certain arrangements made in the course of a profession or a business which is not investment business. Article 5 amends the references to a collective investment scheme in Schedule 1 and elsewhere in the Act so that they do not include references to business expansion schemes which meet the conditions described in the article. Article 5 also has the effect that the provisions of the Act relating to collective investment schemes do not extend to certain arrangements under which the rights or interests of participants are represented by investments of a kind described in the article or are rights to or interests in money held in a common account in the circumstances described in the article.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 3) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie above the Montrose oilfield, east of the coast of Scotland.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 4) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie betweeen offshore installations above the Indefatigable gasfield, east of the coast of Lincolnshire.

items.title: The Crown Prosecution Service (Witnesses' Allowances) (Amendment No. 7) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations increase the maximum financial loss allowances payable under regulation 6 of the Crown Prosecution Service (Witnesses' Allowances) Regulations 1986 to a witness other than a professional or expert witness who, at the instance of the Crown Prosecution Service, attends court to give evidence and thereby incurs certain expenditure or suffers certain loss as described in that regulation.

items.title: The National Health Service (Joint Ophthalmic Committees) (Scotland) Revocation Order 1988
items.description: This Order abolishes Joint Ophthalmic Committees which were formed to exercise on behalf of groups of Scottish Health Boards certain functions relating to the provision by such Boards of general ophthalmic services under the National Health Service. The functions of those Committees (which relate to the maintenance of ophthalmic lists, and the investigation of complaints against opticians and of excessive prescribing of optical applicances) will consequently revert to individual Health Boards on 1st June 1988.

items.title: The Excise Warehousing (Etc.) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st June 1988, replace the Excise Warehousing (Etc.) Regulations 1982 and the Excise Warehousing (Etc.) (Amendment) Regulations 1986.

items.title: The Anatomy Act 1984 (Commencement) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Anatomy Act 1984 (Commencement) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Transit of Animals (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Transit of Animals (General) Order 1973 (“the 1973 Order”) and the Transit of Animals (Road and Rail) Order 1975 (“the 1975 Order”), thereby implementing certain requirements of Council Directive 77/489/EEC (OJ No. L200, 8.8.77, p.10) on the protection of animals during international transport, in respect of which those Orders do not at present make specific provision.

items.title: The Teachers' Superannuation (Miscellaneous Provisions) (No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Teachers' Superannuation Regulations 1976 (“the principal Regulations”) and also make provision for membership of the scheme constituted by the principal Regulations to be voluntary.

items.title: The Court Funds (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Court Funds Rules 1987 so as to require the crediting of accrued interest to funds held in court to take place always on the last Friday in an accounting cycle. They also remove reference to approved forms, and make a number of minor amendments for the sake of clarity.

items.title: The Control of Pollution Act 1974 (Commencement No. 19) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into Operation provisions of Part I of the Control of Pollution Act 1974 relating to the collection and disposal of waste and the providing of receptacles for waste. It also effects the repeal of certain provisions of the Public Health Act 1936 and other Acts relating to those matters.

items.title: The Collection and Disposal of Waste Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to England and Wales only, make provision for various matters concerning the collection and disposal of controlled waste under Part I of the Control of Pollution Act 1974 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Housing Defects (Expenditure Limits) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Housing Defects (Expenditure Limits) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The South West Water Authority (River Lew Discharge) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The South West Water Authority (River Lew Discharge) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A3 Trunk Road (Service Roads, Kingston upon Thames and Merton) (Restricted Roads) (Direction) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A3 Trunk Road (Service Roads, Kingston upon Thames and Merton) (Restricted Roads) (Direction) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations are all made under the Social Security Act 1986, and are made before the end of a period of 12 months from the commencement of the relevant provisions of that Act. Consequently, by virtue of section 61(5) of that Act, the provisions of section 61(2) and (3) of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (as amended by section 86(1) of, and paragraph 94 of Schedule 10 to, the Social Security Act 1986), which require reference to the Occupational Pensions Board of, and a report by the Board on, proposals to make regulations for certain purposes of both Acts, do not apply to them.

items.title: The Public Telecommunication System Designation (City Centre Cable) Order 1988
items.description: The Secretary of State granted to City Centre Cable on 3rd May 1988 a licence under section 7 of the Telecommunications Act 1984 to run the telecommunication systems specified in Annex A to that licence, in the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea. A copy of the licence was laid before Parliament on 9th May 1988. This Order designates those telecommunication systems as public telecommunication systems. Consequently, by virtue of section 9(3) of that Act, City Centre Cable will be a public telecommunications operator when the Order comes into force.

items.title: The County of Gwynedd (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The County of Gwynedd (Electoral Arrangements) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Stamp Duty Reserve Tax Regulations 1986 (“the Principal Regulations”), which provide for the management of stamp duty reserve tax imposed by Part IV of the Finance Act 1986 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Meat and Livestock Commission Levy (Variation) Scheme (Confirmation) Order 1988
items.description: By this Order the Ministers confirm a variation to the Meat and Livestock Commission Levy Scheme 1987 confirmed by the Meat and Livestock Commission Levy Scheme (Confirmation) Order 1987 (S.I. 1987/1303).

items.title: The Home Purchase Assistance (Recognised Lending Institutions) (No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order adds five additional bodies to the list in section 447(1) of the Housing Act 1985. Such bodies are lending institutions recognised for the purposes of section 445 of the Act and may receive advances from the Secretary of State to enable them to provide assistance to first-timer purchasers of house property (the “Homeloan” Scheme).

items.title: The Secure Accommodation (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Secure Accommodation (Scotland) Regulations 1983 (“the principal Regulations”) which are concerned with the provision and use in Scotland of secure accommodation for restricting the liberty of children in certain circumstances. The amendments take account of changes resulting from the Social Work (Residential Establishments-Child Care) (Scotland) Regulations 1987 (“the 1987 Regulations”) which also come into force on 1st June 1988.

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Excise) (Prescribed Particulars) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Excise) (Prescribed Particulars) Regulations 1981.

items.title: The Animals and Fresh Meat (Examination for Residues) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout the United Kingdom, come into force on 1st May 1988. They implement Articles 8, 9 and 10 of Council Directive 86/469/EEC (OJ No. L275, 26.9.86, p.36) concerning the examination of animals and fresh meat for the presence of residues, Article 8.1(a) of which provides for sampling to be carried out officially in the Member States in accordance with common criteria for the different groups of substances of concern. Articles 3, 4, 5 and 6 of Council Directive 85/358/EEC (OJ No. 191, 23.7.85, p.46) supplementing Directive 81/602/EEC concerning the prohibition of certain substances having hormonal action and of any substances having a thyrostatic action, are also implemented by these Regulations, as modified by regulation 6 of the Animals and Fresh Meat (Hormonal Substances) Regulations 1988 (S.I. 1988/849).

items.title: The Animals and Fresh Meat (Hormonal Substances) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which apply throughout the United Kingdom, come into force on 1st May 1988. They implement in part Council Directive 81/602/EEC (OJ No. L222, 7.8.81, p.32) concerning the prohibition of certain substances having a hormonal action and of any substances having a thyrostatic action, Council Directive 85/358/EEC (OJ No. L191, 23.7.85, p.46) supplementing Directive 81/602/EEC and Council Directive 88/146/EEC (OJ No. L70, 16.3.88, p.16) prohibiting the use in livestock farming of certain substances having a hormonal action.

items.title: The Housing (Right to Buy) (Priority of Charges) (No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: Under the Housing Act 1985 (“the 1985 Act”) liability to repay discount following the exercise of the right to buy is secured by a charge on the dwelling-house. Such charges do not have priority over certain charges securing advances by approved lending institutions.

items.title: The Welfare of Poultry (Transport) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which applies throughout Great Britain, contains provisions relating to the welfare of poultry during their transport by road, rail, air and water which replace (1) provisions contained in the Conveyance of Live Poultry Order of 1919 which are revoked and (2) provisions contained in the Transit of Animals Order 1973, as amended, which are disapplied by this Order. The Order also implements the provisions of Council Directive 77/489/EEC (OJ No. L200, 8.8.77, p.10) on the protection of animals during international transport which relate to the transport of poultry in respect of which those Orders do not at present make specific provision.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Dental Services) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 which make arrangements for the provision by dentists of general dental services under the National Health Service. These Regulations extend the scope of the Statement of Dental Remuneration, so as to make provision for allowances to dentists providing general dental services who train recently registered dentists.

items.title: The Patents (Fees) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Patents (Fees) Rules 1987. The fees payable under the Patents Act 1977 on forms 1/77, 9/77, 10/77, 12/77, 24/77, 40/77, 41/77 and 44/77, and in respect of international applications for patents are increased with effect from 23rd June 1988, except in the case of fees payable for the renewal of a patent in respect of any year beginning on or after 23rd June 1988, which are increased with effect from 9th June 1988. The fees payable on form 46/77 under the Patents Act 1977 and on form 13 under the Patents Act 1949 are abolished. It is estimated that the total amount of fees collected per annum at the new levels will be 4.5 per cent greater than the amount collected per annum at the existing levels.

items.title: The Registered Designs (Fees) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Registered Designs (Fees) Rules 1986. The fees payable on forms Designs Nos. 2, 3A (in some circumstances), 9, 10 and 23 are increased with effect from 23rd June 1988, except in the case of fees paid in advance for the extension of the period of copyright in a design for any period beginning on or after 23rd June 1988, which are increased with effect from 9th June 1988. The fee payable on form Designs No. 27 is abolished. It is estimated that the total amount of fees collected per annum at the new levels will be 3.5% greater than the amount collected per annum at the existing levels.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The North Surrey Water Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The North Surrey Water Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The National Health Service Functions (Directions to Authorities and Administration Arrangements) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service Functions (Directions to Authorities and Administration Arrangements) Regulations 1982. Regulation 2 allows a Family Practitioner Committee to exercise any functions of a District Health Authority under section 103(1) of the National Health Service Act 1977 (which concerns special arrangements for remuneration).

items.title: The National Health Service (Payment of Remuneration — Special Arrangement) Order 1988
items.description: This Order enables a District Health Authority or a special health authority to remunerate chemists and others providing pharmaceutical services who have supplied drugs, medicines or listed appliances which have been ordered by a doctor in pursuance of the authority’s functions under the National Health Service Act 1977.

items.title: The National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services and Charges for Drugs) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Medical and Pharmaceutical Services) Regulations 1974 (the 1974 Regulations) and the National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) Regulations 1980 (the 1980 Regulations).

items.title: The Social Security Revaluation of Earnings Factors Order 1988
items.description: This Order is made consequent upon a review under section 21 of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 (“the Pensions Act”), which provides for the revaluation of earnings factors so that they maintain their value in relation to the general level of earnings obtaining in Great Britain.

items.title: The Domestic Courts (Constitution) (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Domestic Courts (Constitution) Rules 1979. Under the 1979 Rules domestic court panels are appointed for petty sessions area every three years, but a magistrates' courts committee may make a direction for the formation or dissolution of a combined panel in respect of two or more petty sessions areas to take effect at the beginning of each three year period. These Rules enable such a direction to take effect during a current three year period of appointment, and provide that the members of a panel formed under such a direction will be appointed in the first instance until the end of the current three year period and thereafter every three years.

items.title: The Domestic Courts (Constitution) (Inner London) (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Domestic Courts (Constitution) (Inner London) Rules 1979. Under the 1979 Rules domestic court panels are appointed for a petty sessions area every three years, but a magistrates' courts committee may make a direction for the formation or dissolution of a combined panel in respect of two or more petty sessions areas to take effect at the beginning of each three year period. These Rules enable such a direction to take effect during a current three year period of appointment, and provide that the members of a panel formed by such a direction will be appointed in the first instance until the end of the current three year period and thereafter every three years.

items.title: The Mortgage Indemnities (Recognised Bodies) (No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order specifies five additional bodies as recognised bodies for the purposes of sections 442 and 443 of the Act. Local authorities may, with the approval of the Secretary of State, enter into agreements with recognised bodies providing for indemnities, where such bodies make advances secured by way of mortgage on dwelling-houses bought from the public sector landlords listed in section 444(4) of the Act

items.title: The Matrimonial Causes Fees (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that the exemption from paying fees in matrimonial proceedings granted to recipients of supplementary benefit and family income supplement is to apply to recipients of income support and family credit under the Social Security Act 1986, which replaced the previous benefits on 11th April 1988.

items.title: The Building (Amendment of Prescribed Fees) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Building (Prescribed Fees etc) Regulations 1985 so as to increase the flat rate fees payable to a local authority—1by approximately 7% in respect of the erection of small domestic building and connected work (Schedule 1 to the 1985 Regulations), and2by approximately 10% in respect of the erection of certain garages, carports and the making of small extensions and alterations to domestic buildings (Schedule 2 to the 1985 Regulations).

items.title: The Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations reduce from 11 per cent. to 9¼ per cent. per annum, in respect of any period after the coming into force of these Regulations, the rate of interest payable where entry is made, before payment of compensation, on land in England and Wales which is being purchased compulsorily, and revoke the Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (No. 3) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Aquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations reduce from 11 per cent. to 9¼ per cent. per annum, in respect of any period after the coming into force of these Regulations, the rate of interest payable where entry is made, before payment of compensation, on land in Scotland which is being purchased compulsorily, and revoke the Acquisition of Land (Rate of Interest after Entry) (Scotland) (No.3) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Weighing Equipment (Non-automatic Weighing Machines) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations prescribe non-automatic weighing machines for the purposes of section 11(1) of the Weights and Measures Act 1985 and exclude them from the application of the Weights and Measures Regulations 1963 (where they were formerly prescribed as individual types or classes of weighing equipment). The effect of prescription is to make it unlawful to use such machines for trade purposes, unless they have been tested, passed as fit for such use and stamped by an inspector of weights and measures. (These requirements of prescription are also satisfied by a non-automatic weighing machine complying with Council Directive No. 73/360/EEC as amended and bearing the mark of EEC initial verification and, where appropriate, the sign of EEC pattern approval. This mark and sign are described in Schedule 1 to the Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) Regulations 1988, S.I. 1988/186.)

items.title: The National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
