items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) (Scotland) Regulations 1974 (“the principal Regulations”). They are concerned mainly with 3 topics: the substitution in the principal Regulations of a new regulation 19 (appeal from Scottish Dental Estimates Board), the setting up by health boards of ophthalmic service committees to consider complaints against ophthalmic medical practitioners and opticians, and the extension of the duties of area optical committees.

items.title: The Public Utility Transfers and Water Charges Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Public Utility Transfers and Water Charges Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Foreign Loan Interest) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Foreign Loan Interest) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 5) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-line described in Part II of the Schedule, which lies between two offshore installations situated east of the coast of Scotland.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 6) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie between offshore installations above the Beatrice oilfield, which is situated east of the Moray Firth.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 7) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie between an offshore installation situated above the Thames gasfield, east of the coast of Lincolnshire, and two nearby gasfields.

items.title: The Housing Defects (Reinstatement Grant) (Amendment of Conditions for Assistance) Order 1988
items.description: Under Part XVI of the Housing Act 1985 (“the Act”) the owner of a defective dwelling (one in a class designated by the Secretary of State or the local housing authority) can, if the appropriate conditions are satisfied, require the local housing authority either to give him a grant towards the reinstatement of the dwelling or to repurchase it. The conditions for determining whether assistance takes the form of a reinstatement grant or repurchase at 95% defect-free value, are set out in sections 537 and 538 of the Act. One of those conditions (section 538(1)(d)) is that if the amount of grant is likely to exceed the total amount payable on repurchase (including incidental expenses) assistance must be by way of repurchase.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Annual Accounting) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations provide, subject to certain conditions, for a taxable person to be authorised to account for and pay tax on an annual basis. They also set out the consequential requirements where a person ceases to be authorised for annual accounting.

items.title: The Companies (Fees) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Companies (Fees) Regulations 1980 as amended by the Companies (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 1982. They re-enact some of the fees prescribed by those Regulations and increase some of the fees relating to certain matters covered by those Regulations.

items.title: The Prison (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison Rules 1964 to remove the right of an unconvicted prisoner to be supplied with food at his own expense or that of his friends. They revoke the Prison (Amendment No. 2) Rules 1987, which they reproduce, save that rule 2(d), which was included in the 1987 Rules in error, has been omitted.

items.title: The Opencast Coal (Rate of Interest on Compensation) Order 1988
items.description: Section 35 of the Opencast Coal Act 1958 provides that interest shall be payable in addition to compensation in certain circumstances. This Order decreases the rate of interest from 10¼ per cent. to 9¼ per cent. per annum and revokes the Opencast Coal (Rate of Interest on Compensation) Order 1987.

items.title: The Trade Marks and Service Marks (Fees) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Trade Marks and Service Marks (Fees) Rules 1987. All fees apart from those payable on forms TM 9 and TM 20 are increased with effect from 23rd June 1988. The fees payable on forms TM 9 and TM 20 are abolished. It is estimated that the total amount of fees collected per annum at the new levels will be 4.5 per cent greater than the amount collected per annum at the existing levels.

items.title: Weights and Measures (Knitting Yarns) Order 1988
items.description: This Order implements the requirements of Council Directive No. 87/356/EEC (OJ No. L192, 11.7.1987, p.48). The Order provides that knitting yarns are to be pre-packed only in prescribed metric quantities.

items.title: The Pressure Vessels (Verification) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Pressure Vessels (Verification) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment No. 2) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the provisions relating to costs in the County Court Rules 1981 by increasing fixed costs by approximately 5.4% (rules 2 to 4 and 6 to 9) and by introducing a new item for which costs are fixed (rule 5).

items.title: The Wireless Telegraphy (Broadcast Licence Charges and Exemption) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations, which amend the Wireless Telegraphy (Broadcast Licence Charges and Exemption) Regulations 1984, redefine certain of the accommodation which qualifies for the concessionary television licence scheme.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No.5) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No.5) Order 1987 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Construction Board) Order 1988
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals of the Construction Industry Training Board which were submitted to and approved by the Manpower Services Commission, and thereafter submitted by the Manpower Services Commission to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the construction industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Urban Development Corporations (Appropriate Ministers) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that for the purposes of Part XVI of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980, which relates to urban development, the Secretary of State for Energy shall be the appropriate Minister for the British Coal Corporation and its wholly-owned subsidiaries and the Secretary of State for Trade and Industry shall be the appropriate Minister for British Shipbuilders and the British Steel Corporation and their wholly-owned subsidiaries.

items.title: The Industrial Training Levy (Hotel and Catering) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 21st June 1988, gives effect to proposals of the Hotel and Catering Industry Training Board which were submitted to and approved by the Manpower Services Commission, and thereafter submitted by the Manpower Services Commission to the Secretary of State. The proposals are for the imposition of a levy on employers in the hotel and catering industry for the purpose of raising money towards meeting the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The A2 Trunk Road (Rochester Way Relief Road, Greenwich and Bexley) (Speed Limits) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The A2 Trunk Road (Rochester Way Relief Road, Greenwich and Bexley) (Speed Limits) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Scottish Transport Group (Castle Bay Pier) Empowerment Order 1988
items.description: This Order confers jurisdiction as a harbour authority on the Scottish Transport Group in respect of the Castle Bay Pier undertaking and defines the area within which the powers of the harbourmaster may be exercised.

items.title: The Caribbean Development Bank (Further Payments) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for the payment on behalf of the Government of the United Kingdom of a sum not exceeding £9,252,510 as a further contribution to the Special Development Fund of the Caribbean Development Bank in accordance with arrangements made with the Bank pursuant to the Resolution entitled “Resolution of Contributors to Special Development Fund II of the Caribbean Development Bank” adopted at the Meeting of Contributors on 22nd September 1987. The Order also provides for the redemption of non-interest-bearing and non-negotiable notes issued by the Secretary of State in payment of the further contribution. The Order also provides that any sums which may be received by the Government in pursuance of the arrangements relating to the further contribution shall be paid into the Consolidated Fund.The provisions of the Resolution of Contributors may be obtained by application to the Overseas Development Administration, Eland House, Stag Place, London SW1E 5DH.

items.title: The Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on the fourteenth day after the day on which it is made, amends the Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) Order 1983 as amended by the Redundancy Payments (Local Government) (Modification) (Amendment) Order 1985. Those Orders modified certain redundancy payments provisions of the Employment Protection (Consolidation) Act 1978 in their application to persons employed in relevant local government service so that their employment in the service is to be treated as if it was continuous for the purposes of those provisions. This Order adds to the list of employers to whose employees the 1983 Order as amended applies.

items.title: The Family Credit (General) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (General) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Housing Benefit (General) Regulations 1987. They raise the capital limit over which a person is not to be entitled to housing benefit, the limit for treating outstanding capital as income and the limit for calculating weekly tariff income, to £8,000 (regulation 2). They also disregard, in the calculation of income, any training premium paid to a person participating in arrangements for training under the Employment and Training Act 1973 and, in the calculation of income and capital, any payment made to compensate for the loss of housing benefit on the introduction of the scheme on 1st April 1988 (regulations 3 and 4). In addition, in the calculation of capital, they disregard the claimant’s interest in his former home where he has left that home following his estrangement or divorce from his former partner or in premises where he is seeking possession of it or essential repairs are required before occupation, for a period of 26 weeks or longer if reasonable (regulation 4).

items.title: The Income Support (General) Amendment No. 2 Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 8) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie above and between a number of offshore gasfields situated east of the North Yorkshire coast.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 9) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule which lie above and between a number of offshore oilfields situated east of the coast of Scotland.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Children and Young Persons) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules consolidate with amendments the Rules listed in Schedule 1. The amendments are mainly to take account of section 32A(4A) of the Children and Young Persons Act 1969 (which, in care proceedings where there is a conflict of interest between parent and child, makes the parent a party to the proceedings) and section 32C of that Act (which enables a court to make a grandparent a party to care proceedings), both inserted by section 3 of the Children and Young Persons (Amendment) Act 1986. Rule 15 requires notice (Form 9 in Schedule 2) to be sent to a parent or guardian who is made a party to care proceedings. Rule 14 is amended to require a grandparent to be given notice of care proceedings, a notice of application (Form 10) is prescribed for a grandparent who wishes to be made a party and Rule 17 specifies the circumstances in which the court may grant such an application. Rules 18 to 28 contain revised procedural provision for care and related proceedings, principally to take account of possible participation as parties by parents and grandparents and to permit greater participation by non-parties; Rules 30 and 33 make corresponding provision for participation by non-parties in access proceedings and proceedings relating to parental rights resolutions respectively. The Rules provide for greater disclosure of written reports, where practicable before the hearing (Rules 10(2), 25(1) and (2), 26(2) and 34(1) and (2)). There are other minor procedural and drafting amendments.

items.title: The Control of Misleading Advertisements Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive No. 84/450/EEC (OJ No. L 250 19.9.1984, p. 17) relating to misleading advertising.

items.title: The Misuse of Drugs (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Misuse of Drugs Regulations 1985 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The (A1237) York Outer Ring Road (Northern) (Trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A1237) York Outer Ring Road (Northern) (Trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The (A41) East of Birmingham to Birkenhead and the (A49) Shrewsbury to Warrington Trunk Roads (Whitchurch Bypass) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A41) East of Birmingham to Birkenhead and the (A49) Shrewsbury to Warrington Trunk Roads (Whitchurch Bypass) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Assured Tenancies (Approved Bodies) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: Assured tenancies are tenancies which, but for section 56 of the Housing Act 1980, would have been protected tenancies or housing association tenancies under the Rent Act 1977 (c. 42), and which meet certain other conditions. Such tenancies are subject to Part II of the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954 (c. 56), as modified by Schedule 5 to the 1980 Act. They can only be granted by bodies approved under section 56 of the 1980 Act.

items.title: The (A696) Newcastle—Edinburgh Trunk Road (Prestwick Terrace to Kenton Bank Foot De-Trunking) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A696) Newcastle—Edinburgh Trunk Road (Prestwick Terrace to Kenton Bank Foot De-Trunking) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The (A696) Newcastle-Edinburgh Trunk Road (Woolsington Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The (A696) Newcastle-Edinburgh Trunk Road (Woolsington Bypass and Slip Roads) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Criminal Proceedings) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees in Criminal Proceedings) Regulations 1984 so as to increase the fees allowable to counsel for legal aid in criminal proceedings given under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1967. The increased fees will apply to proceedings concluded on or after 1st June 1988.

items.title: The Housing Defects (Expenditure Limits) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Housing Defects (Expenditure Limits) (No. 2) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Anglian Water Authority (Newton on Trent) Water Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Anglian Water Authority (Newton on Trent) Water Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Consular Fees (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the Consular Fees Order 1987 so as to render non-refundable consular fees payable with respect to entry clearances (fees 20(a) and 22), to introduce a new fee for granting a double entry visa (fee 22) and to increase the fees payable in respect of entry clearances for settlement from £50 to £60 and for multiple entries for employment and various other purposes listed under fee 22 from £40 to £44.

items.title: The European Committee for the Prevention of Torture and Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment (Immunities and Privileges) Order 1988
items.description: This Order confers immunities on members of the European Committee for the Prevention of Torture and Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment and on experts assisting it in its work. It will enable Her Majesty’s Government to become a party to the European Convention on the Prevention of Torture and Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment, done at Strasbourg on 26th November 1987.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Confirmation of Legislation) (Bermuda) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, made under section 735(1) of the Merchant Shipping Act 1894, confirms an Act of the Legislature of Bermuda which amends the Bermuda Merchant Shipping Act 1979 so as to apply in Bermuda those provisions of United Kingdom merchant shipping legislation which give effect to the Safety of Life at Sea Convention 1974 (Cmnd. 7874).

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Confirmation of Legislation) (Cayman Islands) Order 1988
items.description: This Order, made under section 735(1) of the Merchant Shipping Act 1894, confirms a Law of 1987 enacted by the Legislature of the Cayman Islands as amended by a further Law of 1987, which provides for the extension to the Cayman Islands of certain maritime safety and anti-pollution conventions.

items.title: The Department of Trade and Industry (Fees) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Department of Trade and Industry (Fees) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Summer Time Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides for the period of summer time in 1989 during which the time for general purposes in the United Kingdom will be one hour in advance of Greenwich mean time. The Order applies also to the Bailiwick of Guernsey but not to the Bailiwick of Jersey or the Isle of Man which have their own legislation on the subject.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Turkey) Order 1988
items.description: The Agreement with the Republic of Turkey is set out in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Venezuela) Order 1988
items.description: The Protocol scheduled to this Order makes certain alterations to the Agreement with Venezuela which was signed on 8th March 1978 and which entered into force on 8th June 1987.

items.title: The Disabled Persons (Services, Consultation and Representation) Act 1986 (Commencement No.5) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Disabled Persons (Services, Consultation and Representation) Act 1986 (Commencement No.5) (Scotland) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Inquiries Procedure) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules regulate the procedure to be followed in connection with local inquiries in England or Wales caused by the Secretary of State to be held by inspectors before he determines applications referred to him, or appeals made to him, in relation to planning permission, consent under tree preservation orders, listed building consent and consent for the demolition of unlisted buildings in conservation areas (known as “conservation area consent”).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning Appeals (Determination by Inspectors) (Inquiries Procedure) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules regulate the procedure to be followed in connection with local inquiries in England or Wales held by inspectors appointed by the Secretary of State to determine appeals made to him in relation to planning permission, listed building consent and consent for the demolition of unlisted buildings in conservation areas (known as“conservation area consent”).

items.title: The Insolvency Fees (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Insolvency Fees (Amendment) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Insurance Brokers Registration Council (Conduct of Investment Business) Rules Approval Order 1988
items.description: This Order approves Rules made by the Insurance Brokers Registration Council in exercise of their powers under section 11 of the Insurance Brokers (Registration) Act 1977 and paragraphs 6(1), (2) and (3) of the Financial Services Act 1986. The Rules approved by this Order were made by the Council pursuant to their interim recognition as a professional body by the Securities and Investments Board under section 18 of, and paragraph 4 of Schedule 15 to, the Financial Services Act.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Amendment No. 2 Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (Wales) (No. 5) Order 1987, as amended by S.I. 1988/9, contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR. That Order designates areas of land in Wales (“the designated areas”) affected by the escape from and into which the movement of sheep, and in which the slaughter of sheep, are prohibited and provides that restrictions on the slaughter of sheep which were in the designated areas after specified dates, and the supply of meat derived from such sheep, extend throughout the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) Rules 1988
items.description: These Rules amend the Crown Court Rules 1982 in relation to appeals in care and care-related proceedings. The amendments are mainly to take account of provisions of the Children and Young Persons (Amendment) Act 1986 which enable parents and grandparents in certain circumstances to be parties to such proceedings and to appeals therefrom; and to reflect provision in the Magistrates' Courts (Children and Young Persons) Rules 1988 permitting greater participation by non-parties and providing for greater disclosure of written reports. Notice of the commencement and progress of an appeal is required to be given to persons entitled to participate in the proceedings in the juvenile court (paragraphs 1, 2, 3 and 8 of the Schedule); parents' and guardians' rights to participate in an appeal are extended (paragraph 6 of the Schedule); new Rule 10A (inserted by paragraph 7 of the Schedule) specifies the circumstances in which the Crown Court may grant an application by a grandparent to be made a party to an appeal; and new Rule 10B (also inserted by paragraph 7) concerns disclosure of written reports for the purposes of an appeal. There are other minor procedural and drafting amendments.

items.title: The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment No. 2 Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Export of Sheep (Prohibition) (No. 2) Amendment No. 2 Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Amendment No. 2 Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) (England) Order 1987 contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the USSR.

items.title: Norfolk and Suffolk Broads Act 1988 (Commencement) Order 1988
items.description: Article 2 of this Order specifies 6th June 1988 as the date on which the Broads Authority, created by section 1 of the Norfolk and Suffolk Broads Act 1988, shall be deemed to be duly constituted. By section 26(2) of the Act, section 9 (the Navigation Committee) and sections 14 to 17 (which relate to finance) of that Act are brought into force on the same date.

items.title: The Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government (Direct Labour Organisations) (Competition) (Scotland) Regulations 1984 in their application to highway work.

items.title: The Coast Protection (Notices) (Scotland) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations replace the Coast Protection (Notices) (Scotland) Regulations 1950. They provide forms of notices of proposed coast protection work under sections 5 and 8 of the Coast Protection Act 1949 and provide for service on additional interested parties and for service of notices of objection. The estimated cost of works which require publication of notices in the Edinburgh Gazette, as well as in a local newspaper, is increased from £50,000 to £500,000.

items.title: The Consumer Protection (Cancellation of Contracts Concluded away from Business Premises) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the Consumer Protection (Cancellation of Contracts Concluded away from Business Premises) Regulations 1987 (the principal Regulations) and come into force at the same time as those Regulations.

items.title: The Heavy Goods Vehicles (Drivers' Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Heavy Goods Vehicles (Drivers' Licences) Regulations 1977 by increasing the fee payable under regulation 23 thereof, by a person who applies for a driving test, from £40 to £42.

items.title: The Cardiff-Glan Conwy Trunk Road A470 (Llanidloes By-pass) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Cardiff-Glan Conwy Trunk Road A470 (Llanidloes By-pass) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Drivers' Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend paragraph (2) of regulation 10 of the Public Service Vehicles (Drivers' Licences) Regulations 1985 by increasing the fee, for the provision by a traffic commissioner of facilities for an applicant for a licence to drive a public service vehicle to undergo a test, from £40 to £42.

items.title: The Social Security Act 1986 (Consequential) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations modify the Social Security Act 1986 to enable the uprating of the transitional additions to income support payable under regulations made under section 89 of that Act.

items.title: The British Transport (Alteration of Pension Schemes) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends the British Transport (Alteration of Pension Schemes) Order 1969 to relate the powers of investment in respect of the London and North Eastern Railway Superannuation Fund in unit trusts to the relevant provisions of the Financial Services Act 1986.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Tree Preservation Order) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the form of tree preservation order contained in the Schedule to the Town and Country Planning (Tree Preservation Order) Regulations 1969 so as to enable local authorities, for the purposes of compensation and except where an application is referred to them under the Forestry Act 1967, to treat trees in woodlands which have an outstanding or special amenity value in the same way as all other such trees.

items.title: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: The Food Protection (Emergency Prohibitions) Order 1988 (“the principal Order”) contains emergency prohibitions restricting various activities in order to prevent human consumption of food which has been or which may have been rendered unsuitable for that purpose in consequence of the escape of radioactive substances from a nuclear reactor situated at Chernobyl in the Ukraine, USSR.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Sheriff Court Fees Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that the exemption from paying fees in simplified divorce proceedings granted to recipients of supplementary benefit and family income supplement is to apply to recipients of income support and family credit under the Social Security Act 1986, which replaced the previous benefits on 11th April 1988.

items.title: The Rate Limitation (Designation of Authorities) (Exemption) (Wales) Order 1988
items.description: Section 2(1) of the Rates Act 1984 provides that an authority in whose case a maximum rate or precept is to be prescribed in any financial year is to be designated by the Secretary of State in the preceding financial year.

items.title: The Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment (No.2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order provides that the exemption from paying fees in simplified divorce proceedings granted to recipients of supplementary benefit and family income supplement is to apply to recipients of income support and family credit under the Social Security Act 1986, which replaced the previous benefits on 11th April 1988.

items.title: The Forestry (Exceptions from Restriction of Felling) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These regulations further amend the Forestry (Exceptions from Restriction of Felling) Regulations 1979 (as amended by the Forestry (Exceptions from Restriction of Felling) (Amendment) Regulations 1985). The 1979 Regulations (as amended) provide for six exceptions from the provision in section 9(1) of the Forestry Act 1967 that a felling licence granted by the Forestry Commissioners is required for the felling of growing trees. The present regulations further amend the 1979 Regulations by enlarging the exception introduced by the 1985 Regulations, so that this exception relates not only (as before) to felling in accordance with a plan of operations approved under the Forestry Grant Scheme or the Broadleaved Woodland Grant Scheme but now also relates to felling in accordance with a plan of operations approved under the newly introduced Woodland Grant Scheme. All these schemes are schemes under which the Commissioners agree, subject to the conditions of the scheme, to make grants pursuant to section 1 of the Forestry Act 1979 for and in connection with the use and management of land for forestry purposes.

items.title: The Restriction on Movement of Spruce Wood (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: This Order further amends the Restriction on Movement of Spruce Wood Order 1982 by adding to the list of areas in respect of which the movement of spruce wood is restricted the remaining parts of the counties of Dyfed and Gwynedd not included in the previous Order and the county of West Glamorgan.

items.title: The Docks and Harbours (Rateable Values) (Amendment) Order 1988
items.description: The rateable values of hereditaments comprising dock or harbour undertakings occupied by statutory undertakers are, by virtue of the Docks and Harbours (Rateable Values) Order 1976, (“the principal order”) calculated by a formula applied to the undertaking’s revenue receipts, adjusted for each year beginning on 1st April by reference to the retail price index in September of the preceding year. The index was re-referenced in January 1987. This order amends the principal order to substitute, in relation to the year 1988—9 and subsequent years, a formula which reproduces the effect of the previous formula by reference to the re-referenced index.

items.title: The Medicines (Medicated Animal Feeding Stuffs) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Medicines (Medicated Animal Feeding Stuffs) Regulations 1985 and implement in part the provisions of Article 13 of Council Directive 70/524/EEC (OJ No. L270, 14.12.70, p. 1 (OJ/SE 1970 (III) p. 840)) concerning additives in feeding stuffs, as substituted by Article 1 of Council Directive 84/587/EEC (OJ No. L319, 8.12.84, p. 13). The relevant provisions of the Regulations which implement provisions in that Article are to be found in regulations 3(1)(a), 4(1)(a) and 6.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Development) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1988
items.description: This Order amends Class V (Agricultural buildings, works and uses) of Schedule 1 to the Town and Country Planning (General Development) (Scotland) Order 1981 to remove from permitted development (a) buildings of more than 465 square metres which are used to house pigs, poultry, rabbits or animals bred for their skin or fur, and (b) any structures associated with such buildings and used for storing slurry or sewage sludge. A definition of “slurry” is inserted into article 2 of the 1981 Order.

items.title: The Housing Defects (Reinstatement Grant) (Amendment of Conditions for Assistance) (Scotland) Order 1988
items.description: Under Part XIV of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the Act”) the owner of a defective dwelling (one in a class designated by the Secretary of State or the local authority) can, if the appropriate conditions are satisfied, require the local authority either to give him a grant towards the reinstatement of the dwelling or to repurchase it. The conditions for determining whether assistance takes the form of reinstatement grant or, alternatively, repurchase at 95% of defect-free value, are set out in sections 265 and 266 of the Act. One of those conditions (section 266(1)(d)) provides that, if the amount of any such grant is likely to exceed the total amount payable on the repurchase (including incidental expenses), the form of assistance must be by way or repurchase.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force in two stages in England and Wales all the provisions of Schedule 6 to the Local Government Act 1988 which are not brought into force by the Act directly.

items.title: The Gas (Meters) (Amendment) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations amend the provisions relating to fees contained in regulation 5 of, and Schedule 1 to, the Gas (Meters) Regulations 1983 (“the 1983 Regulations”).

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 10) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule, which lie above the Hewett gas field, situated east of the Norfolk coast.

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 11) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates BP Petroleum Development Limited as the owner of the pipe-lines described in the Schedule. In the case of the pipe-lines specified in Part I of the Schedule, the designation applies only to those parts which are in controlled waters, that is the territorial sea and areas designated under the Continental Shelf Act 1964 (c. 29).

items.title: The Submarine Pipe-lines (Designated Owners) (No. 12) Order 1988
items.description: This Order designates the companies listed in Part I of the Schedule as owners of the pipe-lines described in Part II of the Schedule. The pipe-lines are used in connection with production from the Balmoral oilfield, situated east of the Highland Region of Scotland.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) (No.2) Regulations 1988
items.description: This version of the The Motor Vehicles (Tests) (Amendment) (No.2) Regulations 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Sex Discrimination Act 1986 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 26th February 1988 those provisions of the Sex Discrimination Act 1986 which are not already in force. They relate to the repeal of the provisions of the Hours of Employment (Conventions) Act 1936 (c. 22) which impose restrictions on the employment of women at night in industrial undertakings.

items.title: The Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) (No. 2) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This version of the The Consumer Credit (Exempt Agreements) (No. 2) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1988 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) Amendment (No. 4) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 1979 (“the principal Regulations”) and are made in consequence of sections 1 and 7 of the Social Security Act 1986 (“the 1986 Act”). As they are made before the end of a period of 12 months from the coming into force of the provisions in consequence of which they are made, they are exempted by section 61(5)(b) of the 1986 Act from the requirement under section 10(1) of the Social Security Act 1980 (c. 30) to refer proposals to the Social Security Advisory Committee and are made without reference to that Committee.

items.title: The Personal Pension Schemes (Establishment of Schemes) Order 1988
items.description: Section 632(1) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 provides that the Board (the Commissioners of Inland Revenue) shall not approve a personal pension scheme established by any person other than one mentioned in the subsection.

items.title: The Esk Salmon Fishery District Designation Order 1988
items.description: This Order creates a new salmon fishery district by amalgamating the former districts of the River Bervie, the River North Esk and the River South Esk which are abolished. Regulation 3 provides for the retention of regulations relating to the due observance of the weekly close time and to the meshes of nets (matters specified in section 6(6) of the Salmon Fisheries (Scotland) Act 1862) which applied in the superseded districts. These will apply in the same way in the new district. The Order makes different provision for the annual close times and periods when rod and line angling only is permitted in (a) the former district of the River Bervie, and (b) the former districts of the River North Esk and the River South Esk.

items.title: The Financial Services Act 1986 (Commencement) (No. 9) Order 1988
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1 July 1988 those provisions of the Financial Services Act 1986 which effect the repeal of the Restrictive Trade Practices (Stock Exchange) Act 1984.

items.title: The London Cab Order 1988
items.description: This Order increases the fares payable for the hiring of a motor cab in the Metropolitan Police District and the City of London in respect of all journeys beginning and ending there. This is achieved by substituting a new paragraph 40 (scale of fares for motor cabs) — see article 3. The Schedule to the Order provides a conversion table setting out the new fares for cabs with taximeters which are not capable of recording those fares automatically. The table is required to be kept prominently displayed in the cab and may only be used during the transitional period from 25th June 1988 to 30th September 1988 after which all taximeters must be capable of automatically recording and displaying the increased fare.

items.title: The Measuring Equipment (Cold-water Meters) Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations apply to cold-water meters for use for trade in the measuring of any supply of water for domestic purposes in England and Wales. The Regulations only apply to those meters installed on or after 1st August 1988, and prescribe such meters for the purposes of section 11(1) of the Weights and Measures Act 1985. The effect of prescription is to make it unlawful to use these meters for trade unless they have been tested, passed as fit for such use and either marked by the manufacturer in accordance with the Regulations or stamped by an inspector of weights and measures. These requirements of prescription are also satisfied by a cold-water meter complying with Council Directive No 75/33/EEC and bearing the sign of EEC pattern approval and mark of EEC initial verification.

items.title: The Commissioners for Oaths (Fees) (No. 2) Order 1988
items.description: This Order replaces the Commissioners for Oaths (Fees) Order 1988 which increased the fees chargeable by commissioners for oaths and practising solicitors for taking affidavits and similar declarations from £3.00 to £3.50 and for marking exhibits from 75p to £1.00. The increased amounts include value added tax where payable.

items.title: The Family Credit and Income Support (General) Amendment Regulations 1988
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Family Credit (General) Regulations 1987 and the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 to provide, in the calculation of income and capital, for the disregard of payments made to the incapacitated and very severely disabled out of the Independent Living Fund.
